sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The book of the genealogy of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Abraham became the father of Isaac. Isaac became the father of Jacob. Jacob became the father of Judah and his brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judah became the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar. Perez became the father of Hezron. Hezron became the father of Ram.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Ram became the father of Amminadab. Amminadab became the father of Nahshon. Nahshon became the father of Salmon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Salmon became the father of Boaz by Rahab. Boaz became the father of Obed by Ruth. Obed became the father of Jesse.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesse became the father of King David. David became the father of Solomon by her who had been Uriah’s wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Solomon became the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam became the father of Abijah. Abijah became the father of Asa.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Asa became the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat became the father of Joram. Joram became the father of Uzziah.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Uzziah became the father of Jotham. Jotham became the father of Ahaz. Ahaz became the father of Hezekiah.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Hezekiah became the father of Manasseh. Manasseh became the father of Amon. Amon became the father of Josiah.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Josiah became the father of Jechoniah and his brothers, at the time of the exile to Babylon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the exile to Babylon, Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel became the father of Zerubbabel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Zerubbabel became the father of Abiud. Abiud became the father of Eliakim. Eliakim became the father of Azor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Azor became the father of Zadok. Zadok became the father of Achim. Achim became the father of Eliud.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Eliud became the father of Eleazar. Eleazar became the father of Matthan. Matthan became the father of Jacob.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jacob became the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, from whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; from David to the exile to Babylon fourteen generations; and from the carrying away to Babylon to the Christ, fourteen generations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was like this: After his mother, Mary, was engaged to Joseph, before they came together, she was found pregnant by the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph, her husband, being a righteous man, and not willing to make her a public example, intended to put her away secretly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he thought about these things, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, “Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to take to yourself Mary, your wife, for that which is conceived in her is of the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She shall give birth to a son. You shall name him Jesus, for it is he who shall save his people from their sins.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now all this has happened that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph arose from his sleep, and did as the angel of the Lord commanded him, and took his wife to himself;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and didn’t know her sexually until she had given birth to her firstborn son. He named him Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of King Herod, behold, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Where is he who is born King of the Jews? For we saw his star in the east, and have come to worship him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When King Herod heard it, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Gathering together all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he asked them where the Christ would be born.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for this is written through the prophet,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Herod secretly called the wise men, and learned from them exactly what time the star appeared.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent them to Bethlehem, and said, “Go and search diligently for the young child. When you have found him, bring me word, so that I also may come and worship him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, having heard the king, went their way; and behold, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, until it came and stood over where the young child was.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceedingly great joy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came into the house and saw the young child with Mary, his mother, and they fell down and worshiped him. Opening their treasures, they offered to him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being warned in a dream not to return to Herod, they went back to their own country another way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when they had departed, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream, saying, “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and stay there until I tell you, for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose and took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and was there until the death of Herod; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, “Out of Egypt I called my son.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Herod, when he saw that he was mocked by the wise men, was exceedingly angry, and sent out, and killed all the male children who were in Bethlehem and in all the surrounding countryside, from two years old and under, according to the exact time which he had learned from the wise men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then that which was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Arise and take the young child and his mother, and go into the land of Israel, for those who sought the young child’s life are dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in the place of his father, Herod, he was afraid to go there. Being warned in a dream, he withdrew into the region of Galilee,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and came and lived in a city called Nazareth; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets that he will be called a Nazarene.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In those days, John the Baptizer came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Repent, for the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is he who was spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now John himself wore clothing made of camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then people from Jerusalem, all of Judea, and all the region around the Jordan went out to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were baptized by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming for his baptism, he said to them, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore produce fruit worthy of repentance!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t think to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father,’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees. Therefore every tree that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and cast into the fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I indeed baptize you in water for repentance, but he who comes after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor. He will gather his wheat into the barn, but the chaff he will burn up with unquenchable fire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptized by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But John would have hindered him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and you come to me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus, answering, said to him, “Allow it now, for this is the fitting way for us to fulfill all righteousness.” Then he allowed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, when he was baptized, went up directly from the water: and behold, the heavens were opened to him. He saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a voice out of the heavens said, “This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had fasted forty days and forty nights, he was hungry afterward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The tempter came and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of God’s mouth.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the devil took him into the holy city. He set him on the pinnacle of the temple,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down, for it is written, ‘He will command his angels concerning you,’ and,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Again, it is written, ‘You shall not test the Lord, your God.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, the devil took him to an exceedingly high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “I will give you all of these things, if you will fall down and worship me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to him, “Get behind me, Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the devil left him, and behold, angels came and served him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus heard that John was delivered up, he withdrew into Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Leaving Nazareth, he came and lived in Capernaum, which is by the sea, in the region of Zebulun and Naphtali,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From that time, Jesus began to preach, and to say, “Repent! For the Kingdom of Heaven is at hand.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Walking by the sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers: Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew, his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you fishers for men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They immediately left their nets and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. He called them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They immediately left the boat and their father, and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went about in all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the Good News of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness among the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The report about him went out into all Syria. They brought to him all who were sick, afflicted with various diseases and torments, possessed with demons, epileptics, and paralytics; and he healed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great multitudes from Galilee, Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea and from beyond the Jordan followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing the multitudes, he went up onto the mountain. When he had sat down, his disciples came to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He opened his mouth and taught them, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Blessed are you when people reproach you, persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely, for my sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Rejoice, and be exceedingly glad, for great is your reward in heaven. For that is how they persecuted the prophets who were before you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You are the salt of the earth, but if the salt has lost its flavor, with what will it be salted? It is then good for nothing, but to be cast out and trodden under the feet of men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can’t be hidden.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither do you light a lamp, and put it under a measuring basket, but on a stand; and it shines to all who are in the house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, let your light shine before men; that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t think that I came to destroy the law or the prophets. I didn’t come to destroy, but to fulfill.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly, I tell you, until heaven and earth pass away, not even one smallest letter or one tiny pen stroke shall in any way pass away from the law, until all things are accomplished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever, therefore, shall break one of these least commandments, and teach others to do so, shall be called least in the Kingdom of Heaven; but whoever shall do and teach them shall be called great in the Kingdom of Heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I tell you that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, there is no way you will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You have heard that it was said to the ancient ones, ‘You shall not murder;’ and ‘Whoever murders will be in danger of the judgment.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you that everyone who is angry with his brother without a cause   will be in danger of the judgment. Whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca!’  will be in danger of the council. Whoever says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of Gehenna.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If therefore you are offering your gift at the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Agree with your adversary quickly, while you are with him on the way; lest perhaps the prosecutor deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and you be cast into prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, you shall by no means get out of there, until you have paid the last penny.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You have heard that it was said,  ‘You shall not commit adultery;’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I tell you that everyone who gazes at a woman to lust after her has committed adultery with her already in his heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off, and throw it away from you. For it is more profitable for you that one of your members should perish, than for your whole body to be cast into Gehenna.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “It was also said, ‘Whoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorce,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I tell you that whoever puts away his wife, except for the cause of sexual immorality, makes her an adulteress; and whoever marries her when she is put away commits adultery.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Again you have heard that it was said to the ancient ones, ‘You shall not make false vows, but shall perform to the Lord your vows,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I tell you, don’t swear at all: neither by heaven, for it is the throne of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither shall you swear by your head, for you can’t make one hair white or black.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes’ and your ‘No’ be ‘No.’ Whatever is more than these is of the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You have heard that it was said, ‘An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you, don’t resist him who is evil; but whoever strikes you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone sues you to take away your coat, let him have your cloak also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever compels you to go one mile, go with him two.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Give to him who asks you, and don’t turn away him who desires to borrow from you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall love your neighbor  and hate your enemy.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you, love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who mistreat you and persecute you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may be children of your Father who is in heaven. For he makes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the just and the unjust.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Don’t even the tax collectors do the same?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you only greet your friends, what more do you do than others? Don’t even the tax collectors do the same?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore you shall be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Be careful that you don’t do your charitable giving before men, to be seen by them, or else you have no reward from your Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when you do merciful deeds, don’t sound a trumpet before yourself, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may get glory from men. Most certainly I tell you, they have received their reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when you do merciful deeds, don’t let your left hand know what your right hand does,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that your merciful deeds may be in secret, then your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When you pray, you shall not be as the hypocrites, for they love to stand and pray in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen by men. Most certainly, I tell you, they have received their reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, when you pray, enter into your inner room, and having shut your door, pray to your Father who is in secret, and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In praying, don’t use vain repetitions, as the Gentiles do; for they think that they will be heard for their much speaking.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be like them, for your Father knows what things you need, before you ask him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray like this: ‘Our Father in heaven, may your name be kept holy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let your Kingdom come. Let your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Give us today our daily bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For yours is the Kingdom, the power, and the glory forever. Amen.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you don’t forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Moreover when you fast, don’t be like the hypocrites, with sad faces. For they disfigure their faces, that they may be seen by men to be fasting. Most certainly I tell you, they have received their reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, when you fast, anoint your head, and wash your face;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that you are not seen by men to be fasting, but by your Father who is in secret, and your Father, who sees in secret, will reward you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t lay up treasures for yourselves on the earth, where moth and rust consume, and where thieves break through and steal;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consume, and where thieves don’t break through and steal;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The lamp of the body is the eye. If therefore your eye is sound, your whole body will be full of light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if your eye is evil, your whole body will be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in you is darkness, how great is the darkness!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “No one can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other; or else he will be devoted to one and despise the other. You can’t serve both God and Mammon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life: what you will eat, or what you will drink; nor yet for your body, what you will wear. Isn’t life more than food, and the body more than clothing?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See the birds of the sky, that they don’t sow, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns. Your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren’t you of much more value than they?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Which of you, by being anxious, can add one moment to his lifespan?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why are you anxious about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet I tell you that even Solomon in all his glory was not dressed like one of these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, won’t he much more clothe you, you of little faith?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Therefore don’t be anxious, saying, ‘What will we eat?’, ‘What will we drink?’ or, ‘With what will we be clothed?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Gentiles seek after all these things; for your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be anxious for tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Each day’s own evil is sufficient.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t judge, so that you won’t be judged.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For with whatever judgment you judge, you will be judged; and with whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why do you see the speck that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or how will you tell your brother, ‘Let me remove the speck from your eye;’ and behold, the beam is in your own eye?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You hypocrite! First remove the beam out of your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck out of your brother’s eye.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t give that which is holy to the dogs, neither throw your pearls before the pigs, lest perhaps they trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Ask, and it will be given you. Seek, and you will find. Knock, and it will be opened for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or who is there among you, who, if his son asks him for bread, will give him a stone?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or if he asks for a fish, who will give him a serpent?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask him!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore whatever you desire for men to do to you, you shall also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Enter in by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many are those who enter in by it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How narrow is the gate, and restricted is the way that leads to life! Few are those who find it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly are ravening wolves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By their fruits you will know them. Do you gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, every good tree produces good fruit; but the corrupt tree produces evil fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A good tree can’t produce evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree produce good fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every tree that doesn’t grow good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven; but he who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many will tell me in that day, ‘Lord, Lord, didn’t we prophesy in your name, in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then I will tell them, ‘I never knew you. Depart from me, you who work iniquity.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Everyone therefore who hears these words of mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man, who built his house on a rock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it didn’t fall, for it was founded on the rock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who hears these words of mine, and doesn’t do them will be like a foolish man, who built his house on the sand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rain came down, the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat on that house; and it fell—and great was its fall.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the multitudes were astonished at his teaching,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he taught them with authority, and not like the scribes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a leper came to him and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “See that you tell nobody, but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came into Capernaum, a centurion came to him, asking him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saying, “Lord, my servant lies in the house paralyzed, grievously tormented.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “I will come and heal him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The centurion answered, “Lord, I’m not worthy for you to come under my roof. Just say the word, and my servant will be healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am also a man under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and tell another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and tell my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus heard it, he marveled, and said to those who followed, “Most certainly I tell you, I haven’t found so great a faith, not even in Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you that many will come from the east and the west, and will sit down with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the Kingdom of Heaven,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but the children of the Kingdom will be thrown out into the outer darkness. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to the centurion, “Go your way. Let it be done for you as you have believed.” His servant was healed in that hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus came into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother lying sick with a fever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. She got up and served him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening came, they brought to him many possessed with demons. He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were sick;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying, “He took our infirmities, and bore our diseases.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes around him, he gave the order to depart to the other side.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A scribe came, and said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he got into a boat, his disciples followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a violent storm came up on the sea, so much that the boat was covered with the waves, but he was asleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to him, and woke him up, saying, “Save us, Lord! We are dying!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Why are you fearful, O you of little faith?”  Then he got up, rebuked the wind and the sea, and there was a great calm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The men marveled, saying, “What kind of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came to the other side, into the country of the Gergesenes,  two people possessed by demons met him there, coming out of the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that nobody could pass that way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, they cried out, saying, “What do we have to do with you, Jesus, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was a herd of many pigs feeding far away from them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The demons begged him, saying, “If you cast us out, permit us to go away into the herd of pigs.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Go!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came out, and went into the herd of pigs: and behold, the whole herd of pigs rushed down the cliff into the sea, and died in the water.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who fed them fled, and went away into the city, and told everything, including what happened to those who were possessed with demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, all the city came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged that he would depart from their borders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered into a boat, and crossed over, and came into his own city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, they brought to him a man who was paralyzed, lying on a bed. Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, cheer up! Your sins are forgiven you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, some of the scribes said to themselves, “This man blasphemes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, “Why do you think evil in your hearts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For which is easier, to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Get up, and walk?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins-” (then he said to the paralytic), “Get up, and take up your mat, and go to your house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose and departed to his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the multitudes saw it, they marveled and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Jesus passed by from there, he saw a man called Matthew sitting at the tax collection office. He said to him, “Follow me.” He got up and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he sat in the house, behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and sat down with Jesus and his disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the Pharisees saw it, they said to his disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you go and learn what this means: ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’ for I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then John’s disciples came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples don’t fast?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Can the friends of the bridegroom mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; for the patch would tear away from the garment, and a worse hole is made.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither do people put new wine into old wine skins, or else the skins would burst, and the wine be spilled, and the skins ruined. No, they put new wine into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he told these things to them, behold, a ruler came and worshiped him, saying, “My daughter has just died, but come and lay your hand on her, and she will live.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus got up and followed him, as did his disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a woman who had a discharge of blood for twelve years came behind him, and touched the fringe of his garment;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for she said within herself, “If I just touch his garment, I will be made well.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus, turning around and seeing her, said, “Daughter, cheer up! Your faith has made you well.” And the woman was made well from that hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the flute players, and the crowd in noisy disorder,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he said to them, “Make room, because the girl isn’t dead, but sleeping.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were ridiculing him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the crowd was put out, he entered in, took her by the hand, and the girl arose.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The report of this went out into all that land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Jesus passed by from there, two blind men followed him, calling out and saying, “Have mercy on us, son of David!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come into the house, the blind men came to him. Jesus said to them, “Do you believe that I am able to do this?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “Yes, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he touched their eyes, saying, “According to your faith be it done to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Their eyes were opened. Jesus strictly commanded them, saying, “See that no one knows about this.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they went out and spread abroad his fame in all that land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went out, behold, a mute man who was demon possessed was brought to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the demon was cast out, the mute man spoke. The multitudes marveled, saying, “Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Pharisees said, “By the prince of the demons, he casts out demons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went about all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the Good News of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness among the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed and scattered, like sheep without a shepherd.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest indeed is plentiful, but the laborers are few.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray therefore that the Lord of the harvest will send out laborers into his harvest.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called to himself his twelve disciples, and gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal every disease and every sickness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these. The first, Simon, who is called Peter; Andrew, his brother; James the son of Zebedee; John, his brother;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip; Bartholomew; Thomas; Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus; Lebbaeus, who was also called Thaddaeus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon the Zealot; and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus sent these twelve out, and commanded them, saying, “Don’t go among the Gentiles, and don’t enter into any city of the Samaritans.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Rather, go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As you go, preach, saying, ‘The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Heal the sick, cleanse the lepers, and cast out demons. Freely you received, so freely give.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t take any gold, silver, or brass in your money belts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take no bag for your journey, neither two coats, nor sandals, nor staff: for the laborer is worthy of his food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Into whatever city or village you enter, find out who in it is worthy; and stay there until you go on.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As you enter into the household, greet it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the household is worthy, let your peace come on it, but if it isn’t worthy, let your peace return to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever doesn’t receive you, nor hear your words, as you go out of that house or that city, shake the dust off your feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I send you out as sheep among wolves. Therefore be wise as serpents, and harmless as doves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to councils, and in their synagogues they will scourge you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, and you will be brought before governors and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the nations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when they deliver you up, don’t be anxious how or what you will say, for it will be given you in that hour what you will say.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father who speaks in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end will be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when they persecute you in this city, flee into the next, for most certainly I tell you, you will not have gone through the cities of Israel until the Son of Man has come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is enough for the disciple that he be like his teacher, and the servant like his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more those of his household!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be afraid of them, for there is nothing covered that will not be revealed; and hidden that will not be known.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What I tell you in the darkness, speak in the light; and what you hear whispered in the ear, proclaim on the housetops.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul. Rather, fear him who is able to destroy both soul and body in Gehenna.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Aren’t two sparrows sold for an assarion coin? Not one of them falls on the ground apart from your Father’s will,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone therefore who confesses me before men, I will also confess him before my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But whoever denies me before men, I will also deny him before my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t think that I came to send peace on the earth. I didn’t come to send peace, but a sword.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I came to set a man at odds against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A man’s foes will be those of his own household.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he who loves son or daughter more than me isn’t worthy of me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who doesn’t take his cross and follow after me, isn’t worthy of me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who seeks his life will lose it; and he who loses his life for my sake will find it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who receives you receives me, and he who receives me receives him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward. He who receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man will receive a righteous man’s reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever gives one of these little ones just a cup of cold water to drink in the name of a disciple, most certainly I tell you he will in no way lose his reward.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had finished directing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when John heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As these went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what did you go out to see? A man in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But why did you go out? To see a prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is he, of whom it is written, ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, among those who are born of women there has not arisen anyone greater than John the Baptizer; yet he who is least in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From the days of John the Baptizer until now, the Kingdom of Heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you are willing to receive it, this is Elijah, who is to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces, who call to their companions

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and say, ‘We played the flute for you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned for you, and you didn’t lament.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is justified by her children.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he began to denounce the cities in which most of his mighty works had been done, because they didn’t repent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, you will go down to Hades.  For if the mighty works had been done in Sodom which were done in you, it would have remained until today.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you that it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom, on the day of judgment, than for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At that time, Jesus answered, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you hid these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to infants.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows the Son, except the Father; neither does anyone know the Father, except the Son, and he to whom the Son desires to reveal him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him, “Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did, when he was hungry, and those who were with him;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how he entered into God’s house, and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for those who were with him, but only for the priests?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or have you not read in the law, that on the Sabbath day, the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath, and are guiltless?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’ you wouldn’t have condemned the guiltless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He departed from there, and went into their synagogue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And behold there was a man with a withered hand. They asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath day?” that they might accuse him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “What man is there among you, who has one sheep, and if this one falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, won’t he grab on to it, and lift it out?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out; and it was restored whole, just like the other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Pharisees went out, and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, perceiving that, withdrew from there. Great multitudes followed him; and he healed them all,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and commanded them that they should not make him known:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then one possessed by a demon, blind and mute, was brought to him and he healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the multitudes were amazed, and said, “Can this be the son of David?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “This man does not cast out demons, except by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come upon you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man, and plunder his goods, unless he first bind the strong man? Then he will plunder his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He who is not with me is against me, and he who doesn’t gather with me, scatters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, neither in this age, nor in that which is to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Either make the tree good, and its fruit good, or make the tree corrupt, and its fruit corrupt; for the tree is known by its fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You offspring of vipers, how can you, being evil, speak good things? For out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The good man out of his good treasure brings out good things, and the evil man out of his evil treasure brings out evil things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you that every idle word that men speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then certain of the scribes and Pharisees answered, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, but no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the whale, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, someone greater than Jonah is here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, someone greater than Solomon is here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When an unclean spirit has gone out of a man, he passes through waterless places, seeking rest, and doesn’t find it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he says, ‘I will return into my house from which I came out,’ and when he has come back, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he goes, and takes with himself seven other spirits more evil than he is, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first. Even so will it be also to this evil generation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold, his mother and his brothers stood outside, seeking to speak to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One said to him, “Behold, your mother and your brothers stand outside, seeking to speak to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered him who spoke to him, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples, and said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On that day Jesus went out of the house, and sat by the seaside.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great multitudes gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat, and sat, and all the multitude stood on the beach.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke to them many things in parables, saying, “Behold, a farmer went out to sow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he sowed, some seeds fell by the roadside, and the birds came and devoured them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell on rocky ground, where they didn’t have much soil, and immediately they sprang up, because they had no depth of earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the sun had risen, they were scorched. Because they had no root, they withered away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell among thorns. The thorns grew up and choked them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell on good soil, and yielded fruit: some one hundred times as much, some sixty, and some thirty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples came, and said to him, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of Heaven, but it is not given to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever has, to him will be given, and he will have abundance, but whoever doesn’t have, from him will be taken away even that which he has.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I speak to them in parables, because seeing they don’t see, and hearing, they don’t hear, neither do they understand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled, which says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But blessed are your eyes, for they see; and your ears, for they hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly I tell you that many prophets and righteous men desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them; and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Hear, then, the parable of the farmer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When anyone hears the word of the Kingdom, and doesn’t understand it, the evil one comes, and snatches away that which has been sown in his heart. This is what was sown by the roadside.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What was sown on the rocky places, this is he who hears the word, and immediately with joy receives it;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet he has no root in himself, but endures for a while. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he stumbles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What was sown among the thorns, this is he who hears the word, but the cares of this age and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and he becomes unfruitful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What was sown on the good ground, this is he who hears the word, and understands it, who most certainly bears fruit, and produces, some one hundred times as much, some sixty, and some thirty.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He set another parable before them, saying, “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but while people slept, his enemy came and sowed darnel weeds also among the wheat, and went away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the blade sprang up and produced fruit, then the darnel weeds appeared also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The servants of the householder came and said to him, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where did these darnel weeds come from?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to them, ‘An enemy has done this.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and gather them up?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But he said, ‘No, lest perhaps while you gather up the darnel weeds, you root up the wheat with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let both grow together until the harvest, and in the harvest time I will tell the reapers, “First, gather up the darnel weeds, and bind them in bundles to burn them; but gather the wheat into my barn.” ’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He set another parable before them, saying, “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which indeed is smaller than all seeds. But when it is grown, it is greater than the herbs, and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in its branches.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke another parable to them. “The Kingdom of Heaven is like yeast, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, until it was all leavened.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus spoke all these things in parables to the multitudes; and without a parable, he didn’t speak to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus sent the multitudes away, and went into the house. His disciples came to him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the darnel weeds of the field.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “He who sows the good seed is the Son of Man,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the field is the world; and the good seed, these are the children of the Kingdom; and the darnel weeds are the children of the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The enemy who sowed them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As therefore the darnel weeds are gathered up and burned with fire; so will it be at the end of this age.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather out of his Kingdom all things that cause stumbling, and those who do iniquity,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the Kingdom of their Father. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like treasure hidden in the field, which a man found, and hid. In his joy, he goes and sells all that he has, and buys that field.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who is a merchant seeking fine pearls,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who having found one pearl of great price, he went and sold all that he had, and bought it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like a dragnet, that was cast into the sea, and gathered some fish of every kind,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which, when it was filled, they drew up on the beach. They sat down, and gathered the good into containers, but the bad they threw away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So will it be in the end of the world. The angels will come and separate the wicked from among the righteous,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Have you understood all these things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “Yes, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Therefore every scribe who has been made a disciple in the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who is a householder, who brings out of his treasure new and old things.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had finished these parables, he departed from there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Coming into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished, and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom, and these mighty works?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t his mother called Mary, and his brothers, James, Joses, Simon, and Judas?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Aren’t all of his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all of these things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were offended by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and in his own house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He didn’t do many mighty works there because of their unbelief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At that time, Herod the tetrarch heard the report concerning Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptizer. He is risen from the dead. That is why these powers work in him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Herod had arrested John, and bound him, and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For John said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced among them and pleased Herod.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatever she should ask.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She, being prompted by her mother, said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptizer.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The king was grieved, but for the sake of his oaths, and of those who sat at the table with him, he commanded it to be given,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he sent and beheaded John in the prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His head was brought on a platter, and given to the young lady; and she brought it to her mother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples came, and took the body, and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat, to a deserted place apart. When the multitudes heard it, they followed him on foot from the cities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went out, and he saw a great multitude. He had compassion on them, and healed their sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening had come, his disciples came to him, saying, “This place is deserted, and the hour is already late. Send the multitudes away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves food.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to them, “They don’t need to go away. You give them something to eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “We only have here five loaves and two fish.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Bring them here to me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded the multitudes to sit down on the grass; and he took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, broke and gave the loaves to the disciples, and the disciples gave to the multitudes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all ate, and were filled. They took up twelve baskets full of that which remained left over from the broken pieces.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who ate were about five thousand men, in addition to women and children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead of him to the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into the mountain by himself to pray. When evening had come, he was there alone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the boat was now in the middle of the sea, distressed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the fourth watch of the night, Jesus came to them, walking on the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, “It’s a ghost!” and they cried out for fear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Cheer up! It is I!  Don’t be afraid.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered him and said, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the waters.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Come!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter stepped down from the boat, and walked on the waters to come to Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he saw that the wind was strong, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, he cried out, saying, “Lord, save me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand, took hold of him, and said to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they got up into the boat, the wind ceased.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who were in the boat came and worshiped him, saying, “You are truly the Son of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the people of that place recognized him, they sent into all that surrounding region, and brought to him all who were sick;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they begged him that they might just touch the fringe of his garment. As many as touched it were made whole.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Why do your disciples disobey the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “Why do you also disobey the commandment of God because of your tradition?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God commanded, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you say, ‘Whoever may tell his father or his mother, “Whatever help you might otherwise have gotten from me is a gift devoted to God,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he shall not honor his father or mother.’ You have made the commandment of God void because of your tradition.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy of you, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He summoned the multitude, and said to them, “Hear, and understand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which enters into the mouth doesn’t defile the man; but that which proceeds out of the mouth, this defiles the man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the disciples came, and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this saying?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered, “Every plant which my heavenly Father didn’t plant will be uprooted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Leave them alone. They are blind guides of the blind. If the blind guide the blind, both will fall into a pit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered him, “Explain the parable to us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So Jesus said, “Do you also still not understand?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you understand that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the belly, and then out of the body?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the things which proceed out of the mouth come out of the heart, and they defile the man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual sins, thefts, false testimony, and blasphemies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashed hands doesn’t defile the man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went out from there, and withdrew into the region of Tyre and Sidon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a Canaanite woman came out from those borders, and cried, saying, “Have mercy on me, Lord, you son of David! My daughter is severely possessed by a demon!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered her not a word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples came and begged him, saying, “Send her away; for she cries after us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered, “I wasn’t sent to anyone but the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But she came and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, help me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered, “It is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But she said, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs eat the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus departed from there, and came near to the sea of Galilee; and he went up into the mountain, and sat there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great multitudes came to him, having with them the lame, blind, mute, maimed, and many others, and they put them down at his feet. He healed them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that the multitude wondered when they saw the mute speaking, the injured healed, the lame walking, and the blind seeing—and they glorified the God of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus summoned his disciples and said, “I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days and have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away fasting, or they might faint on the way.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples said to him, “Where should we get so many loaves in a deserted place as to satisfy so great a multitude?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he took the seven loaves and the fish. He gave thanks and broke them, and gave to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes.

sources.label: New Testament
