sources.text: They all ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets full of the broken pieces that were left over.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who ate were four thousand men, in addition to women and children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he sent away the multitudes, got into the boat, and came into the borders of Magdala.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing him, asked him to show them a sign from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered them, “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the morning, ‘It will be foul weather today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ Hypocrites! You know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but you can’t discern the signs of the times!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and there will be no sign given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He left them, and departed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples came to the other side and had forgotten to take bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Take heed and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They reasoned among themselves, saying, “We brought no bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, perceiving it, said, “Why do you reason among yourselves, you of little faith, ‘because you have brought no bread?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you yet perceive, neither remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you took up?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nor the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you took up?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How is it that you don’t perceive that I didn’t speak to you concerning bread? But beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they understood that he didn’t tell them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus came into the parts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, “Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Some say John the Baptizer, some, Elijah, and others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Blessed are you, Simon Bar Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I also tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock  I will build my assembly, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will give to you the keys of the Kingdom of Heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven; and whatever you release on earth will have been released in heaven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he commanded the disciples that they should tell no one that he was Jesus the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From that time, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter took him aside, and began to rebuke him, saying, “Far be it from you, Lord! This will never be done to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he turned, and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, for you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, and whoever will lose his life for my sake will find it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what will it profit a man, if he gains the whole world, and forfeits his life? Or what will a man give in exchange for his life?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man will come in the glory of his Father with his angels, and then he will render to everyone according to his deeds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death, until they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After six days, Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them up into a high mountain by themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was changed before them. His face shone like the sun, and his garments became as white as the light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them talking with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered, and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, let’s make three tents here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them. Behold, a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces, and were very afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus came and touched them and said, “Get up, and don’t be afraid.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Lifting up their eyes, they saw no one, except Jesus alone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, “Don’t tell anyone what you saw, until the Son of Man has risen from the dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples asked him, saying, “Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and will restore all things,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I tell you that Elijah has come already, and they didn’t recognize him, but did to him whatever they wanted to. Even so the Son of Man will also suffer by them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the Baptizer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came to the multitude, a man came to him, kneeling down to him, and saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic, and suffers grievously; for he often falls into the fire, and often into the water.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So I brought him to your disciples, and they could not cure him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation! How long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus rebuked him, the demon went out of him, and the boy was cured from that hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately, and said, “Why weren’t we able to cast it out?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Because of your unbelief. For most certainly I tell you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this kind doesn’t go out except by prayer and fasting.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered up into the hands of men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they will kill him, and the third day he will be raised up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were exceedingly sorry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come to Capernaum, those who collected the didrachma coins came to Peter, and said, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the didrachma?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Yes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth receive toll or tribute? From their children, or from strangers?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “From strangers.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Therefore the children are exempt.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But, lest we cause them to stumble, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take up the first fish that comes up. When you have opened its mouth, you will find a stater coin. Take that, and give it to them for me and you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that hour the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Who then is greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus called a little child to himself, and set him in the middle of them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you turn, and become as little children, you will in no way enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever therefore humbles himself as this little child is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if a huge millstone were hung around his neck, and that he were sunk in the depths of the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to the world because of occasions of stumbling! For it must be that the occasions come, but woe to that person through whom the occasion comes!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off, and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life maimed or crippled, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into the eternal fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out, and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna of fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See that you don’t despise one of these little ones, for I tell you that in heaven their angels always see the face of my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man came to save that which was lost.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “What do you think? If a man has one hundred sheep, and one of them goes astray, doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine, go to the mountains, and seek that which has gone astray?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If he finds it, most certainly I tell you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine which have not gone astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so it is not the will of your Father who is in heaven that one of these little ones should perish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If your brother sins against you, go, show him his fault between you and him alone. If he listens to you, you have gained back your brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if he doesn’t listen, take one or two more with you, that at the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the assembly. If he refuses to hear the assembly also, let him be to you as a Gentile or a tax collector.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, whatever things you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven, and whatever things you release on earth will have been released in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, assuredly I tell you, that if two of you will agree on earth concerning anything that they will ask, it will be done for them by my Father who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there I am in the middle of them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Peter came and said to him, “Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? Until seven times?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “I don’t tell you until seven times, but, until seventy times seven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king, who wanted to reconcile accounts with his servants.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had begun to reconcile, one was brought to him who owed him ten thousand talents.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But because he couldn’t pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, with his wife, his children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The servant therefore fell down and knelt before him, saying, ‘Lord, have patience with me, and I will repay you all!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The lord of that servant, being moved with compassion, released him, and forgave him the debt.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But that servant went out, and found one of his fellow servants, who owed him one hundred denarii, and he grabbed him, and took him by the throat, saying, ‘Pay me what you owe!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “So his fellow servant fell down at his feet and begged him, saying, ‘Have patience with me, and I will repay you!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He would not, but went and cast him into prison, until he should pay back that which was due.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when his fellow servants saw what was done, they were exceedingly sorry, and came and told their lord all that was done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then his lord called him in, and said to him, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt, because you begged me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Shouldn’t you also have had mercy on your fellow servant, even as I had mercy on you?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His lord was angry, and delivered him to the tormentors, until he should pay all that was due to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So my heavenly Father will also do to you, if you don’t each forgive your brother from your hearts for his misdeeds.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had finished these words, he departed from Galilee, and came into the borders of Judea beyond the Jordan.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great multitudes followed him, and he healed them there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pharisees came to him, testing him, and saying, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “Haven’t you read that he who made them from the beginning made them male and female,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, ‘For this cause a man shall leave his father and mother, and shall be joined to his wife; and the two shall become one flesh?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So that they are no more two, but one flesh. What therefore God has joined together, don’t let man tear apart.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Why then did Moses command us to give her a certificate of divorce, and divorce her?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts, allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it has not been so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery; and he who marries her when she is divorced commits adultery.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples said to him, “If this is the case of the man with his wife, it is not expedient to marry.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “Not all men can receive this saying, but those to whom it is given.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there are eunuchs who were born that way from their mother’s womb, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men; and there are eunuchs who made themselves eunuchs for the Kingdom of Heaven’s sake. He who is able to receive it, let him receive it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then little children were brought to him, that he should lay his hands on them and pray; and the disciples rebuked them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said, “Allow the little children, and don’t forbid them to come to me; for the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to ones like these.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He laid his hands on them, and departed from there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, one came to him and said, “Good teacher, what good thing shall I do, that I may have eternal life?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good but one, that is, God. But if you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Which ones?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “ ‘You shall not murder.’ ‘You shall not commit adultery.’ ‘You shall not steal.’ ‘You shall not offer false testimony.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ And, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The young man said to him, “All these things I have observed from my youth. What do I still lack?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell what you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the young man heard the saying, he went away sad, for he was one who had great possessions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to his disciples, “Most certainly I say to you, a rich man will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven with difficulty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the disciples heard it, they were exceedingly astonished, saying, “Who then can be saved?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Looking at them, Jesus said, “With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Peter answered, “Behold, we have left everything, and followed you. What then will we have?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that you who have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of Man will sit on the throne of his glory, you also will sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who has left houses, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, will receive one hundred times, and will inherit eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But many will be last who are first; and first who are last.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who was the master of a household, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went their way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle. He said to them, ‘Why do you stand here all day idle?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and you will receive whatever is right.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening had come, the lord of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning from the last to the first.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When those who were hired at about the eleventh hour came, they each received a denarius.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise each received a denarius.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they received it, they murmured against the master of the household,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, ‘These last have spent one hour, and you have made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me for a denarius?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take that which is yours, and go your way. It is my desire to give to this last just as much as to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isn’t it lawful for me to do what I want to with what I own? Or is your eye evil, because I am good?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the last will be first, and the first last. For many are called, but few are chosen.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will hand him over to the Gentiles to mock, to scourge, and to crucify; and the third day he will be raised up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, kneeling and asking a certain thing of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to her, “What do you want?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She said to him, “Command that these, my two sons, may sit, one on your right hand, and one on your left hand, in your Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “We are able.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with, but to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it is for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the ten heard it, they were indignant with the two brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus summoned them, and said, “You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It shall not be so among you, but whoever desires to become great among you shall be your servant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever desires to be first among you shall be your bondservant,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude rebuked them, telling them that they should be quiet, but they cried out even more, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus stood still, and called them, and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, being moved with compassion, touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes received their sight, and they followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came near to Jerusalem, and came to Bethsphage, to the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying to them, “Go into the village that is opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Untie them, and bring them to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone says anything to you, you shall say, ‘The Lord needs them,’ and immediately he will send them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples went, and did just as Jesus commanded them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and brought the donkey and the colt, and laid their clothes on them; and he sat on them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A very great multitude spread their clothes on the road. Others cut branches from the trees, and spread them on the road.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitudes who went in front of him, and those who followed, kept shouting, “Hosanna  to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come into Jerusalem, all the city was stirred up, saying, “Who is this?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitudes said, “This is the prophet, Jesus, from Nazareth of Galilee.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus entered into the temple of God, and drove out all of those who sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables and the seats of those who sold the doves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “It is written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a den of robbers!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The blind and the lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the children who were crying in the temple and saying, “Hosanna to the son of David!” they were indignant,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “Do you hear what these are saying?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Yes. Did you never read, ‘Out of the mouth of babes and nursing babies you have perfected praise?’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He left them, and went out of the city to Bethany, and camped there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the morning, as he returned to the city, he was hungry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing a fig tree by the road, he came to it, and found nothing on it but leaves. He said to it, “Let there be no fruit from you forever!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the fig tree withered away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, “How did the fig tree immediately wither away?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, if you have faith, and don’t doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you told this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ it would be done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things, whatever you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority do you do these things? Who gave you this authority?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “I also will ask you one question, which if you tell me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The baptism of John, where was it from? From heaven or from men?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask us, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we say, ‘From men,’ we fear the multitude, for all hold John as a prophet.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered Jesus, and said, “We don’t know.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first, and said, ‘Son, go work today in my vineyard.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, ‘I will not,’ but afterward he changed his mind, and went.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to the second, and said the same thing. He answered, ‘I’m going, sir,’ but he didn’t go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which of the two did the will of his father?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “The first.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that the tax collectors and the prostitutes are entering into God’s Kingdom before you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you didn’t believe him, but the tax collectors and the prostitutes believed him. When you saw it, you didn’t even repent afterward, that you might believe him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Hear another parable. There was a man who was a master of a household, who planted a vineyard, set a hedge about it, dug a wine press in it, built a tower, leased it out to farmers, and went into another country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the season for the fruit came near, he sent his servants to the farmers, to receive his fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The farmers took his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they treated them the same way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But afterward he sent to them his son, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the farmers, when they saw the son, said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and seize his inheritance.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they took him, and threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore the lord of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those farmers?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “He will miserably destroy those miserable men, and will lease out the vineyard to other farmers, who will give him the fruit in its season.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Did you never read in the Scriptures,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Therefore I tell you, God’s Kingdom will be taken away from you, and will be given to a nation producing its fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, but on whomever it will fall, it will scatter him as dust.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard his parables, they perceived that he spoke about them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they sought to seize him, they feared the multitudes, because they considered him to be a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered and spoke to them again in parables, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king, who made a wedding feast for his son,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and sent out his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “Behold, I have prepared my dinner. My cattle and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast!” ’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his merchandise,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the rest grabbed his servants, and treated them shamefully, and killed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the king heard that, he was angry, and sent his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited weren’t worthy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Go therefore to the intersections of the highways, and as many as you may find, invite to the wedding feast.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those servants went out into the highways, and gathered together as many as they found, both bad and good. The wedding was filled with guests.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man who didn’t have on wedding clothing,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you come in here not wearing wedding clothing?’ He was speechless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the king said to the servants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and throw him into the outer darkness. That is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many are called, but few chosen.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the Pharisees went and took counsel how they might entrap him in his talk.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and teach the way of God in truth, no matter whom you teach, for you aren’t partial to anyone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Tell us therefore, what do you think? Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, “Why do you test me, you hypocrites?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Show me the tax money.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought to him a denarius.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked them, “Whose is this image and inscription?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Caesar’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to them, “Give therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard it, they marveled, and left him, and went away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On that day Sadducees (those who say that there is no resurrection) came to him. They asked him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there were with us seven brothers. The first married and died, and having no offspring left his wife to his brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, the second also, and the third, to the seventh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After them all, the woman died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the resurrection therefore, whose wife will she be of the seven? For they all had her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus answered them, “You are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in the resurrection they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like God’s angels in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, haven’t you read that which was spoken to you by God, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?’ God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the multitudes heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Pharisees, when they heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, gathered themselves together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of them, a lawyer, asked him a question, testing him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the law?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “ ‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the first and great commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A second likewise is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The whole law and the prophets depend on these two commandments.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “What do you think of the Christ? Whose son is he?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Of David.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “How then does David in the Spirit call him Lord, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one was able to answer him a word, neither did any man dare ask him any more questions from that day forward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “The scribes and the Pharisees sat on Moses’ seat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things therefore whatever they tell you to observe, observe and do, but don’t do their works; for they say, and don’t do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they bind heavy burdens that are grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not lift a finger to help them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they do all their works to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries  broad, enlarge the fringes of their garments,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and love the place of honor at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the salutations in the marketplaces, and to be called ‘Rabbi, Rabbi’ by men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But don’t you be called ‘Rabbi,’ for one is your teacher, the Christ, and all of you are brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Call no man on the earth your father, for one is your Father, he who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither be called masters, for one is your master, the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who is greatest among you will be your servant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows’ houses, and as a pretense you make long prayers. Therefore you will receive greater condemnation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you shut up the Kingdom of Heaven against men; for you don’t enter in yourselves, neither do you allow those who are entering in to enter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel around by sea and land to make one proselyte; and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of Gehenna as yourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, you blind guides, who say, ‘Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obligated.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifies the gold?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is obligated?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifies the gift?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He therefore who swears by the altar, swears by it, and by everything on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who swears by the temple, swears by it, and by him who has been living in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who swears by heaven, swears by the throne of God, and by him who sits on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and have left undone the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faith. But you ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You blind guides, who strain out a gnat, and swallow a camel!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of extortion and unrighteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and of the platter, that its outside may become clean also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitened tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but inwardly are full of dead men’s bones, and of all uncleanness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so you also outwardly appear righteous to men, but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and decorate the tombs of the righteous,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we wouldn’t have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore you testify to yourselves that you are children of those who killed the prophets.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You serpents, you offspring of vipers, how will you escape the judgment of Gehenna?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore behold, I send to you prophets, wise men, and scribes. Some of them you will kill and crucify; and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues, and persecute from city to city;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zachariah son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the sanctuary and the altar.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, all these things will come upon this generation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets, and stones those who are sent to her! How often I would have gathered your children together, even as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you would not!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, your house is left to you desolate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I tell you, you will not see me from now on, until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went out from the temple, and was going on his way. His disciples came to him to show him the buildings of the temple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered them, “You see all of these things, don’t you? Most certainly I tell you, there will not be left here one stone on another, that will not be thrown down.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign of your coming, and of the end of the age?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and will lead many astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you aren’t troubled, for all this must happen, but the end is not yet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there will be famines, plagues, and earthquakes in various places.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But all these things are the beginning of birth pains.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they will deliver you up to oppression, and will kill you. You will be hated by all of the nations for my name’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then many will stumble, and will deliver up one another, and will hate one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many false prophets will arise, and will lead many astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because iniquity will be multiplied, the love of many will grow cold.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who endures to the end will be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This Good News of the Kingdom will be preached in the whole world for a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When, therefore, you see the abomination of desolation,  which was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take out the things that are in his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let him who is in the field not return back to get his clothes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But woe to those who are with child and to nursing mothers in those days!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray that your flight will not be in the winter, nor on a Sabbath,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for then there will be great suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, nor ever will be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Unless those days had been shortened, no flesh would have been saved. But for the sake of the chosen ones, those days will be shortened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then if any man tells you, ‘Behold, here is the Christ,’ or, ‘There,’ don’t believe it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there will arise false christs, and false prophets, and they will show great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I have told you beforehand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore they tell you, ‘Behold, he is in the wilderness,’ don’t go out; or ‘Behold, he is in the inner rooms,’ don’t believe it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the lightning flashes from the east, and is seen even to the west, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For wherever the carcass is, that is where the vultures  gather together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But immediately after the suffering of those days, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky. Then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky with power and great glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will send out his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from one end of the sky to the other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now from the fig tree learn this parable. When its branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so you also, when you see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away, until all these things are accomplished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But no one knows of that day and hour, not even the angels of heaven, but my Father only.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “As the days of Noah were, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as in those days which were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ship,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they didn’t know until the flood came, and took them all away, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and one will be left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and one will be left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch therefore, for you don’t know in what hour your Lord comes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have watched, and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore also be ready, for in an hour that you don’t expect, the Son of Man will come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his lord has set over his household, to give them their food in due season?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you that he will set him over all that he has.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if that evil servant should say in his heart, ‘My lord is delaying his coming,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eat and drink with the drunkards,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the lord of that servant will come in a day when he doesn’t expect it, and in an hour when he doesn’t know it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will cut him in pieces, and appoint his portion with the hypocrites. That is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then the Kingdom of Heaven will be like ten virgins, who took their lamps, and went out to meet the bridegroom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Five of them were foolish, and five were wise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who were foolish, when they took their lamps, took no oil with them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while the bridegroom delayed, they all slumbered and slept.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But at midnight there was a cry, ‘Behold! The bridegroom is coming! Come out to meet him!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their lamps.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the wise answered, saying, ‘What if there isn’t enough for us and you? You go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they went away to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Afterward the other virgins also came, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered, ‘Most certainly I tell you, I don’t know you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch therefore, for you don’t know the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man is coming.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For it is like a man, going into another country, who called his own servants, and entrusted his goods to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one; to each according to his own ability. Then he went on his journey.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he who received the five talents went and traded with them, and made another five talents.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, he also who got the two gained another two.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who received the one talent went away and dug in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now after a long time the lord of those servants came, and reconciled accounts with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who received the five talents came and brought another five talents, saying, ‘Lord, you delivered to me five talents. Behold, I have gained another five talents in addition to them.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He also who got the two talents came and said, ‘Lord, you delivered to me two talents. Behold, I have gained another two talents in addition to them.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “His lord said to him, ‘Well done, good and faithful servant. You have been faithful over a few things, I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He also who had received the one talent came and said, ‘Lord, I knew you that you are a hard man, reaping where you didn’t sow, and gathering where you didn’t scatter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was afraid, and went away and hid your talent in the earth. Behold, you have what is yours.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But his lord answered him, ‘You wicked and slothful servant. You knew that I reap where I didn’t sow, and gather where I didn’t scatter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You ought therefore to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I should have received back my own with interest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take away therefore the talent from him, and give it to him who has the ten talents.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to everyone who has will be given, and he will have abundance, but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Throw out the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But when the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the holy angels with him, then he will sit on the throne of his glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Before him all the nations will be gathered, and he will separate them one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will set the sheep on his right hand, but the goats on the left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the King will tell those on his right hand, ‘Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the Kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I was hungry, and you gave me food to eat. I was thirsty, and you gave me drink. I was a stranger, and you took me in.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was naked, and you clothed me. I was sick, and you visited me. I was in prison, and you came to me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then the righteous will answer him, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, and feed you; or thirsty, and give you a drink?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When did we see you as a stranger, and take you in; or naked, and clothe you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When did we see you sick, or in prison, and come to you?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The King will answer them, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you did it to one of the least of these my brothers,  you did it to me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he will say also to those on the left hand, ‘Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire which is prepared for the devil and his angels;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I was hungry, and you didn’t give me food to eat; I was thirsty, and you gave me no drink;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was a stranger, and you didn’t take me in; naked, and you didn’t clothe me; sick, and in prison, and you didn’t visit me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then they will also answer, saying, ‘Lord, when did we see you hungry, or thirsty, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and didn’t help you?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Then he will answer them, saying, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you didn’t do it to one of the least of these, you didn’t do it to me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had finished all these words, he said to his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You know that after two days the Passover is coming, and the Son of Man will be delivered up to be crucified.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders of the people were gathered together in the court of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took counsel together that they might take Jesus by deceit, and kill him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they said, “Not during the feast, lest a riot occur among the people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: a woman came to him having an alabaster jar of very expensive ointment, and she poured it on his head as he sat at the table.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when his disciples saw this, they were indignant, saying, “Why this waste?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, knowing this, Jesus said to them, “Why do you trouble the woman? She has done a good work for me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you always have the poor with you; but you don’t always have me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in pouring this ointment on my body, she did it to prepare me for burial.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be spoken of as a memorial of her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then one of the twelve, who was called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “What are you willing to give me, that I should deliver him to you?” They weighed out for him thirty pieces of silver.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From that time he sought opportunity to betray him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the first day of unleavened bread, the disciples came to Jesus, saying to him, “Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Go into the city to a certain person, and tell him, ‘The Teacher says, “My time is at hand. I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.” ’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples did as Jesus commanded them, and they prepared the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when evening had come, he was reclining at the table with the twelve disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were eating, he said, “Most certainly I tell you that one of you will betray me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were exceedingly sorrowful, and each began to ask him, “It isn’t me, is it, Lord?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “He who dipped his hand with me in the dish will betray me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Son of Man goes, even as it is written of him, but woe to that man through whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas, who betrayed him, answered, “It isn’t me, is it, Rabbi?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “You said it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, gave thanks for it, and broke it. He gave to the disciples, and said, “Take, eat; this is my body.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took the cup, gave thanks, and gave to them, saying, “All of you drink it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for this is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many for the remission of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you that I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on, until that day when I drink it anew with you in my Father’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter answered him, “Even if all will be made to stumble because of you, I will never be made to stumble.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you that tonight, before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you.” All of the disciples also said likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane, and said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I go there and pray.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and severely troubled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch with me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went forward a little, fell on his face, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to the disciples, and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “What, couldn’t you watch with me for one hour?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch and pray, that you don’t enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, a second time he went away, and prayed, saying, “My Father, if this cup can’t pass away from me unless I drink it, your desire be done.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came again and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He left them again, went away, and prayed a third time, saying the same words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he came to his disciples, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. Behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Arise, let’s be going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was still speaking, behold, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and elders of the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he who betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, “Whoever I kiss, he is the one. Seize him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he came to Jesus, and said, “Hail, Rabbi!” and kissed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Friend, why are you here?” Then they came and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and struck off his ear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place, for all those who take the sword will die by the sword.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or do you think that I couldn’t ask my Father, and he would even now send me more than twelve legions of angels?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How then would the Scriptures be fulfilled that it must be so?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that hour Jesus said to the multitudes, “Have you come out as against a robber with swords and clubs to seize me? I sat daily in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But all this has happened that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then all the disciples left him and fled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who had taken Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter followed him from a distance, to the court of the high priest, and entered in and sat with the officers, to see the end.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the chief priests, the elders, and the whole council sought false testimony against Jesus, that they might put him to death;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they found none. Even though many false witnesses came forward, they found none. But at last two false witnesses came forward,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest stood up, and said to him, “Have you no answer? What is this that these testify against you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus held his peace. The high priest answered him, “I adjure you by the living God, that you tell us whether you are the Christ, the Son of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “You have said it. Nevertheless, I tell you, after this you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming on the clouds of the sky.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the high priest tore his clothing, saying, “He has spoken blasphemy! Why do we need any more witnesses? Behold, now you have heard his blasphemy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What do you think?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered, “He is worthy of death!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they spat in his face and beat him with their fists, and some slapped him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Prophesy to us, you Christ! Who hit you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Peter was sitting outside in the court, and a maid came to him, saying, “You were also with Jesus, the Galilean!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he denied it before them all, saying, “I don’t know what you are talking about.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had gone out onto the porch, someone else saw him, and said to those who were there, “This man also was with Jesus of Nazareth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he denied it with an oath, “I don’t know the man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After a little while those who stood by came and said to Peter, “Surely you are also one of them, for your speech makes you known.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he began to curse and to swear, “I don’t know the man!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the rooster crowed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter remembered the word which Jesus had said to him, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.” Then he went out and wept bitterly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when morning had come, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they bound him, and led him away, and delivered him up to Pontius Pilate, the governor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Judas, who betrayed him, when he saw that Jesus was condemned, felt remorse, and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “I have sinned in that I betrayed innocent blood.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they said, “What is that to us? You see to it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He threw down the pieces of silver in the sanctuary, and departed. He went away and hanged himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests took the pieces of silver, and said, “It’s not lawful to put them into the treasury, since it is the price of blood.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took counsel, and bought the potter’s field with them, to bury strangers in.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore that field was called “The Field of Blood” to this day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then that which was spoken through Jeremiah the prophet was fulfilled, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “So you say.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was accused by the chief priests and elders, he answered nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Pilate said to him, “Don’t you hear how many things they testify against you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He gave him no answer, not even one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release to the multitude one prisoner, whom they desired.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore they were gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus, who is called Christ?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he knew that because of envy they had delivered him up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, “Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the multitudes to ask for Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the governor answered them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Barabbas!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do to Jesus, who is called Christ?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all said to him, “Let him be crucified!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the governor said, “Why? What evil has he done?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they cried out exceedingly, saying, “Let him be crucified!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when Pilate saw that nothing was being gained, but rather that a disturbance was starting, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this righteous person. You see to it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the people answered, “May his blood be on us, and on our children!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he released to them Barabbas, but Jesus he flogged and delivered to be crucified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and gathered the whole garrison together against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They stripped him, and put a scarlet robe on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They braided a crown of thorns and put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and they kneeled down before him, and mocked him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They spat on him, and took the reed and struck him on the head.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had mocked him, they took the robe off him, and put his clothes on him, and led him away to crucify him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name, and they compelled him to go with them, that he might carry his cross.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came to a place called “Golgotha”, that is to say, “The place of a skull,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: they gave him sour wine to drink mixed with gall. When he had tasted it, he would not drink.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had crucified him, they divided his clothing among them, casting lots,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they sat and watched him there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They set up over his head the accusation against him written, “THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then there were two robbers crucified with him, one on his right hand and one on the left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saying, “You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise the chief priests also mocking, with the scribes, the Pharisees, and the elders, said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He saved others, but he can’t save himself. If he is the King of Israel, let him come down from the cross now, and we will believe in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He trusts in God. Let God deliver him now, if he wants him; for he said, ‘I am the Son of God.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The robbers also who were crucified with him cast on him the same reproach.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eli, Eli, lima sabachthani?”  That is, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of them who stood there, when they heard it, said, “This man is calling Elijah.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him a drink.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rest said, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to save him.”

sources.label: New Testament
