sources.text: Jesus cried again with a loud voice, and yielded up his spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection, they entered into the holy city and appeared to many.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the centurion, and those who were with him watching Jesus, when they saw the earthquake, and the things that were done, feared exceedingly, saying, “Truly this was the Son of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many women were there watching from afar, who had followed Jesus from Galilee, serving him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening had come, a rich man from Arimathaea, named Joseph, who himself was also Jesus’ disciple came.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph took the body, and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and laid it in his own new tomb, which he had cut out in the rock, and he rolled a great stone against the door of the tomb, and departed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the next day, which was the day after the Preparation Day, the chief priests and the Pharisees were gathered together to Pilate,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Sir, we remember what that deceiver said while he was still alive: ‘After three days I will rise again.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest perhaps his disciples come at night and steal him away, and tell the people, ‘He is risen from the dead;’ and the last deception will be worse than the first.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to them, “You have a guard. Go, make it as secure as you can.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they went with the guard and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now after the Sabbath, as it began to dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary came to see the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from the sky, and came and rolled away the stone from the door, and sat on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing white as snow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For fear of him, the guards shook, and became like dead men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel answered the women, “Don’t be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus, who has been crucified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is not here, for he has risen, just like he said. Come, see the place where the Lord was lying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Go quickly and tell his disciples, ‘He has risen from the dead, and behold, he goes before you into Galilee; there you will see him.’ Behold, I have told you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to bring his disciples word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went to tell his disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, “Rejoice!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came and took hold of his feet, and worshiped him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t be afraid. Go tell my brothers  that they should go into Galilee, and there they will see me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while they were going, behold, some of the guards came into the city, and told the chief priests all the things that had happened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave a large amount of silver to the soldiers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Say that his disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If this comes to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him and make you free of worry.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they took the money and did as they were told. This saying was spread abroad among the Jews, and continues until today.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the eleven disciples went into Galilee, to the mountain where Jesus had sent them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they saw him, they bowed down to him, but some doubted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus came to them and spoke to them, saying, “All authority has been given to me in heaven and on earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written in the prophets,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching the baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the country of Judea and all those of Jerusalem went out to him. They were baptized by him in the Jordan river, confessing their sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John was clothed with camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He preached, saying, “After me comes he who is mightier than I, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and loosen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I baptized you in water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately coming up from the water, he saw the heavens parting, and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A voice came out of the sky, “You are my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was there in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Good News of God’s Kingdom,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand! Repent, and believe in the Good News.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Passing along by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew, the brother of Simon, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you into fishers for men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately they left their nets, and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Going on a little further from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he called them, and they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired servants, and went after him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Ha! What do we have to do with you, Jesus, you Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The report of him went out immediately everywhere into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately, when they had come out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came and took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her immediately, and she served them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick, and those who were possessed by demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the city was gathered together at the door.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. He didn’t allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Early in the morning, while it was still dark, he rose up and went out, and departed into a deserted place, and prayed there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon and those who were with him searched for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They found him and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Let’s go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because I came out for this reason.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A leper came to him, begging him, kneeling down to him, and saying to him, “If you want to, you can make me clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being moved with compassion, he stretched out his hand, and touched him, and said to him, “I want to. Be made clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He strictly warned him, and immediately sent him out,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “See you say nothing to anybody, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he went out, and began to proclaim it much, and to spread about the matter, so that Jesus could no more openly enter into a city, but was outside in desert places. People came to him from everywhere.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins”—he said to the paralytic—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in that day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry—he, and those who were with him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How he entered into God’s house at the time of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the man who had his hand withered, “Stand up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.”  He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon (to whom he gave the name Peter);

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: James the son of Zebedee; and John, the brother of James, (whom he called Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder);

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he came into a house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him; for they said, “He is insane.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He summoned them, and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder unless he first binds the strong man; then he will plunder his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: —because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters are outside looking for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever does the will of God is my brother, my sister, and mother.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and devoured it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The farmer sows the word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket  or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever has, to him more will be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, though he doesn’t know how.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come out of the boat, immediately a man with an unclean spirit met him out of the tombs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He lived in the tombs. Nobody could bind him any more, not even with chains,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Always, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying out, and cutting himself with stones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked him, “What is your name?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “My name is Legion, for we are many.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He begged him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the mountainside there was a great herd of pigs feeding.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the demons begged him, saying, “Send us into the pigs, that we may enter into them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At once Jesus gave them permission. The unclean spirits came out and entered into the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and they were drowned in the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who fed them fled, and told it in the city and in the country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The people came to see what it was that had happened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who saw it declared to them what happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They began to beg him to depart from their region.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had crossed back over in the boat to the other side, a great multitude was gathered to him; and he was by the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, came; and seeing him, he fell at his feet,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and begged him much, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Please come and lay your hands on her, that she may be made healthy, and live.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain woman, who had a discharge of blood for twelve years,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and had suffered many things by many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better, but rather grew worse,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples said to him, “You see the multitude pressing against you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He looked around to see her who had done this thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus, when he heard the message spoken, immediately said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Don’t be afraid, only believe.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to the synagogue ruler’s house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child, her mother, and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, “Talitha cumi!”  which means, being interpreted, “Girl, I tell you, get up!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” They were offended at him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He marveled because of their unbelief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went around the villages teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out and preached that people should repent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, “John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, for he had married her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For John said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn’t,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, “Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He swore to her, “Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She went out, and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She said, “The head of John the Baptizer.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, “I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn’t wish to refuse her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John’s head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, “This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread, and give them something to eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they knew, they said, “Five, and two fish.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all ate, and were filled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea,  and he would have passed by them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, “Cheer up! It is I! Don’t be afraid.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they hadn’t understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might just touch the fringe of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered together to him, having come from Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews don’t eat unless they wash their hands and forearms, holding to the tradition of the elders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They don’t eat when they come from the marketplace unless they bathe themselves, and there are many other things, which they have received to hold to: washings of cups, pitchers, bronze vessels, and couches.)

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why don’t your disciples walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with unwashed hands?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For you set aside the commandment of God, and hold tightly to the tradition of men—the washing of pitchers and cups, and you do many other such things.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Full well do you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother;’ and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you say, ‘If a man tells his father or his mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban,” ’ ” that is to say, given to God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “then you no longer allow him to do anything for his father or his mother,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: making void the word of God by your tradition, which you have handed down. You do many things like this.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called all the multitude to himself, and said to them, “Hear me, all of you, and understand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is nothing from outside of the man, that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Are you also without understanding? Don’t you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can’t defile him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because it doesn’t go into his heart, but into his stomach, then into the latrine, making all foods clean?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For from within, out of the hearts of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, sexual sins, murders, thefts,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From there he arose, and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house, and didn’t want anyone to know it, but he couldn’t escape notice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell down at his feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by race. She begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to her, “Let the children be filled first, for it is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to her, “For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She went away to her house, and found the child having been laid on the bed, with the demon gone out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he departed from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and came to the sea of Galilee, through the middle of the region of Decapolis.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took him aside from the multitude, privately, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat, and touched his tongue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him, “Ephphatha!”  that is, “Be opened!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was released, and he spoke clearly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded them that they should tell no one, but the more he commanded them, so much the more widely they proclaimed it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear, and the mute speak!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In those days, when there was a very great multitude, and they had nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples to himself, and said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I have compassion on the multitude, because they have stayed with me now three days, and have nothing to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way, for some of them have come a long way.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples answered him, “From where could one satisfy these people with bread here in a deserted place?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Seven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground, and he took the seven loaves. Having given thanks, he broke them, and gave them to his disciples to serve, and they served the multitude.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They had a few small fish. Having blessed them, he said to serve these also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who had eaten were about four thousand. Then he sent them away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he entered into the boat with his disciples, and came into the region of Dalmanutha.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees came out and began to question him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, and testing him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sighed deeply in his spirit, and said, “Why does this generation seek a sign? Most certainly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He left them, and again entering into the boat, departed to the other side.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They forgot to take bread; and they didn’t have more than one loaf in the boat with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He warned them, saying, “Take heed: beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They reasoned with one another, saying, “It’s because we have no bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, perceiving it, said to them, “Why do you reason that it’s because you have no bread? Don’t you perceive yet, neither understand? Is your heart still hardened?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having eyes, don’t you see? Having ears, don’t you hear? Don’t you remember?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I broke the five loaves among the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “Twelve.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When the seven loaves fed the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “Seven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked them, “Don’t you understand yet?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to Bethsaida. They brought a blind man to him, and begged him to touch him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took hold of the blind man by the hand, and brought him out of the village. When he had spat on his eyes, and laid his hands on him, he asked him if he saw anything.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He looked up, and said, “I see men; for I see them like trees walking.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then again he laid his hands on his eyes. He looked intently, and was restored, and saw everyone clearly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent him away to his house, saying, “Don’t enter into the village, nor tell anyone in the village.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went out, with his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, “Who do men say that I am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “John the Baptizer, and others say Elijah, but others: one of the prophets.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered, “You are the Christ.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded them that they should tell no one about him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke to them openly. Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, turning around, and seeing his disciples, rebuked Peter, and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you have in mind not the things of God, but the things of men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called the multitude to himself with his disciples, and said to them, “Whoever wants to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it; and whoever will lose his life for my sake and the sake of the Good News will save it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what does it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and forfeit his life?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what will a man give in exchange for his life?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also will be ashamed of him, when he comes in his Father’s glory, with the holy angels.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste death until they see God’s Kingdom come with power.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John, and brought them up onto a high mountain privately by themselves, and he was changed into another form in front of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His clothing became glistening, exceedingly white, like snow, such as no launderer on earth can whiten them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Elijah and Moses appeared to them, and they were talking with Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he didn’t know what to say, for they were very afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A cloud came, overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Suddenly looking around, they saw no one with them any more, except Jesus only.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were coming down from the mountain, he commanded them that they should tell no one what things they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They kept this saying to themselves, questioning what the “rising from the dead” meant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, saying, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and restores all things. How is it written about the Son of Man, that he should suffer many things and be despised?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they have also done to him whatever they wanted to, even as it is written about him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Coming to the disciples, he saw a great multitude around them, and scribes questioning them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately all the multitude, when they saw him, were greatly amazed, and running to him, greeted him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked the scribes, “What are you asking them?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the multitude answered, “Teacher, I brought to you my son, who has a mute spirit;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and wherever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams at the mouth, and grinds his teeth, and wastes away. I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they weren’t able.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered him, “Unbelieving generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought him to him, and when he saw him, immediately the spirit convulsed him, and he fell on the ground, wallowing and foaming at the mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked his father, “How long has it been since this has come to him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “From childhood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Often it has cast him both into the fire and into the water to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us, and help us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the father of the child cried out with tears, “I believe. Help my unbelief!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus saw that a multitude came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to him, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After crying out and convulsing him greatly, it came out of him. The boy became like one dead, so much that most of them said, “He is dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus took him by the hand, and raised him up; and he arose.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why couldn’t we cast it out?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “This kind can come out by nothing, except by prayer and fasting.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out from there, and passed through Galilee. He didn’t want anyone to know it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he was teaching his disciples, and said to them, “The Son of Man is being handed over to the hands of men, and they will kill him; and when he is killed, on the third day he will rise again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they didn’t understand the saying, and were afraid to ask him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to Capernaum, and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing among yourselves on the way?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they were silent, for they had disputed with one another on the way about who was the greatest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sat down, and called the twelve; and he said to them, “If any man wants to be first, he shall be last of all, and servant of all.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took a little child, and set him in the middle of them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Whoever receives one such little child in my name, receives me, and whoever receives me, doesn’t receive me, but him who sent me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone who doesn’t follow us casting out demons in your name; and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said, “Don’t forbid him, for there is no one who will do a mighty work in my name, and be able quickly to speak evil of me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever is not against us is on our side.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever will give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because you are Christ’s, most certainly I tell you, he will in no way lose his reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever will cause one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if he were thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around his neck.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed, rather than having your two hands to go into Gehenna,  into the unquenchable fire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life lame, rather than having your two feet to be cast into Gehenna,  into the fire that will never be quenched—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If your eye causes you to stumble, cast it out. It is better for you to enter into God’s Kingdom with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna of fire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose from there and came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan. Multitudes came together to him again. As he usually did, he was again teaching them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pharisees came to him testing him, and asked him, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “What did Moses command you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Moses allowed a certificate of divorce to be written, and to divorce her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to them, “For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male and female.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will join to his wife,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the two will become one flesh, so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife, and marries another, commits adultery against her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a woman herself divorces her husband, and marries another, she commits adultery.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were bringing to him little children, that he should touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation, and said to them, “Allow the little children to come to me! Don’t forbid them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, whoever will not receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took them in his arms, and blessed them, laying his hands on them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except one—God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not give false testimony,’ ‘Do not defraud,’ ‘Honor your father and mother.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him, “One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus looked around, and said to his disciples, “How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, “Children, how hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him, “Then who can be saved?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, looking at them, said, “With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter began to tell him, “Behold, we have left all, and have followed you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the sake of the Good News,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time: houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But many who are first will be last; and the last first.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “What do you want me to do for you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand, and one at your left hand, in your glory.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to them, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “We are able.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant toward James and John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus summoned them, and said to them, “You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever of you wants to become first among you, shall be bondservant of all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho, with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out, and say, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus stood still, and said, “Call him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They called the blind man, saying to him, “Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus asked him, “What do you want me to do for you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The blind man said to him, “Rabboni, that I may see again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your faith has made you well.”  Immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus on the way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him, and bring him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs him;’ and immediately he will send him back here.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of those who stood there asked them, “What are you doing, untying the young donkey?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought the young donkey to Jesus, and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees, and spreading them on the road.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who went in front, and those who followed, cried out, “Hosanna!  Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus told it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again!”  and his disciples heard it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple, and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables, and the seats of those who sold the doves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He taught, saying to them, “Isn’t it written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations?’ But you have made it a den of robbers!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening came, he went out of the city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter, remembering, said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ and doesn’t doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening; he shall have whatever he says.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The baptism of John—was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we should say, ‘From heaven;’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we should say, ‘From men’ ”—they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine press, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he sent another; and they killed him; and many others, beating some, and killing some.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore still having one, his beloved son, he sent him last to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But those farmers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took him, killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Haven’t you even read this Scripture:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They tried to seize him, but they feared the multitude; for they perceived that he spoke the parable against them. They left him, and went away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sent some of the Pharisees and the Herodians to him, that they might trap him with words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come, they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and don’t defer to anyone; for you aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Shall we give, or shall we not give?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, “Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius, that I may see it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Whose is this image and inscription?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Caesar’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
