sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They marveled greatly at him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection, came to him. They asked him, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us, ‘If a man’s brother dies, and leaves a wife behind him, and leaves no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second took her, and died, leaving no children behind him. The third likewise;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t this because you are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when they will rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But about the dead, that they are raised; haven’t you read in the book of Moses, about the Bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are therefore badly mistaken.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the scribes came, and heard them questioning together, and knowing that he had answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the greatest of all?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “The greatest is, ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’ This is the first commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second is like this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribe said to him, “Truly, teacher, you have said well that he is one, and there is none other but he,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one dared ask him any question after that.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus responded, as he taught in the temple, “How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The common people heard him gladly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In his teaching he said to them, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and to get greetings in the marketplaces,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: those who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury, and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A poor widow came, and she cast in two small brass coins, which equal a quadrans coin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called his disciples to himself, and said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they all gave out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, gave all that she had to live on.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he went out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, see what kind of stones and what kind of buildings!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, answering, began to tell them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and will lead many astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But watch yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will stand before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they lead you away and deliver you up, don’t be anxious beforehand, or premeditate what you will say, but say whatever will be given you in that hour. For it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end will be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when you see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not” (let the reader understand), “then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let him who is in the field not return back to take his cloak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But woe to those who are with child and to those who nurse babies in those days!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray that your flight won’t be in the winter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he picked out, he shortened the days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ don’t believe it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there will arise false christs and false prophets, and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you watch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But in those days, after that oppression, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he will send out his angels, and will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the sky.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now from the fig tree, learn this parable. When the branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But of that day or that hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch, keep alert, and pray; for you don’t know when the time is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “It is like a man, traveling to another country, having left his house, and given authority to his servants, and to each one his work, and also commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming, whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: lest coming suddenly he might find you sleeping.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What I tell you, I tell all: Watch.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was now two days before the feast of the Passover and the unleavened bread, and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might seize him by deception, and kill him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they said, “Not during the feast, because there might be a riot among the people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster jar of ointment of pure nard—very costly. She broke the jar, and poured it over his head.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, saying, “Why has this ointment been wasted?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii,  and given to the poor.” So they grumbled against her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want to, you can do them good; but you will not always have me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body beforehand for the burying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News may be preached throughout the whole world, that which this woman has done will also be spoken of for a memorial of her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests, that he might deliver him to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, when they heard it, were glad, and promised to give him money. He sought how he might conveniently deliver him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare that you may eat the Passover?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent two of his disciples, and said to them, “Go into the city, and there you will meet a man carrying a pitcher of water. Follow him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and wherever he enters in, tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?” ’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found things as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was evening he came with the twelve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me—he who eats with me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They began to be sorrowful, and to ask him one by one, “Surely not I?” And another said, “Surely not I?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “It is one of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man goes, even as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed, he broke it, and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, I will no more drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it anew in God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter said to him, “Although all will be offended, yet I will not.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you, that you today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to a place which was named Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I pray.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be greatly troubled and distressed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you watch one hour?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he went away, and prayed, saying the same words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he returned, and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they didn’t know what to answer him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came the third time, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Arise! Let’s get going. Behold: he who betrays me is at hand.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came—and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he who betrayed him had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I will kiss, that is he. Seize him, and lead him away safely.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come, immediately he came to him, and said, “Rabbi! Rabbi!” and kissed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They laid their hands on him, and seized him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But a certain one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. But this is so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all left him, and fled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes came together with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the chief priests and the whole council sought witnesses against Jesus to put him to death, and found none.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony didn’t agree with each other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some stood up, and gave false testimony against him, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, their testimony didn’t agree.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest stood up in the middle, and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it which these testify against you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he stayed quiet, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “I am. You will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of the sky.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest tore his clothes, and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?” They all condemned him to be worthy of death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with fists, and to tell him, “Prophesy!” The officers struck him with the palms of their hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maids of the high priest came,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him, and said, “You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know, nor understand what you are saying.” He went out on the porch, and the rooster crowed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The maid saw him, and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he again denied it. After a little while again those who stood by said to Peter, “You truly are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he began to curse, and to swear, “I don’t know this man of whom you speak!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rooster crowed the second time. Peter remembered the word, how that Jesus said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” When he thought about that, he wept.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately in the morning the chief priests, with the elders and scribes, and the whole council, held a consultation, bound Jesus, carried him away, and delivered him up to Pilate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “So you say.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests accused him of many things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they testify against you!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate marveled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now at the feast he used to release to them one prisoner, whom they asked of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was one called Barabbas, bound with his fellow insurgents, men who in the insurrection had committed murder.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do as he always did for them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he perceived that for envy the chief priests had delivered him up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the chief priests stirred up the multitude, that he should release Barabbas to them instead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate again asked them, “What then should I do to him whom you call the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cried out again, “Crucify him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has he done?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they cried out exceedingly, “Crucify him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate, wishing to please the multitude, released Barabbas to them, and handed over Jesus, when he had flogged him, to be crucified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The soldiers led him away within the court, which is the Praetorium; and they called together the whole cohort.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They clothed him with purple, and weaving a crown of thorns, they put it on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They struck his head with a reed, and spat on him, and bowing their knees, did homage to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had mocked him, they took the purple off him, and put his own garments on him. They led him out to crucify him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them, that he might bear his cross.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought him to the place called Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, “The place of a skull.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They offered him wine mixed with myrrh to drink, but he didn’t take it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Crucifying him, they parted his garments among them, casting lots on them, what each should take.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was the third hour,  and they crucified him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The superscription of his accusation was written over him, “THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: With him they crucified two robbers; one on his right hand, and one on his left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Scripture was fulfilled, which says, “He was counted with transgressors.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying, “Ha! You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise, also the chief priests mocking among themselves with the scribes said, “He saved others. He can’t save himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, now come down from the cross, that we may see and believe him.”  Those who were crucified with him also insulted him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the sixth hour  had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?” which is, being interpreted, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of those who stood by, when they heard it, said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One ran, and filling a sponge full of vinegar, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to take him down.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus cried out with a loud voice, and gave up the spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the centurion, who stood by opposite him, saw that he cried out like this and breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were also women watching from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, when he was in Galilee, followed him and served him; and many other women who came up with him to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening had now come, because it was the Preparation Day, that is, the day before the Sabbath,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph of Arimathaea, a prominent council member who also himself was looking for God’s Kingdom, came. He boldly went in to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate marveled if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead long.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he found out from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He bought a linen cloth, and taking him down, wound him in the linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb which had been cut out of a rock. He rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of Joses, saw where he was laid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that they might come and anoint him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were saying among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for it was very big. Looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled back.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were amazed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Don’t be amazed. You seek Jesus, the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen. He is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, as he said to you.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out, and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had come on them. They said nothing to anyone; for they were afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when he had risen early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard that he was alive, and had been seen by her, they disbelieved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things he was revealed in another form to two of them, as they walked, on their way into the country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went away and told it to the rest. They didn’t believe them, either.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Afterward he was revealed to the eleven themselves as they sat at the table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they didn’t believe those who had seen him after he had risen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Go into all the world, and preach the Good News to the whole creation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who disbelieves will be condemned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new languages;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: they will take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it will in no way hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then the Lord, after he had spoken to them, was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word by the signs that followed. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Since many have undertaken to set in order a narrative concerning those matters which have been fulfilled among us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word delivered them to us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: it seemed good to me also, having traced the course of all things accurately from the first, to write to you in order, most excellent Theophilus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you might know the certainty concerning the things in which you were instructed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the priestly division of Abijah. He had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they both were well advanced in years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his division

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to enter into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The whole multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of incense.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: An angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Zacharias was troubled when he saw him, and fear fell upon him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the angel said to him, “Don’t be afraid, Zacharias, because your request has been heard. Your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and he will drink no wine nor strong drink. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother’s womb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, ‘to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,’ and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to prepare a people prepared for the Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you and to bring you this good news.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, you will be silent and not able to speak until the day that these things will happen, because you didn’t believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and they marveled that he delayed in the temple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came out, he could not speak to them. They perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple. He continued making signs to them, and remained mute.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the days of his service were fulfilled, he departed to his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these days Elizabeth his wife conceived, and she hid herself five months, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Thus has the Lord done to me in the days in which he looked at me, to take away my reproach among men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man whose name was Joseph, of David’s house. The virgin’s name was Mary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having come in, the angel said to her, “Rejoice, you highly favored one! The Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when she saw him, she was greatly troubled at the saying, and considered what kind of salutation this might be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel said to her, “Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, you will conceive in your womb and give birth to a son, and shall name him ‘Jesus.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father David,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. There will be no end to his Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, seeing I am a virgin?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, Elizabeth your relative also has conceived a son in her old age; and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For nothing spoken by God is impossible.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary said, “Behold, the servant of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel departed from her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and entered into the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She called out with a loud voice and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For behold, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary stayed with her about three months, and then returned to her house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the time that Elizabeth should give birth was fulfilled, and she gave birth to a son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had magnified his mercy toward her, and they rejoiced with her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His mother answered, “Not so; but he will be called John.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They made signs to his father, what he would have him called.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, “His name is John.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all marveled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His mouth was opened immediately and his tongue freed, and he spoke, blessing God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fear came on all who lived around them, and all these sayings were talked about throughout all the hill country of Judea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, “What then will this child be?” The hand of the Lord was with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The child was growing and becoming strong in spirit, and was in the desert until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be enrolled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to David’s city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to enroll himself with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him as wife, being pregnant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they were there, the day had come for her to give birth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a feeding trough, because there was no room for them in the inn.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were shepherds in the same country staying in the field, and keeping watch by night over their flock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the sign to you: you will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a feeding trough.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Suddenly, there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly army praising God, and saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the angels went away from them into the sky, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem, now, and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby was lying in the feeding trough.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they saw it, they publicized widely the saying which was spoken to them about this child.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who heard it wondered at the things which were spoken to them by the shepherds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, just as it was told them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When eight days were fulfilled for the circumcision of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came in the Spirit into the temple. When the parents brought in the child, Jesus, that they might do concerning him according to the custom of the law,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then he received him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things which were spoken concerning him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary, his mother, “Behold, this child is set for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which is spoken against.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul, that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher (she was of a great age, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and she had been a widow for about eighty-four years), who didn’t depart from the temple, worshiping with fastings and petitions night and day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His parents went every year to Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and when they had fulfilled the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. Joseph and his mother didn’t know it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but supposing him to be in the company, they went a day’s journey, and they looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they didn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, looking for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the middle of the teachers, both listening to them, and asking them questions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us this way? Behold, your father and I were anxiously looking for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t understand the saying which he spoke to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth. He was subject to them, and his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came into all the region around the Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for remission of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said therefore to the multitudes who went out to be baptized by him, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore produce fruits worthy of repentance, and don’t begin to say among yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father;’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even now the ax also lies at the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitudes asked him, “What then must we do?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “He who has two coats, let him give to him who has none. He who has food, let him do likewise.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Tax collectors also came to be baptized, and they said to him, “Teacher, what must we do?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Collect no more than that which is appointed to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Soldiers also asked him, saying, “What about us? What must we do?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Extort from no one by violence, neither accuse anyone wrongfully. Be content with your wages.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As the people were in expectation, and all men reasoned in their hearts concerning John, whether perhaps he was the Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John answered them all, “I indeed baptize you with water, but he comes who is mightier than I, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to loosen. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit and fire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor, and will gather the wheat into his barn; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then with many other exhortations he preached good news to the people,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Herod the tetrarch, being reproved by him for Herodias, his brother’s  wife, and for all the evil things which Herod had done,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: added this also to them all, that he shut up John in prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when all the people were baptized, Jesus also had been baptized, and was praying. The sky was opened,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form like a dove on him; and a voice came out of the sky, saying “You are my beloved Son. In you I am well pleased.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus himself, when he began to teach, was about thirty years old, being the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Amminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The devil said to him, “I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answering, said to him, “It has been said, ‘You shall not tempt the Lord your God.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He began to tell them, “Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Doubtless you will tell me this parable, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, passing through the middle of them, went his way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are: the Holy One of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”  When the demon had thrown him down in the middle of them, he came out of him, having done him no harm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Demons also came out of many, crying out, and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn’t go away from them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “I must preach the good news of God’s Kingdom to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon answered him, “Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately the leprosy left him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded him to tell no one, “But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On one of those days, he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the middle before Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, “Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you;’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” (he said to the paralyzed man), “I tell you, arise, take up your cot, and go to your house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, “Follow me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the second Sabbath after the first, he was going through the grain fields. His disciples plucked the heads of grain and ate, rubbing them in their hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some of the Pharisees said to them, “Why do you do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, answering them, said, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, he, and those who were with him;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how he entered into God’s house, and took and ate the show bread, and gave also to those who were with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It also happened on another Sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught. There was a man there, and his right hand was withered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might find an accusation against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he knew their thoughts; and he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Rise up, and stand in the middle.” He arose and stood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to them, “I will ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He looked around at them all, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they were filled with rage, and talked with one another about what they might do to Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In these days, he went out to the mountain to pray, and he continued all night in prayer to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was day, he called his disciples, and from them he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, his brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called the Zealot;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who also became a traitor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came down with them, and stood on a level place, with a crowd of his disciples, and a great number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as well as those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and they were being healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out of him and healed them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But I tell you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don’t withhold your coat also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “As you would like people to do to you, do exactly so to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive back as much.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke a parable to them. “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or how can you tell your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is no good tree that produces rotten fruit; nor again a rotten tree that produces good fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people don’t gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bramble bush.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings out that which is good, and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart brings out that which is evil, for out of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things which I say?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words, and does them, I will show you who he is like.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock. When a flood arose, the stream broke against that house, and could not shake it, because it was founded on the rock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who hears, and doesn’t do, is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After he had finished speaking in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to do this for him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I didn’t even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Soon afterwards, he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when he came near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, “Don’t cry.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples of John told him about all these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When John’s messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is he of whom it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in God’s Kingdom is greater than he.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To what then should I compare the people of this generation? What are they like?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call to one another, saying, ‘We piped to you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned, and you didn’t weep.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wisdom is justified by all her children.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee’s house, and sat at the table.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee’s house, brought an alabaster jar of ointment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”

sources.label: New Testament
