sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Teacher, say on.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But one to whom little is forgiven, loves little.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Soon afterwards, he went about through cities and villages, preaching and bringing the good news of God’s Kingdom. With him were the twelve,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others; who served them from their possessions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When a great multitude came together, and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The farmer went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell along the road, and it was trampled under foot, and the birds of the sky devoured it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Other seed fell on the rock, and as soon as it grew, it withered away, because it had no moisture.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Other fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it, and choked it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Other fell into the good ground, and grew, and produced one hundred times as much fruit.” As he said these things, he called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then his disciples asked him, “What does this parable mean?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of God’s Kingdom, but to the rest in parables; that ‘seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those along the road are those who hear, then the devil comes, and takes away the word from their heart, that they may not believe and be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those on the rock are they who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; but these have no root, who believe for a while, then fall away in time of temptation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which fell among the thorns, these are those who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with cares, riches, and pleasures of life, and bring no fruit to maturity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those in the good ground, these are those who with an honest and good heart, having heard the word, hold it tightly, and produce fruit with perseverance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “No one, when he has lit a lamp, covers it with a container, or puts it under a bed; but puts it on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For nothing is hidden that will not be revealed; nor anything secret that will not be known and come to light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be careful therefore how you hear. For whoever has, to him will be given; and whoever doesn’t have, from him will be taken away even that which he thinks he has.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His mother and brothers came to him, and they could not come near him for the crowd.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some people told him, “Your mother and your brothers stand outside, desiring to see you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered them, “My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God, and do it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on one of those days, he entered into a boat, himself and his disciples, and he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side of the lake.” So they launched out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as they sailed, he fell asleep. A wind storm came down on the lake, and they were taking on dangerous amounts of water.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to him, and awoke him, saying, “Master, master, we are dying!” He awoke, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water, and they ceased, and it was calm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” Being afraid they marveled, saying to one another, “Who is this then, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus stepped ashore, a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him. He wore no clothes, and didn’t live in a house, but in the tombs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, “What do I have to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don’t torment me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Jesus was commanding the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For the unclean spirit had often seized the man. He was kept under guard, and bound with chains and fetters. Breaking the bonds apart, he was driven by the demon into the desert.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus asked him, “What is your name?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Legion,” for many demons had entered into him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain, and they begged him that he would allow them to enter into those. Then he allowed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The demons came out of the man, and entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake, and were drowned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When those who fed them saw what had happened, they fled and told it in the city and in the country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: People went out to see what had happened. They came to Jesus and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus’ feet, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the people of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, for they were very much afraid. Then he entered into the boat and returned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the man from whom the demons had gone out begged him that he might go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Return to your house, and declare what great things God has done for you.” He went his way, proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a man named Jairus came. He was a ruler of the synagogue. He fell down at Jesus’ feet, and begged him to come into his house,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she was dying. But as he went, the multitudes pressed against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians and could not be healed by any

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: came behind him, and touched the fringe of his cloak. Immediately the flow of her blood stopped.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Who touched me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When all denied it, Peter and those with him said, “Master, the multitudes press and jostle you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said, “Someone did touch me, for I perceived that power has gone out of me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him declared to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to her, “Daughter, cheer up. Your faith has made you well. Go in peace.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he still spoke, one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house came, saying to him, “Your daughter is dead. Don’t trouble the Teacher.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus hearing it, answered him, “Don’t be afraid. Only believe, and she will be healed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came to the house, he didn’t allow anyone to enter in, except Peter, John, James, the father of the child, and her mother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All were weeping and mourning her, but he said, “Don’t weep. She isn’t dead, but sleeping.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were ridiculing him, knowing that she was dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he put them all outside, and taking her by the hand, he called, saying, “Child, arise!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Her spirit returned, and she rose up immediately. He commanded that something be given to her to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Her parents were amazed, but he commanded them to tell no one what had been done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called the twelve together, and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent them out to preach God’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey—no staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money. Don’t have two coats each.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Into whatever house you enter, stay there, and depart from there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As many as don’t receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They departed and went throughout the villages, preaching the Good News and healing everywhere.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him; and he was very perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Herod said, “I beheaded John, but who is this about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The apostles, when they had returned, told him what things they had done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took them and withdrew apart to a desert region of a city called Bethsaida.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the multitudes, perceiving it, followed him. He welcomed them, spoke to them of God’s Kingdom, and he cured those who needed healing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The day began to wear away; and the twelve came and said to him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms, and lodge, and get food, for we are here in a deserted place.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they were about five thousand men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to his disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They did so, and made them all sit down.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They ate and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he asked them, “Who do the multitudes say that I am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered, “ ‘John the Baptizer,’ but others say, ‘Elijah,’ and others, that one of the old prophets has risen again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he warned them, and commanded them to tell this to no one,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to all, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake, will save it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits his own self?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed, when he comes in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here who will in no way taste of death until they see God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About eight days after these sayings, he took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up onto the mountain to pray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, two men were talking with him, who were Moses and Elijah,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who appeared in glory, and spoke of his departure, which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they were fully awake, they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah,” not knowing what he said.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he said these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great multitude met him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a man from the crowd called out, saying, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a spirit takes him, he suddenly cries out, and it convulses him so that he foams, and it hardly departs from him, bruising him severely.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they couldn’t.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were all astonished at the majesty of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Let these words sink into your ears, for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they didn’t understand this saying. It was concealed from them, that they should not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: An argument arose among them about which of them was the greatest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow with us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Don’t forbid him, for he who is not against us is for us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and sent messengers before his face. They went and entered into a village of the Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t receive him, because he was traveling with his face set toward Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When his disciples, James and John, saw this, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky, and destroy them, just as Elijah did?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man didn’t come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went to another village.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to another, “Follow me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another also said, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first allow me to say good-bye to those who are at my house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to him, “No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him into every city and place where he was about to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to them, “The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don’t go from house to house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Heal the sick who are there, and tell them, ‘God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But into whatever city you enter, and they don’t receive you, go out into its streets and say,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, “I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Turning to the disciples, he said, “All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus, “Who is my neighbor?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, gave them to the host, and said to him, ‘Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “He who showed mercy on him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he finished praying in a certain place, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “When you pray, say,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Which of you, if you go to a friend at midnight, and tell him, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he from within will answer and say, ‘Don’t bother me. The door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed. I can’t get up and give it to you’?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, although he will not rise and give it to him because he is his friend, yet because of his persistence, he will get up and give him as many as he needs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I tell you, keep asking, and it will be given you. Keep seeking, and you will find. Keep knocking, and it will be opened to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Which of you fathers, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, he won’t give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or if he asks for an egg, he won’t give him a scorpion, will he?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was casting out a demon, and it was mute. When the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke; and the multitudes marveled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some of them said, “He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others, testing him, sought from him a sign from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation. A house divided against itself falls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if I by God’s finger cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When the strong man, fully armed, guards his own dwelling, his goods are safe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when someone stronger attacks him and overcomes him, he takes from him his whole armor in which he trusted, and divides his plunder.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He that is not with me is against me. He who doesn’t gather with me scatters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The unclean spirit, when he has gone out of the man, passes through dry places, seeking rest, and finding none, he says, ‘I will turn back to my house from which I came out.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he returns, he finds it swept and put in order.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he goes, and takes seven other spirits more evil than himself, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It came to pass, as he said these things, a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice, and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that bore you, and the breasts which nursed you!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said, “On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God, and keep it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the multitudes were gathering together to him, he began to say, “This is an evil generation. It seeks after a sign. No sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah, the prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so the Son of Man will also be to this generation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and will condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, one greater than Solomon is here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, one greater than Jonah is here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “No one, when he has lit a lamp, puts it in a cellar or under a basket, but on a stand, that those who come in may see the light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore when your eye is good, your whole body is also full of light; but when it is evil, your body also is full of darkness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore see whether the light that is in you isn’t darkness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore your whole body is full of light, having no part dark, it will be wholly full of light, as when the lamp with its bright shining gives you light.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now as he spoke, a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him. He went in, and sat at the table.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the Pharisee saw it, he marveled that he had not first washed himself before dinner.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter, but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You foolish ones, didn’t he who made the outside make the inside also?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But give for gifts to the needy those things which are within, and behold, all things will be clean to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb, but you bypass justice and God’s love. You ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the best seats in the synagogues, and the greetings in the marketplaces.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like hidden graves, and the men who walk over them don’t know it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the lawyers answered him, “Teacher, in saying this you insult us also.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Woe to you lawyers also! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won’t even lift one finger to help carry those burdens.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers. For they killed them, and you build their tombs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore also the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.’ Yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to you lawyers! For you took away the key of knowledge. You didn’t enter in yourselves, and those who were entering in, you hindered.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be terribly angry, and to draw many things out of him;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch him in something he might say, that they might accuse him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Meanwhile, when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together, so much so that they trampled on each other, he began to tell his disciples first of all, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there is nothing covered up that will not be revealed, nor hidden that will not be known.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I tell you, my friends, don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I will warn you whom you should fear. Fear him who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna. Yes, I tell you, fear him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Aren’t five sparrows sold for two assaria coins? Not one of them is forgotten by God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the very hairs of your head are all counted. Therefore don’t be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I tell you, everyone who confesses me before men, the Son of Man will also confess before the angels of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of God’s angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, don’t be anxious how or what you will answer, or what you will say;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the multitude said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to him, “Man, who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Beware! Keep yourselves from covetousness, for a man’s life doesn’t consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke a parable to them, saying, “The ground of a certain rich man produced abundantly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He reasoned within himself, saying, ‘What will I do, because I don’t have room to store my crops?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, ‘This is what I will do. I will pull down my barns, build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will tell my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years. Take your ease, eat, drink, and be merry.” ’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But God said to him, ‘You foolish one, tonight your soul is required of you. The things which you have prepared—whose will they be?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Consider the ravens: they don’t sow, they don’t reap, they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit to his height?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then you aren’t able to do even the least things, why are you anxious about the rest?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Consider the lilies, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the nations of the world seek after all of these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But seek God’s Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Sell that which you have, and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don’t grow old, a treasure in the heavens that doesn’t fail, where no thief approaches, neither moth destroys.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Let your waist be dressed and your lamps burning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be like men watching for their lord, when he returns from the wedding feast; that when he comes and knocks, they may immediately open to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most certainly I tell you that he will dress himself, make them recline, and will come and serve them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch, and finds them so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not allowed his house to be broken into.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don’t expect him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everybody?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the right times?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Truly I tell you, that he will set him over all that he has.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My lord delays his coming,’ and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn’t expecting him, and in an hour that he doesn’t know, and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That servant, who knew his lord’s will, and didn’t prepare, nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he who didn’t know, and did things worthy of stripes, will be beaten with few stripes. To whomever much is given, of him will much be required; and to whom much was entrusted, of him more will be asked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I came to throw fire on the earth. I wish it were already kindled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For from now on, there will be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the multitudes also, “When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it happens.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When a south wind blows, you say, ‘There will be a scorching heat,’ and it happens.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don’t interpret this time?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there, until you have paid the very last penny.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell and killed them; do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke this parable. “A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it, and found none.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down. Why does it waste the soil?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, ‘Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it and fertilize it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years. She was bent over, and could in no way straighten herself up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight and glorified God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, “There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath, and lead him away to water?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “What is God’s Kingdom like? To what shall I compare it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and put in his own garden. It grew and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky live in its branches.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he said, “To what shall I compare God’s Kingdom?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures  of flour, until it was all leavened.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One said to him, “Lord, are they few who are saved?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in and will not be able.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When once the master of the house has risen up, and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside and to knock at the door, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’ then he will answer and tell you, ‘I don’t know you or where you come from.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, “Get out of here, and go away, for Herod wants to kill you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can’t be that a prophet would perish outside of Jerusalem.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they were silent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took him, and healed him, and let him go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “Which of you, if your son or an ox fell into a well, wouldn’t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They couldn’t answer him regarding these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When you are invited by anyone to a wedding feast, don’t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and you will be blessed, because they don’t have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, “Blessed is he who will feast in God’s Kingdom!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to him, “A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all as one began to make excuses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Another said, ‘I have married a wife, and therefore I can’t come.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The servant said, ‘Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The lord said to the servant, ‘Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If anyone comes to me, and doesn’t disregard his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can’t be my disciple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever doesn’t bear his own cross, and come after me, can’t be my disciple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn’t first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, ‘This man began to build, and wasn’t able to finish.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy, and asks for conditions of peace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So therefore whoever of you who doesn’t renounce all that he has, he can’t be my disciple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He told them this parable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or what woman, if she had ten drachma coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “A certain man had two sons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ He divided his livelihood between them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.” ’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and sandals on his feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let’s eat, and celebrate;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ Then they began to celebrate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when this your son came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also said to his disciples, “There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called him, and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Calling each one of his lord’s debtors to him, he said to the first, ‘How much do you owe to my lord?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, ‘A hundred batos of oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to another, ‘How much do you owe?’ He said, ‘A hundred cors of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. You aren’t able to serve God and Mammon.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Good News of God’s Kingdom is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was taken to his gate, full of sores,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beggar died, and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom. The rich man also died, and was buried.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In Hades, he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He cried and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But Abraham said, ‘Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in the same way, bad things. But here he is now comforted, and you are in anguish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that no one may cross over from there to us.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said, ‘I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father’s house;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I have five brothers, that he may testify to them, so they won’t also come into this place of torment.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say when he comes in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will not rather tell him, ‘Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me, while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink’?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was on his way to Jerusalem, he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Weren’t the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this foreigner?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: neither will they say, ‘Look, here!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ for behold, God’s Kingdom is within you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will tell you, ‘Look, here!’ or ‘Look, there!’ Don’t go away or follow after them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of one part under the sky, shines to another part under the sky; so will the Son of Man be in his day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it was in the days of Noah, even so will it be also in the days of the Son of Man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They ate, they drank, they married, and they were given in marriage until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came and destroyed them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky and destroyed them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that day, he who will be on the housetop and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember Lot’s wife!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. One will be taken and the other will be left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken and the other will be left.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, answering, asked him, “Where, Lord?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Where the body is, there the vultures will also be gathered together.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God, and didn’t respect man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He wouldn’t for a while, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Won’t God avenge his chosen ones who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like the rest of men, extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one: God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. Then you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said, “Look, we have left everything and followed you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took the twelve aside, and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “What do you want me to do?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”

sources.label: New Testament
