sources.text: Immediately he received his sight and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered and was passing through Jericho.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn’t because of the crowd, because he was short.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He ran on ahead, and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was going to pass that way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they saw it, they all murmured, saying, “He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that God’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said therefore, “A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called ten servants of his and gave them ten mina coins,  and told them, ‘Conduct business until I come.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to reign over us.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When he had come back again, having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first came before him, saying, ‘Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to him, ‘Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “So he said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another came, saying, ‘Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn’t lay down, and reap that which you didn’t sow.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to him, ‘Out of your own mouth I will judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn’t lay down, and reaping that which I didn’t sow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to those who stood by, ‘Take the mina away from him and give it to him who has the ten minas.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “They said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten minas!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But bring those enemies of mine who didn’t want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came near to Bethsphage and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, which no man had ever sat upon. Untie it and bring it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ say to him: ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who were sent went away, and found things just as he had told them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the colt?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “The Lord needs it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt, and sat Jesus on them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord!  Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came near, he saw the city and wept over it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the days will come on you, when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn’t know the time of your visitation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered into the temple, and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house is a house of prayer,’  but you have made it a ‘den of robbers’!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests, the scribes, and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He began to tell the people this parable. “A  man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard that, they said, “May that never be!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people—for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They watched him and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered, “Caesar’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer and were silent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Afterward the woman also died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: David himself says in the book of Psalms,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He looked up and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It will turn out as a testimony for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And not a hair of your head will perish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “By your endurance you will win your lives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He told them a parable. “See the fig tree and all the trees.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the Passover, was approaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests and the scribes sought how they might put him to death, for they feared the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Satan entered into Judas, who was also called Iscariot, who was counted with the twelve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went away, and talked with the chief priests and captains about how he might deliver him to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were glad, and agreed to give him money.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He consented, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the multitude.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The day of unleavened bread came, on which the Passover must be sacrificed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, “Go and prepare the Passover for us, that we may eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Behold, when you have entered into the city, a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him into the house which he enters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says to you, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?” ’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will show you a large, furnished upper room. Make preparations there.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went, found things as Jesus had told them, and they prepared the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the hour had come, he sat down with the twelve apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I tell you, I will no longer by any means eat of it until it is fulfilled in God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He received a cup, and when he had given thanks, he said, “Take this, and share it among yourselves,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I tell you, I will not drink at all again from the fruit of the vine, until God’s Kingdom comes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took bread, and when he had given thanks, he broke, and gave it to them, saying, “This is my body which is given for you. Do this in memory of me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise, he took the cup after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But behold, the hand of him who betrays me is with me on the table.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Son of Man indeed goes, as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whom he is betrayed!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They began to question among themselves, which of them it was who would do this thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A dispute also arose among them, which of them was considered to be greatest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “The kings of the nations lord it over them, and those who have authority over them are called ‘benefactors.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But not so with you. But one who is the greater among you, let him become as the younger, and one who is governing, as one who serves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For who is greater, one who sits at the table, or one who serves? Isn’t it he who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you are those who have continued with me in my trials.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I confer on you a kingdom, even as my Father conferred on me,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may eat and drink at my table in my Kingdom. You will sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said, “Simon, Simon, behold, Satan asked to have all of you, that he might sift you as wheat,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I prayed for you, that your faith wouldn’t fail. You, when once you have turned again, establish your brothers.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Lord, I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I tell you, Peter, the rooster will by no means crow today until you deny that you know me three times.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “When I sent you out without purse, wallet, and sandals, did you lack anything?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Nothing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to them, “But now, whoever has a purse, let him take it, and likewise a wallet. Whoever has none, let him sell his cloak, and buy a sword.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I tell you that this which is written must still be fulfilled in me: ‘He was counted with transgressors.’ For that which concerns me has an end.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Lord, behold, here are two swords.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “That is enough.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came out and went, as his custom was, to the Mount of Olives. His disciples also followed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was at the place, he said to them, “Pray that you don’t enter into temptation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was withdrawn from them about a stone’s throw, and he knelt down and prayed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: An angel from heaven appeared to him, strengthening him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being in agony he prayed more earnestly. His sweat became like great drops of blood falling down on the ground.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he rose up from his prayer, he came to the disciples, and found them sleeping because of grief,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to them, “Why do you sleep? Rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who was called Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He came near to Jesus to kiss him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said to him, “Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When those who were around him saw what was about to happen, they said to him, “Lord, shall we strike with the sword?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus answered, “Let me at least do this”—and he touched his ear, and healed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to the chief priests, captains of the temple, and elders, who had come against him, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I was with you in the temple daily, you didn’t stretch out your hands against me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They seized him, and led him away, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter followed from a distance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard, and had sat down together, Peter sat among them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain servant girl saw him as he sat in the light, and looking intently at him, said, “This man also was with him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He denied Jesus, saying, “Woman, I don’t know him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After a little while someone else saw him, and said, “You also are one of them!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter answered, “Man, I am not!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After about one hour passed, another confidently affirmed, saying, “Truly this man also was with him, for he is a Galilean!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter said, “Man, I don’t know what you are talking about!” Immediately, while he was still speaking, a rooster crowed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. Then Peter remembered the Lord’s word, how he said to him, “Before the rooster crows you will deny me three times.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went out, and wept bitterly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The men who held Jesus mocked him and beat him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having blindfolded him, they struck him on the face and asked him, “Prophesy! Who is the one who struck you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They spoke many other things against him, insulting him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As soon as it was day, the assembly of the elders of the people were gathered together, both chief priests and scribes, and they led him away into their council, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If you are the Christ, tell us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “If I tell you, you won’t believe,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and if I ask, you will in no way answer me or let me go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all said, “Are you then the Son of God?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You say it, because I am.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Why do we need any more witness? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered him, “So you say.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and behold, having examined him before you, I found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!”—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were also others, two criminals, led with him to be put to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came to the place that is called “The Skull”, they crucified him there with the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The people stood watching. The rulers with them also scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others. Let him save himself, if this is the Christ of God, his chosen one!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The soldiers also mocked him, coming to him and offering him vinegar,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: An inscription was also written over him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: “THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the criminals who was hanged insulted him, saying, “If you are the Christ, save yourself and us!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, “Don’t you even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the multitudes that came together to see this, when they saw the things that were done, returned home beating their breasts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All his acquaintances and the women who followed with him from Galilee stood at a distance, watching these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, a man named Joseph, who was a member of the council, a good and righteous man

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: this man went to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb that was cut in stone, where no one had ever been laid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was the day of the Preparation, and the Sabbath was drawing near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The women, who had come with him out of Galilee, followed after, and saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But on the first day of the week, at early dawn, they and some others came to the tomb, bringing the spices which they had prepared.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They entered in, and didn’t find the Lord Jesus’ body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they were greatly perplexed about this, behold, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Becoming terrified, they bowed their faces down to the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to them, “Why do you seek the living among the dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men and be crucified, and the third day rise again?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They remembered his words,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, and Mary the mother of James. The other women with them told these things to the apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These words seemed to them to be nonsense, and they didn’t believe them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Stooping and looking in, he saw the strips of linen lying by themselves, and he departed to his home, wondering what had happened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, two of them were going that very day to a village named Emmaus, which was sixty stadia from Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They talked with each other about all of these things which had happened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they talked and questioned together, Jesus himself came near, and went with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But their eyes were kept from recognizing him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “What are you talking about as you walk, and are sad?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, “Are you the only stranger in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things which have happened there in these days?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “What things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “The things concerning Jesus, the Nazarene, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him up to be condemned to death, and crucified him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we were hoping that it was he who would redeem Israel. Yes, and besides all this, it is now the third day since these things happened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Also, certain women of our company amazed us, having arrived early at the tomb;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and when they didn’t find his body, they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels, who said that he was alive.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of us went to the tomb, and found it just like the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that the prophets have spoken!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Didn’t the Christ have to suffer these things and to enter into his glory?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he explained to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came near to the village where they were going, and he acted like he would go further.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They urged him, saying, “Stay with us, for it is almost evening, and the day is almost over.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went in to stay with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had sat down at the table with them, he took the bread and gave thanks. Breaking it, he gave it to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Their eyes were opened and they recognized him, then he vanished out of their sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to one another, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us, while he spoke to us along the way, and while he opened the Scriptures to us?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They rose up that very hour, returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “The Lord is risen indeed, and has appeared to Simon!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They related the things that happened along the way, and how he was recognized by them in the breaking of the bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they said these things, Jesus himself stood among them, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they were terrified and filled with fear, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Why are you troubled? Why do doubts arise in your hearts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See my hands and my feet, that it is truly me. Touch me and see, for a spirit doesn’t have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While they still didn’t believe for joy, and wondered, he said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They gave him a piece of a broiled fish and some honeycomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took them, and ate in front of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “This is what I told you, while I was still with you, that all things which are written in the law of Moses, the prophets, and the psalms, concerning me must be fulfilled.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he opened their minds, that they might understand the Scriptures.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are witnesses of these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I send out the promise of my Father on you. But wait in the city of Jerusalem until you are clothed with power from on high.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He led them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While he blessed them, he withdrew from them, and was carried up into heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They worshiped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The same was in the beginning with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things were made through him. Without him, nothing was made that has been made.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome  it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, has declared him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is John’s testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He declared, and didn’t deny, but he declared, “I am not the Christ.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I am not.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Are you the prophet?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “No.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John answered them, “I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don’t know.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to loosen.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I didn’t recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water said to me, ‘On whomever you will see the Spirit descending and remaining on him is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus turned and saw them following, and said to them, “What are you looking for?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Come, and see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the two who heard John and followed him was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ).

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said, “You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is by interpretation, Peter).

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip said to him, “Come and see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Because I told you, ‘I saw you underneath the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Most certainly, I tell you all, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The third day, there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the wedding.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Fill the water pots with water.” So they filled them up to the brim.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money and overthrew their tables.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To those who sold the doves, he said, “Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he spoke of the temple of his body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus didn’t entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The same came to him by night, and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew,  he can’t see God’s Kingdom.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Most certainly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can’t enter into God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born anew.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The wind blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don’t know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel, and don’t understand these things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don’t receive our witness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I told you earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one has ascended into heaven but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn’t come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them and baptized.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for John was not yet thrown into prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore a dispute arose on the part of John’s disciples with some Jews about purification.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to John and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, he baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John answered, “A man can receive nothing unless it has been given him from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You yourselves testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before him.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He must increase, but I must decrease.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys  the Son won’t see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (although Jesus himself didn’t baptize, but his disciples),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he left Judea and departed into Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He needed to pass through Samaria.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he came to a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son, Joseph.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being tired from his journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, “How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. So where do you get that living water?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are you greater than our father, Jacob, who gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did his children and his livestock?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I don’t get thirsty, neither come all the way here to draw.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman answered, “I have no husband.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “You said well, ‘I have no husband,’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for you have had five husbands; and he whom you now have is not your husband. This you have said truly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour comes, when neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, will you worship the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You worship that which you don’t know. We worship that which we know; for salvation is from the Jews.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the hour comes, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such to be his worshipers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman said to him, “I know that Messiah comes, he who is called Christ. When he has come, he will declare to us all things.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “I am he, the one who speaks to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At this, his disciples came. They marveled that he was speaking with a woman; yet no one said, “What are you looking for?” or, “Why do you speak with her?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the woman left her water pot, went away into the city, and said to the people,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Come, see a man who told me everything that I did. Can this be the Christ?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out of the city, and were coming to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the meanwhile, the disciples urged him, saying, “Rabbi, eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you don’t know about.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples therefore said to one another, “Has anyone brought him something to eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to accomplish his work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you say, ‘There are yet four months until the harvest?’ Behold, I tell you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, that they are white for harvest already.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who reaps receives wages and gathers fruit to eternal life; that both he who sows and he who reaps may rejoice together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in this the saying is true, ‘One sows, and another reaps.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I sent you to reap that for which you haven’t labored. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From that city many of the Samaritans believed in him because of the word of the woman, who testified, “He told me everything that I did.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when the Samaritans came to him, they begged him to stay with them. He stayed there two days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many more believed because of his word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to the woman, “Now we believe, not because of your speaking; for we have heard for ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Savior of the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the two days he went out from there and went into Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Jesus himself testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all the things that he did in Jerusalem at the feast, for they also went to the feast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water into wine. There was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at Capernaum.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he heard that Jesus had come out of Judea into Galilee, he went to him, and begged him that he would come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders, you will in no way believe.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The nobleman said to him, “Sir, come down before my child dies.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your son lives.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him, and he went his way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he was now going down, his servants met him and reported, saying “Your child lives!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he inquired of them the hour when he began to get better. They said therefore to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour, the fever left him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the father knew that it was at that hour in which Jesus said to him, “Your son lives.” He believed, as did his whole house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is again the second sign that Jesus did, having come out of Judea into Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in Jerusalem by the sheep gate, there is a pool, which is called in Hebrew, “Bethesda”, having five porches.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In these lay a great multitude of those who were sick, blind, lame, or paralyzed, waiting for the moving of the water;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for an angel went down at certain times into the pool and stirred up the water. Whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was healed of whatever disease he had.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain man was there who had been sick for thirty-eight years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus saw him lying there, and knew that he had been sick for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be made well?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sick man answered him, “Sir, I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I’m coming, another steps down before me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Arise, take up your mat, and walk.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately, the man was made well, and took up his mat and walked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now it was the Sabbath on that day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the Jews said to him who was cured, “It is the Sabbath. It is not lawful for you to carry the mat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “He who made me well said to me, ‘Take up your mat and walk.’”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they asked him, “Who is the man who said to you, ‘Take up your mat and walk’?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who was healed didn’t know who it was, for Jesus had withdrawn, a crowd being in the place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Afterward Jesus found him in the temple, and said to him, “Behold, you are made well. Sin no more, so that nothing worse happens to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The man went away, and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus, and sought to kill him, because he did these things on the Sabbath.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus answered them, “My Father is still working, so I am working, too.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause therefore the Jews sought all the more to kill him, because he not only broke the Sabbath, but also called God his own Father, making himself equal with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore answered them, “Most certainly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing of himself, but what he sees the Father doing. For whatever things he does, these the Son also does likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Father has affection for the Son, and shows him all things that he himself does. He will show him greater works than these, that you may marvel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son also gives life to whom he desires.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Father judges no one, but he has given all judgment to the Son,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that all may honor the Son, even as they honor the Father. He who doesn’t honor the Son doesn’t honor the Father who sent him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Most certainly I tell you, he who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life, and doesn’t come into judgment, but has passed out of death into life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, the hour comes, and now is, when the dead will hear the Son of God’s voice; and those who hear will live.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the Father has life in himself, even so he gave to the Son also to have life in himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He also gave him authority to execute judgment, because he is a son of man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t marvel at this, for the hour comes in which all who are in the tombs will hear his voice,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will come out; those who have done good, to the resurrection of life; and those who have done evil, to the resurrection of judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I can of myself do nothing. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is righteous; because I don’t seek my own will, but the will of my Father who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If I testify about myself, my witness is not valid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is another who testifies about me. I know that the testimony which he testifies about me is true.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the testimony which I receive is not from man. However, I say these things that you may be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was the burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the testimony which I have is greater than that of John, for the works which the Father gave me to accomplish, the very works that I do, testify about me, that the Father has sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Father himself, who sent me, has testified about me. You have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his form.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You don’t have his word living in you, because you don’t believe him whom he sent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You search the Scriptures, because you think that in them you have eternal life; and these are they which testify about me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet you will not come to me, that you may have life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I don’t receive glory from men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I know you, that you don’t have God’s love in yourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have come in my Father’s name, and you don’t receive me. If another comes in his own name, you will receive him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How can you believe, who receive glory from one another, and you don’t seek the glory that comes from the only God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t think that I will accuse you to the Father. There is one who accuses you, even Moses, on whom you have set your hope.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me; for he wrote about me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you don’t believe his writings, how will you believe my words?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may receive a little.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they were filled, he said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, “This is truly the prophet who comes into the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
