sources.text: Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia,  they saw Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “It is I. Don’t be afraid.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn’t entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “What then do you do for a sign, that we may see and believe you? What work do you do?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, it wasn’t Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will not be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, ‘I have come down out of heaven?’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jesus answered them, “Don’t murmur among yourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is written in the prophets, ‘They will all be taught by God.’   Therefore everyone who hears from the Father and has learned, comes to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the bread of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness and they died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don’t have life in yourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the bread which came down out of heaven—not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said therefore to the twelve, “You don’t also want to go away, do you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, Jesus was walking in Galilee, for he wouldn’t walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the feast of the Jews, the Feast of Booths, was at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His brothers therefore said to him, “Depart from here and go into Judea, that your disciples also may see your works which you do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For no one does anything in secret while he seeks to be known openly. If you do these things, reveal yourself to the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even his brothers didn’t believe in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The world can’t hate you, but it hates me, because I testify about it, that its works are evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You go up to the feast. I am not yet going up to this feast, because my time is not yet fulfilled.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having said these things to them, he stayed in Galilee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when his brothers had gone up to the feast, then he also went up, not publicly, but as it were in secret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore sought him at the feast, and said, “Where is he?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was much murmuring among the multitudes concerning him. Some said, “He is a good man.” Others said, “Not so, but he leads the multitude astray.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet no one spoke openly of him for fear of the Jews.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when it was now the middle of the feast, Jesus went up into the temple and taught.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore marveled, saying, “How does this man know letters, having never been educated?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore answered them, “My teaching is not mine, but his who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone desires to do his will, he will know about the teaching, whether it is from God, or if I am speaking from myself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who speaks from himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Didn’t Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keeps the law? Why do you seek to kill me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude answered, “You have a demon! Who seeks to kill you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “I did one work and you all marvel because of it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moses has given you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers), and on the Sabbath you circumcise a boy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a boy receives circumcision on the Sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be broken, are you angry with me, because I made a man completely healthy on the Sabbath?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t judge according to appearance, but judge righteous judgment.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore some of them of Jerusalem said, “Isn’t this he whom they seek to kill?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Can it be that the rulers indeed know that this is truly the Christ?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However we know where this man comes from, but when the Christ comes, no one will know where he comes from.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, “You both know me, and know where I am from. I have not come of myself, but he who sent me is true, whom you don’t know.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know him, because I am from him, and he sent me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sought therefore to take him; but no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But of the multitude, many believed in him. They said, “When the Christ comes, he won’t do more signs than those which this man has done, will he?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees heard the multitude murmuring these things concerning him, and the chief priests and the Pharisees sent officers to arrest him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said, “I will be with you a little while longer, then I go to him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will seek me, and won’t find me. You can’t come where I am.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore said among themselves, “Where will this man go that we won’t find him? Will he go to the Dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What is this word that he said, ‘You will seek me, and won’t find me;’ and ‘Where I am, you can’t come’?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, from within him will flow rivers of living water.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said this about the Spirit, which those believing in him were to receive. For the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus wasn’t yet glorified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many of the multitude therefore, when they heard these words, said, “This is truly the prophet.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others said, “This is the Christ.” But some said, “What, does the Christ come out of Galilee?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Hasn’t the Scripture said that the Christ comes of the offspring of David,  and from Bethlehem, the village where David was?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So a division arose in the multitude because of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of them would have arrested him, but no one laid hands on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, “Why didn’t you bring him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The officers answered, “No man ever spoke like this man!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees therefore answered them, “You aren’t also led astray, are you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Have any of the rulers believed in him, or of the Pharisees?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this multitude that doesn’t know the law is cursed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nicodemus (he who came to him by night, being one of them) said to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Does our law judge a man, unless it first hears from him personally and knows what he does?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “Are you also from Galilee? Search, and see that no prophet has arisen out of Galilee.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone went to his own house,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now very early in the morning, he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him. He sat down and taught them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The scribes and the Pharisees brought a woman taken in adultery. Having set her in the middle,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: they told him, “Teacher, we found this woman in adultery, in the very act.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in our law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. What then do you say about her?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said this testing him, that they might have something to accuse him of.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when they continued asking him, he looked up and said to them, “He who is without sin among you, let him throw the first stone at her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground with his finger.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, when they heard it, being convicted by their conscience, went out one by one, beginning from the oldest, even to the last. Jesus was left alone with the woman where she was, in the middle.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, standing up, saw her and said, “Woman, where are your accusers? Did no one condemn you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She said, “No one, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Neither do I condemn you. Go your way. From now on, sin no more.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees therefore said to him, “You testify about yourself. Your testimony is not valid.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, for I know where I came from, and where I am going; but you don’t know where I came from, or where I am going.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You judge according to the flesh. I judge no one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even if I do judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It’s also written in your law that the testimony of two people is valid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent me testifies about me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “Where is your Father?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “You know neither me nor my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus spoke these words in the treasury, as he taught in the temple. Yet no one arrested him, because his hour had not yet come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said therefore again to them, “I am going away, and you will seek me, and you will die in your sins. Where I go, you can’t come.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore said, “Will he kill himself, because he says, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come’?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You are from beneath. I am from above. You are of this world. I am not of this world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I said therefore to you that you will die in your sins; for unless you believe that I am he, you will die in your sins.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore to him, “Who are you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Just what I have been saying to you from the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you. However he who sent me is true; and the things which I heard from him, these I say to the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t understand that he spoke to them about the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am he, and I do nothing of myself, but as my Father taught me, I say these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who sent me is with me. The Father hasn’t left me alone, for I always do the things that are pleasing to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he spoke these things, many believed in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will know the truth, and the truth will make you free.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “We are Abraham’s offspring, and have never been in bondage to anyone. How do you say, ‘You will be made free’?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, everyone who commits sin is the bondservant of sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A bondservant doesn’t live in the house forever. A son remains forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know that you are Abraham’s offspring, yet you seek to kill me, because my word finds no place in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I say the things which I have seen with my Father; and you also do the things which you have seen with your father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “Our father is Abraham.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “If you were Abraham’s children, you would do the works of Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth which I heard from God. Abraham didn’t do this.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You do the works of your father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “We were not born of sexual immorality. We have one Father, God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jesus said to them, “If God were your father, you would love me, for I came out and have come from God. For I haven’t come of myself, but he sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why don’t you understand my speech? Because you can’t hear my word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are of your father, the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and doesn’t stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks a lie, he speaks on his own; for he is a liar, and the father of lies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But because I tell the truth, you don’t believe me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which of you convicts me of sin? If I tell the truth, why do you not believe me?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who is of God hears the words of God. For this cause you don’t hear, because you are not of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the Jews answered him, “Don’t we say well that you are a Samaritan, and have a demon?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “I don’t have a demon, but I honor my Father and you dishonor me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I don’t seek my own glory. There is one who seeks and judges.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly, I tell you, if a person keeps my word, he will never see death.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the Jews said to him, “Now we know that you have a demon. Abraham died, as did the prophets; and you say, ‘If a man keeps my word, he will never taste of death.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are you greater than our father, Abraham, who died? The prophets died. Who do you make yourself out to be?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing. It is my Father who glorifies me, of whom you say that he is our God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have not known him, but I know him. If I said, ‘I don’t know him,’ I would be like you, a liar. But I know him and keep his word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Your father Abraham rejoiced to see my day. He saw it, and was glad.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore said to him, “You are not yet fifty years old! Have you seen Abraham?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, before Abraham came into existence, I AM.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore they took up stones to throw at him, but Jesus was hidden, and went out of the temple, having gone through the middle of them, and so passed by.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he passed by, he saw a man blind from birth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples asked him, “Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “This man didn’t sin, nor did his parents; but, that the works of God might be revealed in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I must work the works of him who sent me while it is day. The night is coming, when no one can work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he spat on the ground, made mud with the saliva, anointed the blind man’s eyes with the mud,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to him, “Go, wash in the pool of Siloam” (which means “Sent”). So he went away, washed, and came back seeing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The neighbors therefore, and those who saw that he was blind before, said, “Isn’t this he who sat and begged?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others were saying, “It is he.” Still others were saying, “He looks like him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I am he.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They therefore were asking him, “How were your eyes opened?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “A man called Jesus made mud, anointed my eyes, and said to me, ‘Go to the pool of Siloam and wash.’ So I went away and washed, and I received sight.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they asked him, “Where is he?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I don’t know.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought him who had been blind to the Pharisees.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was a Sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again therefore the Pharisees also asked him how he received his sight. He said to them, “He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and I see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some therefore of the Pharisees said, “This man is not from God, because he doesn’t keep the Sabbath.” Others said, “How can a man who is a sinner do such signs?” There was division among them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore they asked the blind man again, “What do you say about him, because he opened your eyes?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “He is a prophet.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore didn’t believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and had received his sight, until they called the parents of him who had received his sight,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and asked them, “Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then does he now see?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His parents answered them, “We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but how he now sees, we don’t know; or who opened his eyes, we don’t know. He is of age. Ask him. He will speak for himself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews; for the Jews had already agreed that if any man would confess him as Christ, he would be put out of the synagogue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore his parents said, “He is of age. Ask him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they called the man who was blind a second time, and said to him, “Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He therefore answered, “I don’t know if he is a sinner. One thing I do know: that though I was blind, now I see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him again, “What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered them, “I told you already, and you didn’t listen. Why do you want to hear it again? You don’t also want to become his disciples, do you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They insulted him and said, “You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that God has spoken to Moses. But as for this man, we don’t know where he comes from.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The man answered them, “How amazing! You don’t know where he comes from, yet he opened my eyes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that God doesn’t listen to sinners, but if anyone is a worshiper of God, and does his will, he listens to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Since the world began it has never been heard of that anyone opened the eyes of someone born blind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If this man were not from God, he could do nothing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “You were altogether born in sins, and do you teach us?” Then they threw him out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus heard that they had thrown him out, and finding him, he said, “Do you believe in the Son of God?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He answered, “Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “You have both seen him, and it is he who speaks with you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Lord, I believe!” and he worshiped him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “I came into this world for judgment, that those who don’t see may see; and that those who see may become blind.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and said to him, “Are we also blind?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, ‘We see.’ Therefore your sin remains.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Most certainly, I tell you, one who doesn’t enter by the door into the sheep fold, but climbs up some other way, is a thief and a robber.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But one who enters in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The gatekeeper opens the gate for him, and the sheep listen to his voice. He calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whenever he brings out his own sheep, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, for they know his voice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will by no means follow a stranger, but will flee from him; for they don’t know the voice of strangers.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus spoke this parable to them, but they didn’t understand what he was telling them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Most certainly, I tell you, I am the sheep’s door.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who came before me are thieves and robbers, but the sheep didn’t listen to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The thief only comes to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and may have it abundantly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who is a hired hand, and not a shepherd, who doesn’t own the sheep, sees the wolf coming, leaves the sheep, and flees. The wolf snatches the sheep, and scatters them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The hired hand flees because he is a hired hand, and doesn’t care for the sheep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the good shepherd. I know my own, and I’m known by my own;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as the Father knows me, and I know the Father. I lay down my life for the sheep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have other sheep, which are not of this fold. I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice. They will become one flock with one shepherd.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Father loves me, because I lay down my life,  that I may take it again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one takes it away from me, but I lay it down by myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. I received this commandment from my Father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore a division arose again among the Jews because of these words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many of them said, “He has a demon, and is insane! Why do you listen to him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others said, “These are not the sayings of one possessed by a demon. It isn’t possible for a demon to open the eyes of the blind, is it?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was the Feast of the Dedication at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was winter, and Jesus was walking in the temple, in Solomon’s porch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore came around him and said to him, “How long will you hold us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “I told you, and you don’t believe. The works that I do in my Father’s name, these testify about me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you don’t believe, because you are not of my sheep, as I told you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I give eternal life to them. They will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My Father who has given them to me is greater than all. No one is able to snatch them out of my Father’s hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I and the Father are one.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jews took up stones again to stone him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “I have shown you many good works from my Father. For which of those works do you stone me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews answered him, “We don’t stone you for a good work, but for blasphemy: because you, being a man, make yourself God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t it written in your law, ‘I said, you are gods?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If he called them gods, to whom the word of God came (and the Scripture can’t be broken),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: do you say of him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, ‘You blaspheme,’ because I said, ‘I am the Son of God?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I don’t do the works of my Father, don’t believe me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if I do them, though you don’t believe me, believe the works, that you may know and believe that the Father is in me, and I in the Father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sought again to seize him, and he went out of their hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went away again beyond the Jordan into the place where John was baptizing at first, and he stayed there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many came to him. They said, “John indeed did no sign, but everything that John said about this man is true.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many believed in him there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now a certain man was sick, Lazarus from Bethany, of the village of Mary and her sister, Martha.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was that Mary who had anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother, Lazarus, was sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, “Lord, behold, he for whom you have great affection is sick.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Jesus heard it, he said, “This sickness is not to death, but for the glory of God, that God’s Son may be glorified by it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore he heard that he was sick, he stayed two days in the place where he was.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then after this he said to the disciples, “Let’s go into Judea again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples asked him, “Rabbi, the Jews were just trying to stone you. Are you going there again?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Aren’t there twelve hours of daylight? If a man walks in the day, he doesn’t stumble, because he sees the light of this world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if a man walks in the night, he stumbles, because the light isn’t in him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said these things, and after that, he said to them, “Our friend, Lazarus, has fallen asleep, but I am going so that I may awake him out of sleep.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples therefore said, “Lord, if he has fallen asleep, he will recover.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Jesus had spoken of his death, but they thought that he spoke of taking rest in sleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So Jesus said to them plainly then, “Lazarus is dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, so that you may believe. Nevertheless, let’s go to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thomas therefore, who is called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, “Let’s go also, that we may die with him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when Jesus came, he found that he had been in the tomb four days already.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many of the Jews had joined the women around Martha and Mary, to console them concerning their brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then when Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went and met him, but Mary stayed in the house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even now I know that whatever you ask of God, God will give you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Your brother will rise again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Martha said to him, “I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe this?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She said to him, “Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Christ, God’s Son, he who comes into the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she had said this, she went away and called Mary, her sister, secretly, saying, “The Teacher is here and is calling you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she heard this, she arose quickly and went to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was in the place where Martha met him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the Jews who were with her in the house and were consoling her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, “She is going to the tomb to weep there.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when Mary came to where Jesus was and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying to him, “Lord, if you would have been here, my brother wouldn’t have died.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews weeping who came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “Where have you laid him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “Lord, come and see.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus wept.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews therefore said, “See how much affection he had for him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of them said, “Couldn’t this man, who opened the eyes of him who was blind, have also kept this man from dying?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore, again groaning in himself, came to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone lay against it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said, “Take away the stone.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Martha, the sister of him who was dead, said to him, “Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Didn’t I tell you that if you believed, you would see God’s glory?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they took away the stone from the place where the dead man was lying. Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, “Father, I thank you that you listened to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know that you always listen to me, but because of the multitude standing around I said this, that they may believe that you sent me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he cried with a loud voice, “Lazarus, come out!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who was dead came out, bound hand and foot with wrappings, and his face was wrapped around with a cloth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Free him, and let him go.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore many of the Jews who came to Mary and saw what Jesus did believed in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some of them went away to the Pharisees and told them the things which Jesus had done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests therefore and the Pharisees gathered a council, and said, “What are we doing? For this man does many signs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we leave him alone like this, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, “You know nothing at all,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor do you consider that it is advantageous for us that one man should die for the people, and that the whole nation not perish.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he didn’t say this of himself, but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and not for the nation only, but that he might also gather together into one the children of God who are scattered abroad.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So from that day forward they took counsel that they might put him to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews, but departed from there into the country near the wilderness, to a city called Ephraim. He stayed there with his disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the Passover of the Jews was at hand. Many went up from the country to Jerusalem before the Passover, to purify themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they sought for Jesus and spoke with one another as they stood in the temple, “What do you think—that he isn’t coming to the feast at all?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had commanded that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it, that they might seize him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then six days before the Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus was, who had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they made him a supper there. Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those who sat at the table with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Mary took a pound of ointment of pure nard, very precious, and anointed Jesus’s feet and wiped his feet with her hair. The house was filled with the fragrance of the ointment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, one of his disciples, who would betray him, said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Why wasn’t this ointment sold for three hundred denarii, and given to the poor?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he said this, not because he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief, and having the money box, used to steal what was put into it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. She has kept this for the day of my burial.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you always have the poor with you, but you don’t always have me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A large crowd therefore of the Jews learned that he was there, and they came, not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the chief priests conspired to put Lazarus to death also,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because on account of him many of the Jews went away and believed in Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day a great multitude had come to the feast. When they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: they took the branches of the palm trees and went out to meet him, and cried out, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, having found a young donkey, sat on it. As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t be afraid, daughter of Zion. Behold, your King comes, sitting on a donkey’s colt.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples didn’t understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written about him, and that they had done these things to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of the tomb and raised him from the dead was testifying about it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause also the multitude went and met him, because they heard that he had done this sign.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, “See how you accomplish nothing. Behold, the world has gone after him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there were certain Greeks among those that went up to worship at the feast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These, therefore, came to Philip, who was from Bethsaida of Galilee, and asked him, saying, “Sir, we want to see Jesus.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip came and told Andrew, and in turn, Andrew came with Philip, and they told Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “The time has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains by itself alone. But if it dies, it bears much fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who loves his life will lose it. He who hates his life in this world will keep it to eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone serves me, let him follow me. Where I am, there my servant will also be. If anyone serves me, the Father will honor him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now my soul is troubled. What shall I say? ‘Father, save me from this time?’ But I came to this time for this cause.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Father, glorify your name!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then a voice came out of the sky, saying, “I have both glorified it, and will glorify it again.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the multitude who stood by and heard it said that it had thundered. Others said, “An angel has spoken to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “This voice hasn’t come for my sake, but for your sakes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now is the judgment of this world. Now the prince of this world will be cast out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said this, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude answered him, “We have heard out of the law that the Christ remains forever. How do you say, ‘The Son of Man must be lifted up?’ Who is this Son of Man?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Yet a little while the light is with you. Walk while you have the light, that darkness doesn’t overtake you. He who walks in the darkness doesn’t know where he is going.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While you have the light, believe in the light, that you may become children of light.” Jesus said these things, and he departed and hid himself from them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But though he had done so many signs before them, yet they didn’t believe in him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the word of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spoke,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause they couldn’t believe, for Isaiah said again,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isaiah said these things when he saw his glory, and spoke of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless even many of the rulers believed in him, but because of the Pharisees they didn’t confess it, so that they wouldn’t be put out of the synagogue,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they loved men’s praise more than God’s praise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus cried out and said, “Whoever believes in me, believes not in me, but in him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who sees me sees him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have come as a light into the world, that whoever believes in me may not remain in the darkness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone listens to my sayings, and doesn’t believe, I don’t judge him. For I came not to judge the world, but to save the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who rejects me, and doesn’t receive my sayings, has one who judges him. The word that I spoke will judge him in the last day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I spoke not from myself, but the Father who sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know that his commandment is eternal life. The things therefore which I speak, even as the Father has said to me, so I speak.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now before the feast of the Passover, Jesus, knowing that his time had come that he would depart from this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, he loved them to the end.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: During supper, the devil having already put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus, knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he came from God, and was going to God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: arose from supper, and laid aside his outer garments. He took a towel and wrapped a towel around his waist.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he poured water into the basin, and began to wash the disciples’ feet and to wipe them with the towel that was wrapped around him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he came to Simon Peter. He said to him, “Lord, do you wash my feet?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “You don’t know what I am doing now, but you will understand later.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “You will never wash my feet!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “If I don’t wash you, you have no part with me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Someone who has bathed only needs to have his feet washed, but is completely clean. You are clean, but not all of you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he knew him who would betray him, therefore he said, “You are not all clean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when he had washed their feet, put his outer garment back on, and sat down again, he said to them, “Do you know what I have done to you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You call me, ‘Teacher’ and ‘Lord.’ You say so correctly, for so I am.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I have given you an example, that you should also do as I have done to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, a servant is not greater than his lord, neither is one who is sent greater than he who sent him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you know these things, blessed are you if you do them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I don’t speak concerning all of you. I know whom I have chosen. But that the Scripture may be fulfilled, ‘He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From now on, I tell you before it happens, that when it happens, you may believe that I am he.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, he who receives whomever I send, receives me; and he who receives me, receives him who sent me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had said this, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, “Most certainly I tell you that one of you will betray me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples looked at one another, perplexed about whom he spoke.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of his disciples, whom Jesus loved, was at the table, leaning against Jesus’ breast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, and said to him, “Tell us who it is of whom he speaks.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He, leaning back, as he was, on Jesus’ breast, asked him, “Lord, who is it?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore answered, “It is he to whom I will give this piece of bread when I have dipped it.” So when he had dipped the piece of bread, he gave it to Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the piece of bread, then Satan entered into him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus said to him, “What you do, do quickly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now nobody at the table knew why he said this to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For some thought, because Judas had the money box, that Jesus said to him, “Buy what things we need for the feast,” or that he should give something to the poor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore having received that morsel, he went out immediately. It was night.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had gone out, Jesus said, “Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him immediately.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Little children, I will be with you a little while longer. You will seek me, and as I said to the Jews, ‘Where I am going, you can’t come,’ so now I tell you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another. Just as I have loved you, you also love one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter said to him, “Lord, where are you going?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “Where I am going, you can’t follow now, but you will follow afterwards.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “Lord, why can’t I follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Will you lay down your life for me? Most certainly I tell you, the rooster won’t crow until you have denied me three times.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t let your heart be troubled. Believe in God. Believe also in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In my Father’s house are many homes. If it weren’t so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You know where I go, and you know the way.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we don’t know where you are going. How can we know the way?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. From now on, you know him, and have seen him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you such a long time, and do you not know me, Philip? He who has seen me has seen the Father. How do you say, ‘Show us the Father?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? The words that I tell you, I speak not from myself; but the Father who lives in me does his works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me; or else believe me for the very works’ sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, he who believes in me, the works that I do, he will do also; and he will do greater works than these, because I am going to my Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whatever you will ask in my name, I will do it, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you will ask anything in my name, I will do it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you love me, keep my commandments.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will pray to the Father, and he will give you another Counselor,  that he may be with you forever:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the Spirit of truth, whom the world can’t receive; for it doesn’t see him and doesn’t know him. You know him, for he lives with you, and will be in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will not leave you orphans. I will come to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet a little while, and the world will see me no more; but you will see me. Because I live, you will live also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One who has my commandments and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, what has happened that you are about to reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “If a man loves me, he will keep my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him, and make our home with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who doesn’t love me doesn’t keep my words. The word which you hear isn’t mine, but the Father’s who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have said these things to you while still living with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things, and will remind you of all that I said to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, I give to you. Don’t let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You heard how I told you, ‘I go away, and I come to you.’ If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I said ‘I am going to my Father;’ for the Father is greater than I.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I have told you before it happens so that when it happens, you may believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world comes, and he has nothing in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father commanded me, even so I do. Arise, let’s go from here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the farmer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every branch in me that doesn’t bear fruit, he takes away. Every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are already pruned clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remain in me, and I in you. As the branch can’t bear fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me and I in him bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a man doesn’t remain in me, he is thrown out as a branch and is withered; and they gather them, throw them into the fire, and they are burned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you remain in me, and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you desire, and it will be done for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “In this my Father is glorified, that you bear much fruit; and so you will be my disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as the Father has loved me, I also have loved you. Remain in my love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and remain in his love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are my friends, if you do whatever I command you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No longer do I call you servants, for the servant doesn’t know what his lord does. But I have called you friends, for everything that I heard from my Father, I have made known to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You didn’t choose me, but I chose you and appointed you, that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain; that whatever you will ask of the Father in my name, he may give it to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I command these things to you, that you may love one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the world hates you, you know that it has hated me before it hated you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you were of the world, the world would love its own. But because you are not of the world, since I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his lord.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they will do all these things to you for my name’s sake, because they don’t know him who sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have had sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who hates me, hates my Father also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I hadn’t done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn’t have had sin. But now they have seen and also hated both me and my Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this happened so that the word may be fulfilled which was written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When the Counselor has come, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will testify about me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I have said these things to you so that you wouldn’t be caused to stumble.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will put you out of the synagogues. Yes, the time comes that whoever kills you will think that he offers service to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will do these things because they have not known the Father, nor me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have told you these things, so that when the time comes, you may remember that I told you about them. I didn’t tell you these things from the beginning, because I was with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now I am going to him who sent me, and none of you asks me, ‘Where are you going?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But because I have told you these things, sorrow has filled your heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless I tell you the truth: It is to your advantage that I go away, for if I don’t go away, the Counselor won’t come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he has come, he will convict the world about sin, about righteousness, and about judgment;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: about sin, because they don’t believe in me;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: about righteousness, because I am going to my Father, and you won’t see me any more;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: about judgment, because the prince of this world has been judged.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I still have many things to tell you, but you can’t bear them now.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However when he, the Spirit of truth, has come, he will guide you into all truth, for he will not speak from himself; but whatever he hears, he will speak. He will declare to you things that are coming.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will glorify me, for he will take from what is mine, and will declare it to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things that the Father has are mine; therefore I said that he takes of mine and will declare it to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A little while, and you will not see me. Again a little while, and you will see me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of his disciples therefore said to one another, “What is this that he says to us, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and, ‘Because I go to the Father’?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said therefore, “What is this that he says, ‘A little while’? We don’t know what he is saying.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jesus perceived that they wanted to ask him, and he said to them, “Do you inquire among yourselves concerning this, that I said, ‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you that you will weep and lament, but the world will rejoice. You will be sorrowful, but your sorrow will be turned into joy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A woman, when she gives birth, has sorrow because her time has come. But when she has delivered the child, she doesn’t remember the anguish any more, for the joy that a human being is born into the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore you now have sorrow, but I will see you again, and your heart will rejoice, and no one will take your joy away from you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “In that day you will ask me no questions. Most certainly I tell you, whatever you may ask of the Father in my name, he will give it to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Until now, you have asked nothing in my name. Ask, and you will receive, that your joy may be made full.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have spoken these things to you in figures of speech. But the time is coming when I will no more speak to you in figures of speech, but will tell you plainly about the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that day you will ask in my name; and I don’t say to you that I will pray to the Father for you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me, and have believed that I came from God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I came from the Father, and have come into the world. Again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His disciples said to him, “Behold, now you are speaking plainly, and using no figures of speech.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now we know that you know all things, and don’t need for anyone to question you. By this we believe that you came from God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered them, “Do you now believe?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, the time is coming, yes, and has now come, that you will be scattered, everyone to his own place, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have trouble; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said these things, then lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, so he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I glorified you on the earth. I have accomplished the work which you have given me to do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I revealed your name to the people whom you have given me out of the world. They were yours, and you have given them to me. They have kept your word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the words which you have given me I have given to them, and they received them, and knew for sure that I came from you. They have believed that you sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I pray for them. I don’t pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All things that are mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them through your name which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name. I have kept those whom you have given me. None of them is lost except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now I come to you, and I say these things in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have given them your word. The world hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I pray not that you would take them from the world, but that you would keep them from the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They are not of the world even as I am not of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Sanctify them in your truth. Your word is truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not for these only do I pray, but for those also who will believe in me through their word,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that they may all be one; even as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be one in us; that the world may believe that you sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The glory which you have given me, I have given to them; that they may be one, even as we are one;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I in them, and you in me, that they may be perfected into one; that the world may know that you sent me and loved them, even as you loved me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Father, I desire that they also whom you have given me be with me where I am, that they may see my glory, which you have given me, for you loved me before the foundation of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Righteous Father, the world hasn’t known you, but I knew you; and these knew that you sent me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I made known to them your name, and will make it known; that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went out with his disciples over the brook Kidron, where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas then, having taken a detachment of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were happening to him, went out, and said to them, “Who are you looking for?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “Jesus of Nazareth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “I am he.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas also, who betrayed him, was standing with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore he said to them, “I am he,” they went backward, and fell to the ground.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again therefore he asked them, “Who are you looking for?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Jesus of Nazareth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “I told you that I am he. If therefore you seek me, let these go their way,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the word might be fulfilled which he spoke, “Of those whom you have given me, I have lost none.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, struck the high priest’s servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Malchus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to Peter, “Put the sword into its sheath. The cup which the Father has given me, shall I not surely drink it?”
