sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the detachment, the commanding officer, and the officers of the Jews seized Jesus and bound him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and led him to Annas first, for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now it was Caiaphas who advised the Jews that it was expedient that one man should perish for the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter followed Jesus, as did another disciple. Now that disciple was known to the high priest, and entered in with Jesus into the court of the high priest;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Peter was standing at the door outside. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to her who kept the door, and brought in Peter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the maid who kept the door said to Peter, “Are you also one of this man’s disciples?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “I am not.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the servants and the officers were standing there, having made a fire of coals, for it was cold. They were warming themselves. Peter was with them, standing and warming himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest therefore asked Jesus about his disciples and about his teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “I spoke openly to the world. I always taught in synagogues, and in the temple, where the Jews always meet. I said nothing in secret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why do you ask me? Ask those who have heard me what I said to them. Behold, they know the things which I said.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, one of the officers standing by slapped Jesus with his hand, saying, “Do you answer the high priest like that?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “If I have spoken evil, testify of the evil; but if well, why do you beat me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Annas sent him bound to Caiaphas, the high priest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, “You aren’t also one of his disciples, are you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He denied it and said, “I am not.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the servants of the high priest, being a relative of him whose ear Peter had cut off, said, “Didn’t I see you in the garden with him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter therefore denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They led Jesus therefore from Caiaphas into the Praetorium. It was early, and they themselves didn’t enter into the Praetorium, that they might not be defiled, but might eat the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate therefore went out to them, and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “If this man weren’t an evildoer, we wouldn’t have delivered him up to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate therefore said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is illegal for us to put anyone to death,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate therefore entered again into the Praetorium, called Jesus, and said to him, “Are you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered him, “Do you say this by yourself, or did others tell you about me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate answered, “I’m not a Jew, am I? Your own nation and the chief priests delivered you to me. What have you done?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, then my servants would fight, that I wouldn’t be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from here.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate therefore said to him, “Are you a king then?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to him, “What is truth?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no basis for a charge against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you have a custom, that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they all shouted again, saying, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So Pilate then took Jesus, and flogged him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown, and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They kept saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and they kept slapping him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Pilate went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no basis for a charge against him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. Pilate said to them, “Behold, the man!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him, they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves, and crucify him, for I find no basis for a charge against him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore Pilate heard this saying, he was more afraid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered into the Praetorium again, and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate therefore said to him, “Aren’t you speaking to me? Don’t you know that I have power to release you and have power to crucify you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus answered, “You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you release this man, you aren’t Caesar’s friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called “The Pavement”, but in Hebrew, “Gabbatha.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went out, bearing his cross, to the place called “The Place of a Skull”, which is called in Hebrew, “Golgotha”,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: where they crucified him, and with him two others, on either side one, and Jesus in the middle.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, “JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore many of the Jews read this title, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.” ’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also the coat. Now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be,” that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the soldiers did these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But standing by Jesus’ cross were his mother, his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when Jesus saw his mother, and the disciple whom he loved standing there, he said to his mother, “Woman, behold, your son!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to the disciple, “Behold, your mother!” From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After this, Jesus, seeing that all things were now finished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, said, “I am thirsty.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, “It is finished.” He bowed his head, and gave up his spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the Jews, because it was the Preparation Day, so that the bodies wouldn’t remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a special one), asked of Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the soldiers came, and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who was crucified with him;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was already dead, they didn’t break his legs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he tells the truth, that you may believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For these things happened that the Scripture might be fulfilled, “A bone of him will not be broken.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus’ body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nicodemus, who at first came to Jesus by night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred Roman pounds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they took Jesus’ body, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the custom of the Jews is to bury.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden was a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then because of the Jews’ Preparation Day (for the tomb was near at hand) they laid Jesus there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw the stone taken away from the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have laid him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter, and came to the tomb first.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying, yet he didn’t enter in.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as yet they didn’t know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the disciples went away again to their own homes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They asked her, “Woman, why are you weeping?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don’t know where they have laid him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn’t know that it was Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Mary.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She turned and said to him, “Rabboni!” which is to say, “Teacher!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to her, “Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore it was evening on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle, and said to them, “Peace be to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them again, “Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you forgive anyone’s sins, they have been forgiven them. If you retain anyone’s sins, they have been retained.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, wasn’t with them when Jesus came.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After eight days again his disciples were inside and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the middle, and said, “Peace be to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he said to Thomas, “Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter said to them, “I’m going fishing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told him, “We are also coming with you.” They immediately went out, and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach, yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus therefore said to them, “Children, have you anything to eat?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They answered him, “No.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits away), dragging the net full of fish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, with fish and bread laid on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish which you have just caught.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of one hundred fifty-three great fish. Even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to them, “Come and eat breakfast!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples after he had risen from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Feed my lambs.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him again a second time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Tend my sheep.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time, “Do you have affection for me?” He said to him, “Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Most certainly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you and carry you where you don’t want to go.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him, “Follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus’ breast at the supper and asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter seeing him, said to Jesus, “Lord, what about this man?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus said to him, “If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament

sources.text: These words pleased the whole multitude. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch;

sources.text: whom they set before the apostles. When they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.

sources.text: God spoke in this way: that his offspring would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘I will judge the nation to which they will be in bondage,’ said God, ‘and after that they will come out, and serve me in this place.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He gave him the covenant of circumcision. So Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The patriarchs, moved with jealousy against Joseph, sold him into Egypt. God was with him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He made him governor over Egypt and all his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now a famine came over all the land of Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction. Our fathers found no food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers the first time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph’s race was revealed to Pharaoh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Joseph sent and summoned Jacob, his father, and all his relatives, seventy-five souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jacob went down into Egypt and he died, himself and our fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they were brought back to Shechem, and laid in the tomb that Abraham bought for a price in silver from the children of Hamor of Shechem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “But as the time of the promise came close which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: until there arose a different king, who didn’t know Joseph.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The same took advantage of our race, and mistreated our fathers, and forced them to throw out their babies, so that they wouldn’t stay alive.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At that time Moses was born, and was exceedingly handsome. He was nourished three months in his father’s house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was thrown out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up and reared him as her own son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians. He was mighty in his words and works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him who was oppressed, striking the Egyptian.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He supposed that his brothers understood that God, by his hand, was giving them deliverance; but they didn’t understand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The day following, he appeared to them as they fought, and urged them to be at peace again, saying, ‘Sirs, you are brothers. Why do you wrong one another?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who did his neighbor wrong pushed him away, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do you want to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moses fled at this saying, and became a stranger in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When forty years were fulfilled, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight. As he came close to see, a voice of the Lord came to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ Moses trembled, and dared not look.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you stand is holy ground.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have surely seen the affliction of my people that is in Egypt, and have heard their groaning. I have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “This Moses, whom they refused, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’—God has sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is that Moses, who said to the children of Israel, ‘The Lord our God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel that spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, who received living revelations to give to us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom our fathers wouldn’t be obedient, but rejected him, and turned back in their hearts to Egypt,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They made a calf in those days, and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God turned, and gave them up to serve the army of the sky, as it is written in the book of the prophets,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Our fathers had the tabernacle of the testimony in the wilderness, even as he who spoke to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern that he had seen;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which also our fathers, in their turn, brought in with Joshua when they entered into the possession of the nations, whom God drove out before the face of our fathers, to the days of David,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who found favor in the sight of God, and asked to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Solomon built him a house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, the Most High doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “You stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit! As your fathers did, so you do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Which of the prophets didn’t your fathers persecute? They killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You received the law as it was ordained by angels, and didn’t keep it!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed at him with their teeth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, being full of the Holy Spirit, looked up steadfastly into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears, then rushed at him with one accord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They threw him out of the city and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They stoned Stephen as he called out, saying, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, “Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Saul was consenting to his death. A great persecution arose against the assembly which was in Jerusalem in that day. They were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except for the apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Devout men buried Stephen and lamented greatly over him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Saul ravaged the assembly, entering into every house and dragged both men and women off to prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore those who were scattered abroad went around preaching the word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and proclaimed to them the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitudes listened with one accord to the things that were spoken by Philip when they heard and saw the signs which he did.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For unclean spirits came out of many of those who had them. They came out, crying with a loud voice. Many who had been paralyzed and lame were healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was great joy in that city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there was a certain man, Simon by name, who used to practice sorcery in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, making himself out to be some great one,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom they all listened, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is that great power of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They listened to him, because for a long time he had amazed them with his sorceries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when they believed Philip preaching good news concerning God’s Kingdom and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon himself also believed. Being baptized, he continued with Philip. Seeing signs and great miracles occurring, he was amazed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, when they had come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Spirit;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for as yet he had fallen on none of them. They had only been baptized in the name of Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when Simon saw that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Give me also this power, that whomever I lay my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart isn’t right before God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Repent therefore of this, your wickedness, and ask God if perhaps the thought of your heart may be forgiven you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I see that you are in the poison of bitterness and in the bondage of iniquity.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that none of the things which you have spoken happen to me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They therefore, when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the Good News to many villages of the Samaritans.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise, and go toward the south to the way that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza. This is a desert.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He arose and went; and behold, there was a man of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was over all her treasure, who had come to Jerusalem to worship.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was returning and sitting in his chariot, and was reading the prophet Isaiah.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go near, and join yourself to this chariot.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip ran to him, and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet, and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “How can I, unless someone explains it to me?” He begged Philip to come up and sit with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the passage of the Scripture which he was reading was this,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The eunuch answered Philip, “Who is the prophet talking about? About himself, or about someone else?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Philip opened his mouth, and beginning from this Scripture, preached to him about Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went on the way, they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Behold, here is water. What is keeping me from being baptized?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded the chariot to stand still, and they both went down into the water, both Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught Philip away, and the eunuch didn’t see him any more, for he went on his way rejoicing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Philip was found at Azotus. Passing through, he preached the Good News to all the cities, until he came to Caesarea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Saul, still breathing threats and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and asked for letters from him to the synagogues of Damascus, that if he found any who were of the Way, whether men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he traveled, he got close to Damascus, and suddenly a light from the sky shone around him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He fell on the earth, and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Who are you, Lord?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But rise up and enter into the city, then you will be told what you must do.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The men who traveled with him stood speechless, hearing the sound, but seeing no one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Saul arose from the ground, and when his eyes were opened, he saw no one. They led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was without sight for three days, and neither ate nor drank.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was a certain disciple at Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Behold, it’s me, Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said to him, “Arise, and go to the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judah for one named Saul, a man of Tarsus. For behold, he is praying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in a vision he has seen a man named Ananias coming in and laying his hands on him, that he might receive his sight.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he did to your saints at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Lord said to him, “Go your way, for he is my chosen vessel to bear my name before the nations and kings, and the children of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I will show him how many things he must suffer for my name’s sake.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Ananias departed and entered into the house. Laying his hands on him, he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord, who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he received his sight. He arose and was baptized.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took food and was strengthened. Saul stayed several days with the disciples who were at Damascus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately in the synagogues he proclaimed the Christ, that he is the Son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who heard him were amazed, and said, “Isn’t this he who in Jerusalem made havoc of those who called on this name? And he had come here intending to bring them bound before the chief priests!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Saul increased more in strength, and confounded the Jews who lived at Damascus, proving that this is the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When many days were fulfilled, the Jews conspired together to kill him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but their plot became known to Saul. They watched the gates both day and night that they might kill him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but his disciples took him by night and let him down through the wall, lowering him in a basket.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Saul had come to Jerusalem, he tried to join himself to the disciples; but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles, and declared to them how he had seen the Lord on the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how at Damascus he had preached boldly in the name of Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was with them entering into Jerusalem,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: preaching boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus. He spoke and disputed against the Hellenists, but they were seeking to kill him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the brothers knew it, they brought him down to Caesarea, and sent him off to Tarsus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the assemblies throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace, and were built up. They were multiplied, walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Peter went throughout all those parts, he came down also to the saints who lived at Lydda.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There he found a certain man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years, because he was paralyzed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you. Get up and make your bed!” Immediately he arose.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who lived at Lydda and in Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which when translated, means Dorcas. This woman was full of good works and acts of mercy which she did.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In those days, she became sick, and died. When they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men to him, imploring him not to delay in coming to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter got up and went with them. When he had come, they brought him into the upper room. All the widows stood by him weeping, and showing the coats and garments which Dorcas had made while she was with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter sent them all out, and knelt down and prayed. Turning to the body, he said, “Tabitha, get up!” She opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He gave her his hand, and raised her up. Calling the saints and widows, he presented her alive.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He stayed many days in Joppa with a tanner named Simon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there was a certain man in Caesarea, Cornelius by name, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: a devout man, and one who feared God with all his house, who gave gifts for the needy generously to the people, and always prayed to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At about the ninth hour of the day, he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God coming to him, and saying to him, “Cornelius!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He, fastening his eyes on him, and being frightened, said, “What is it, Lord?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to him, “Your prayers and your gifts to the needy have gone up for a memorial before God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now send men to Joppa, and get Simon, who is also called Peter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is staying with a tanner named Simon, whose house is by the seaside.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of those who waited on him continually.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now on the next day as they were on their journey, and got close to the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray at about noon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He became hungry and desired to eat, but while they were preparing, he fell into a trance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He saw heaven opened and a certain container descending to him, like a great sheet let down by four corners on the earth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in which were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, reptiles, and birds of the sky.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A voice came to him, “Rise, Peter, kill and eat!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A voice came to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This was done three times, and immediately the vessel was received up into heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while Peter was very perplexed in himself what the vision which he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon’s house, stood before the gate,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and called and asked whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was lodging there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three  men seek you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But arise, get down, and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter went down to the men, and said, “Behold, I am he whom you seek. Why have you come?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man and one who fears God, and well spoken of by all the nation of the Jews, was directed by a holy angel to invite you to his house, and to listen to what you say.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he called them in and provided a place to stay. On the next day Peter arose and went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, having called together his relatives and his near friends.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell down at his feet, and worshiped him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter raised him up, saying, “Stand up! I myself am also a man.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he talked with him, he went in and found many gathered together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “You yourselves know how it is an unlawful thing for a man who is a Jew to join himself or come to one of another nation, but God has shown me that I shouldn’t call any man unholy or unclean.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I also came without complaint when I was sent for. I ask therefore, why did you send for me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Cornelius said, “Four days ago, I was fasting until this hour, and at the ninth hour, I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer is heard, and your gifts to the needy are remembered in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Send therefore to Joppa, and summon Simon, who is also called Peter. He is staying in the house of a tanner named Simon, by the seaside. When he comes, he will speak to you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I sent to you at once, and it was good of you to come. Now therefore we are all here present in the sight of God to hear all things that have been commanded you by God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter opened his mouth and said, “Truly I perceive that God doesn’t show favoritism;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in every nation he who fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The word which he sent to the children of Israel, preaching good news of peace by Jesus Christ—he is Lord of all—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: you yourselves know what happened, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even Jesus of Nazareth, how God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are witnesses of everything he did both in the country of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they also killed, hanging him on a tree.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God raised him up the third day, and gave him to be revealed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen before by God, to us, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the prophets testify about him, that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive remission of sins.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They of the circumcision who believed were amazed, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was also poured out on the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they heard them speaking in other languages and magnifying God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Peter answered,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Can anyone forbid these people from being baptized with water? They have received the Holy Spirit just like us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay some days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the apostles and the brothers who were in Judea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Peter had come up to Jerusalem, those who were of the circumcision contended with him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “You went in to uncircumcised men, and ate with them!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter began, and explained to them in order, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision: a certain container descending, like it was a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners. It came as far as me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I had looked intently at it, I considered, and saw the four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, creeping things, and birds of the sky.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Rise, Peter, kill and eat!’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I said, ‘Not so, Lord, for nothing unholy or unclean has ever entered into my mouth.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But a voice answered me the second time out of heaven, ‘What God has cleansed, don’t you call unclean.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This was done three times, and all were drawn up again into heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, immediately three men stood before the house where I was, having been sent from Caesarea to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Spirit told me to go with them, without discriminating. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He told us how he had seen the angel standing in his house, and saying to him, ‘Send to Joppa, and get Simon, who is called Peter,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who will speak to you words by which you will be saved, you and all your house.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them, even as on us at the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, ‘John indeed baptized in water, but you will be baptized in the Holy Spirit.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then God gave to them the same gift as us, when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I, that I could withstand God?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, “Then God has also granted to the Gentiles repentance to life!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They therefore who were scattered abroad by the oppression that arose about Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except to Jews only.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who, when they had come to Antioch, spoke to the Hellenists, preaching the Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number believed and turned to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The report concerning them came to the ears of the assembly which was in Jerusalem. They sent out Barnabas to go as far as Antioch,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, when he had come, and had seen the grace of God, was glad. He exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they should remain near to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and many people were added to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Barnabas went out to Tarsus to look for Saul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. For a whole year they were gathered together with the assembly, and taught many people. The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in these days, prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of them named Agabus stood up, and indicated by the Spirit that there should be a great famine all over the world, which also happened in the days of Claudius.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As any of the disciples had plenty, each determined to send relief to the brothers who lived in Judea;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which they also did, sending it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now about that time, King Herod stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He killed James, the brother of John, with the sword.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This was during the days of unleavened bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had arrested him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter therefore was kept in the prison, but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains. Guards in front of the door kept the prison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side, and woke him up, saying, “Stand up quickly!” His chains fell off his hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” He did so. He said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And he went out and followed him. He didn’t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they were past the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went out, and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Peter had come to himself, he said, “Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from everything the Jewish people were expecting.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thinking about that, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she didn’t open the gate for joy, but ran in, and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to her, “You are crazy!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Peter continued knocking. When they had opened, they saw him, and were amazed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Herod had sought for him, and didn’t find him, he examined the guards, then commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus, the king’s personal aide, their friend, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On an appointed day, Herod dressed himself in royal clothing, sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The people shouted, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he didn’t give God the glory. Then he was eaten by worms and died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the word of God grew and multiplied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their service, also taking with them John who was called Mark.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the assembly that was at Antioch there were some prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen the foster brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they served the Lord and fasted, the Holy Spirit said, “Separate Barnabas and Saul for me, for the work to which I have called them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then, when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia. From there they sailed to Cyprus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they were at Salamis, they proclaimed God’s word in the Jewish synagogues. They also had John as their attendant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had gone through the island to Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of understanding. This man summoned Barnabas and Saul, and sought to hear the word of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Saul, who is also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, fastened his eyes on him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said, “You son of the devil, full of all deceit and all cunning, you enemy of all righteousness, will you not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, behold, the hand of the Lord is on you, and you will be blind, not seeing the sun for a season!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately a mist and darkness fell on him. He went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the proconsul, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the teaching of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now Paul and his company set sail from Paphos, and came to Perga in Pamphylia. John departed from them and returned to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they, passing on from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia. They went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and sat down.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the reading of the law and the prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of exhortation for the people, speak.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, “Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The God of this people chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they stayed as aliens in the land of Egypt, and with an uplifted arm, he led them out of it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land for an inheritance for about four hundred fifty years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Afterward they asked for a king, and God gave to them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, to whom he also testified, ‘I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after my heart, who will do all my will.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From this man’s offspring, God has brought salvation to Israel according to his promise,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: before his coming, when John had first preached the baptism of repentance to Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As John was fulfilling his course, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. But behold, one comes after me, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, children of the stock of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, the word of this salvation is sent out to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who dwell in Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they didn’t know him, nor the voices of the prophets which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by condemning him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Though they found no cause for death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had fulfilled all things that were written about him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God raised him from the dead,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he was seen for many days by those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses to the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We bring you good news of the promise made to the fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that God has fulfilled this to us, their children, in that he raised up Jesus. As it is also written in the second psalm,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he has spoken thus: ‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he says also in another psalm, ‘You will not allow your Holy One to see decay.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For David, after he had in his own generation served the counsel of God, fell asleep, was laid with his fathers, and saw decay.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he whom God raised up saw no decay.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be it known to you therefore, brothers, that through this man is proclaimed to you remission of sins,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and by him everyone who believes is justified from all things, from which you could not be justified by the law of Moses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beware therefore, lest that come on you which is spoken in the prophets:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So when the Jews went out of the synagogue, the Gentiles begged that these words might be preached to them the next Sabbath.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when the synagogue broke up, many of the Jews and of the devout proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas; who, speaking to them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The next Sabbath, almost the whole city was gathered together to hear the word of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with jealousy, and contradicted the things which were spoken by Paul, and blasphemed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, and said, “It was necessary that God’s word should be spoken to you first. Since indeed you thrust it from yourselves, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For so has the Lord commanded us, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of God. As many as were appointed to eternal life believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord’s word was spread abroad throughout all the region.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Jews stirred up the devout and prominent women and the chief men of the city, and stirred up a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their borders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came to Iconium.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In Iconium, they entered together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spoke that a great multitude both of Jews and of Greeks believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the disbelieving Jews stirred up and embittered the souls of the Gentiles against the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore they stayed there a long time, speaking boldly in the Lord, who testified to the word of his grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the multitude of the city was divided. Part sided with the Jews, and part with the apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When some of both the Gentiles and the Jews, with their rulers, made a violent attempt to mistreat and stone them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: they became aware of it and fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra, Derbe, and the surrounding region.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There they preached the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At Lystra a certain man sat, impotent in his feet, a cripple from his mother’s womb, who never had walked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He was listening to Paul speaking, who, fastening eyes on him, and seeing that he had faith to be made whole,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: said with a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet!” He leaped up and walked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the multitude saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voice, saying in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They called Barnabas “Jupiter”, and Paul “Mercury”, because he was the chief speaker.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The priest of Jupiter, whose temple was in front of their city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates, and would have made a sacrifice along with the multitudes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of it, they tore their clothes, and sprang into the multitude, crying out,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men of like passions with you, and bring you good news, that you should turn from these vain things to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and all that is in them;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who in the generations gone by allowed all the nations to walk in their own ways.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet he didn’t leave himself without witness, in that he did good and gave you rains from the sky and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even saying these things, they hardly stopped the multitudes from making a sacrifice to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some Jews from Antioch and Iconium came there, and having persuaded the multitudes, they stoned Paul, and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as the disciples stood around him, he rose up, and entered into the city. On the next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had preached the Good News to that city, and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: strengthening the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that through many afflictions we must enter into God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had appointed elders for them in every assembly, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they had believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They passed through Pisidia, and came to Pamphylia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From there they sailed to Antioch, from where they had been committed to the grace of God for the work which they had fulfilled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had arrived, and had gathered the assembly together, they reported all the things that God had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith to the nations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They stayed there with the disciples for a long time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some men came down from Judea and taught the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised after the custom of Moses, you can’t be saved.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when Paul and Barnabas had no small discord and discussion with them, they appointed Paul and Barnabas, and some others of them, to go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, being sent on their way by the assembly, passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles. They caused great joy to all the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come to Jerusalem, they were received by the assembly and the apostles and the elders, and they reported everything that God had done with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some of the sect of the Pharisees who believed rose up, saying, “It is necessary to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to see about this matter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When there had been much discussion, Peter rose up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that a good while ago God made a choice among you that by my mouth the nations should hear the word of the Good News and believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God, who knows the heart, testified about them, giving them the Holy Spirit, just like he did to us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He made no distinction between us and them, cleansing their hearts by faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now therefore why do you tempt God, that you should put a yoke on the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus,  just as they are.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the multitude kept silence, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul reporting what signs and wonders God had done among the nations through them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After they were silent, James answered, “Brothers, listen to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simeon has reported how God first visited the nations to take out of them a people for his name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This agrees with the words of the prophets. As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “All of God’s works are known to him from eternity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore my judgment is that we don’t trouble those from among the Gentiles who turn to God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but that we write to them that they abstain from the pollution of idols, from sexual immorality, from what is strangled, and from blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Moses from generations of old has in every city those who preach him, being read in the synagogues every Sabbath.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole assembly, to choose men out of their company, and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas: Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, chief men among the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They wrote these things by their hand:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The apostles, the elders, and the brothers, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: greetings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because we have heard that some who went out from us have troubled you with words, unsettling your souls, saying, ‘You must be circumcised and keep the law,’ to whom we gave no commandment;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: it seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose out men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who themselves will also tell you the same things by word of mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it seemed good to the Holy Spirit, and to us, to lay no greater burden on you than these necessary things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you abstain from things sacrificed to idols, from blood, from things strangled, and from sexual immorality, from which if you keep yourselves, it will be well with you. Farewell.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So, when they were sent off, they came to Antioch. Having gathered the multitude together, they delivered the letter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had read it, they rejoiced over the encouragement.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judas and Silas, also being prophets themselves, encouraged the brothers with many words and strengthened them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After they had spent some time there, they were sent back with greetings from the brothers to the apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Let’s return now and visit our brothers in every city in which we proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Barnabas planned to take John, who was called Mark, with them also.

sources.label: New Testament
