sources.text: But Paul didn’t think that it was a good idea to take with them someone who had withdrawn from them in Pamphylia, and didn’t go with them to do the work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the contention grew so sharp that they separated from each other. Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Paul chose Silas and went out, being commended by the brothers to the grace of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to Derbe and Lystra: and behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewess who believed; but his father was a Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The brothers who were at Lystra and Iconium gave a good testimony about him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul wanted to have him go out with him, and he took and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those parts; for they all knew that his father was a Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered the decrees to them to keep which had been ordained by the apostles and elders who were at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the assemblies were strengthened in the faith, and increased in number daily.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had gone through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, they were forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come opposite Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit didn’t allow them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Passing by Mysia, they came down to Troas.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A vision appeared to Paul in the night. There was a man of Macedonia standing, begging him, and saying, “Come over into Macedonia and help us.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go out to Macedonia, concluding that the Lord had called us to preach the Good News to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Setting sail therefore from Troas, we made a straight course to Samothrace, and the day following to Neapolis;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and from there to Philippi, which is a city of Macedonia, the foremost of the district, a Roman colony. We were staying some days in this city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the Sabbath day we went outside of the city by a riverside, where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and we sat down and spoke to the women who had come together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, one who worshiped God, heard us. The Lord opened her heart to listen to the things which were spoken by Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When she and her household were baptized, she begged us, saying, “If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house and stay.” So she persuaded us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As we were going to prayer, a certain girl having a spirit of divination met us, who brought her masters much gain by fortune telling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Following Paul and us, she cried out, “These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to us a way of salvation!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She was doing this for many days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul, becoming greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” It came out that very hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when her masters saw that the hope of their gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas, and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had brought them to the magistrates, they said, “These men, being Jews, are agitating our city

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and advocate customs which it is not lawful for us to accept or to observe, being Romans.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude rose up together against them and the magistrates tore their clothes from them, then commanded them to be beaten with rods.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had laid many stripes on them, they threw them into prison, charging the jailer to keep them safely,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, having received such a command, threw them into the inner prison, and secured their feet in the stocks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But about midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken; and immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone’s bonds were loosened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The jailer, being roused out of sleep and seeing the prison doors open, drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, “Don’t harm yourself, for we are all here!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called for lights, sprang in, fell down trembling before Paul and Silas,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: brought them out, and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved, you and your household.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They spoke the word of the Lord to him, and to all who were in his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He took them the same hour of the night and washed their stripes, and was immediately baptized, he and all his household.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He brought them up into his house, and set food before them, and rejoiced greatly, with all his household, having believed in God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when it was day, the magistrates sent the sergeants, saying, “Let those men go.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, “The magistrates have sent to let you go; now therefore come out and go in peace.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul said to them, “They have beaten us publicly without a trial, men who are Romans, and have cast us into prison! Do they now release us secretly? No, most certainly, but let them come themselves and bring us out!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sergeants reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they came and begged them. When they had brought them out, they asked them to depart from the city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out of the prison, and entered into Lydia’s house. When they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them, then departed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, as was his custom, went in to them, and for three Sabbath days reasoned with them from the Scriptures,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and rise again from the dead, and saying, “This Jesus, whom I proclaim to you, is the Christ.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, of the devout Greeks a great multitude, and not a few of the chief women.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the unpersuaded Jews took along some wicked men from the marketplace, and gathering a crowd, set the city in an uproar. Assaulting the house of Jason, they sought to bring them out to the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they didn’t find them, they dragged Jason and certain brothers  before the rulers of the city, crying, “These who have turned the world upside down have come here also,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom Jason has received. These all act contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, Jesus!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The multitude and the rulers of the city were troubled when they heard these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had taken security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Beroea. When they arrived, they went into the Jewish synagogue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now these were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they received the word with all readiness of mind, examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many of them therefore believed; also of the prominent Greek women, and not a few men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Beroea also, they came there likewise, agitating the multitudes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the brothers immediately sent out Paul to go as far as to the sea, and Silas and Timothy still stayed there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But those who escorted Paul brought him as far as Athens. Receiving a commandment to Silas and Timothy that they should come to him very quickly, they departed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw the city full of idols.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who met him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers also were conversing with him. Some said, “What does this babbler want to say?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others said, “He seems to be advocating foreign deities,” because he preached Jesus and the resurrection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took hold of him and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, “May we know what this new teaching is, which you are speaking about?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you bring certain strange things to our ears. We want to know therefore what these things mean.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now all the Athenians and the strangers living there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell or to hear some new thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul stood in the middle of the Areopagus, and said, “You men of Athens, I perceive that you are very religious in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as I passed along and observed the objects of your worship, I also found an altar with this inscription: ‘TO AN UNKNOWN GOD.’ What therefore you worship in ignorance, I announce to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The God who made the world and all things in it, he, being Lord of heaven and earth, doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He isn’t served by men’s hands, as though he needed anything, seeing he himself gives to all life and breath, and all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He made from one blood every nation of men to dwell on all the surface of the earth, having determined appointed seasons, and the boundaries of their dwellings,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that they should seek the Lord, if perhaps they might reach out for him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: ‘For in him we live, move, and have our being.’ As some of your own poets have said, ‘For we are also his offspring.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being then the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Divine Nature is like gold, or silver, or stone, engraved by art and design of man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The times of ignorance therefore God overlooked. But now he commands that all people everywhere should repent,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because he has appointed a day in which he will judge the world in righteousness by the man whom he has ordained; of which he has given assurance to all men, in that he has raised him from the dead.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked; but others said, “We want to hear you again concerning this.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thus Paul went out from among them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But certain men joined with him and believed, among whom also was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He found a certain Jew named Aquila, a man of Pontus by race, who had recently come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had commanded all the Jews to depart from Rome. He came to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and because he practiced the same trade, he lived with them and worked, for by trade they were tent makers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath and persuaded Jews and Greeks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Silas and Timothy came down from Macedonia, Paul was compelled by the Spirit, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they opposed him and blasphemed, he shook out his clothing and said to them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am clean. From now on, I will go to the Gentiles!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He departed there, and went into the house of a certain man named Justus, one who worshiped God, whose house was next door to the synagogue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his house. Many of the Corinthians, when they heard, believed and were baptized.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord said to Paul in the night by a vision, “Don’t be afraid, but speak and don’t be silent;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you, for I have many people in this city.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He lived there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one accord rose up against Paul and brought him before the judgment seat,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “This man persuades men to worship God contrary to the law.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, “If indeed it were a matter of wrong or of wicked crime, you Jews, it would be reasonable that I should bear with you;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but if they are questions about words and names and your own law, look to it yourselves. For I don’t want to be a judge of these matters.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he drove them from the judgment seat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then all the Greeks seized Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. Gallio didn’t care about any of these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, having stayed after this many more days, took his leave of the brothers,  and sailed from there for Syria, together with Priscilla and Aquila. He shaved his head in Cenchreae, for he had a vow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came to Ephesus, and he left them there; but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they asked him to stay with them a longer time, he declined;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but taking his leave of them, he said, “I must by all means keep this coming feast in Jerusalem, but I will return again to you if God wills.” Then he set sail from Ephesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had landed at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the assembly, and went down to Antioch.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having spent some time there, he departed, and went through the region of Galatia, and Phrygia, in order, establishing all the disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now a certain Jew named Apollos, an Alexandrian by race, an eloquent man, came to Ephesus. He was mighty in the Scriptures.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This man had been instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in spirit, he spoke and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, although he knew only the baptism of John.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He began to speak boldly in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside, and explained to him the way of God more accurately.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had determined to pass over into Achaia, the brothers encouraged him, and wrote to the disciples to receive him. When he had come, he greatly helped those who had believed through grace;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he powerfully refuted the Jews, publicly showing by the Scriptures that Jesus was the Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper country, came to Ephesus and found certain disciples.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “No, we haven’t even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Into what then were you baptized?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said, “Into John’s baptism.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul said, “John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him, that is, in Jesus.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them and they spoke with other languages and prophesied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were about twelve men in all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He entered into the synagogue and spoke boldly for a period of three months, reasoning and persuading about the things concerning God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This continued for two years, so that all those who lived in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God worked special miracles by the hands of Paul,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But some of the itinerant Jews, exorcists, took on themselves to invoke over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, “We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, who did this.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The evil spirit answered, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, overpowered them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who lived at Ephesus. Fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many also of those who had believed came, confessing, and declaring their deeds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many of those who practiced magical arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all. They counted their price, and found it to be fifty thousand pieces of silver.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the word of the Lord was growing and becoming mighty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now after these things had ended, Paul determined in the Spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, “After I have been there, I must also see Rome.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who served him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About that time there arose no small disturbance concerning the Way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom he gathered together, with the workmen of like occupation, and said, “Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You see and hear that not at Ephesus alone, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away many people, saying that they are no gods that are made with hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only is there danger that this our trade come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be counted as nothing and her majesty destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worships.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard this they were filled with anger, and cried out, saying, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The whole city was filled with confusion, and they rushed with one accord into the theater, having seized Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul’s companions in travel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Paul wanted to enter in to the people, the disciples didn’t allow him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Certain also of the Asiarchs, being his friends, sent to him and begged him not to venture into the theater.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another, for the assembly was in confusion. Most of them didn’t know why they had come together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. Alexander beckoned with his hand, and would have made a defense to the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when they perceived that he was a Jew, all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the town clerk had quieted the multitude, he said, “You men of Ephesus, what man is there who doesn’t know that the city of the Ephesians is temple keeper of the great goddess Artemis, and of the image which fell down from Zeus?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing then that these things can’t be denied, you ought to be quiet and to do nothing rash.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you have brought these men here, who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of your goddess.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you seek anything about other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For indeed we are in danger of being accused concerning today’s riot, there being no cause. Concerning it, we wouldn’t be able to give an account of this commotion.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, took leave of them, and departed to go into Macedonia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had gone through those parts, and had encouraged them with many words, he came into Greece.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had spent three months there, and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he determined to return through Macedonia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These accompanied him as far as Asia: Sopater of Beroea; Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians; Gaius of Derbe; Timothy; and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But these had gone ahead, and were waiting for us at Troas.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas in five days, where we stayed seven days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the first day of the week, when the disciples were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day, and continued his speech until midnight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were many lights in the upper room where we were gathered together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A certain young man named Eutychus sat in the window, weighed down with deep sleep. As Paul spoke still longer, being weighed down by his sleep, he fell down from the third floor and was taken up dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul went down and fell upon him, and embracing him said, “Don’t be troubled, for his life is in him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had gone up, and had broken bread and eaten, and had talked with them a long while, even until break of day, he departed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They brought the boy in alive, and were greatly comforted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we, going ahead to the ship, set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there; for he had so arranged, intending himself to go by land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard, and came to Mitylene.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Sailing from there, we came the following day opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium, and the day after we came to Miletus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, that he might not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hastening, if it were possible for him, to be in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called to himself the elders of the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had come to him, he said to them, “You yourselves know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I was with you all the time,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: serving the Lord with all humility, with many tears, and with trials which happened to me by the plots of the Jews;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how I didn’t shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, teaching you publicly and from house to house,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, behold, I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions wait for me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But these things don’t count; nor do I hold my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my race with joy, and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to fully testify to the Good News of the grace of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Now, behold, I know that you all, among whom I went about preaching God’s Kingdom, will see my face no more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I testify to you today that I am clean from the blood of all men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I didn’t shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take heed, therefore, to yourselves, and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and  God which he purchased with his own blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I know that after my departure, vicious wolves will enter in among you, not sparing the flock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Men will arise from among your own selves, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore watch, remembering that for a period of three years I didn’t cease to admonish everyone night and day with tears.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, brothers, I entrust you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build up, and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I coveted no one’s silver, gold, or clothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You yourselves know that these hands served my necessities, and those who were with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had spoken these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They all wept freely, and fell on Paul’s neck and kissed him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: sorrowing most of all because of the word which he had spoken, that they should see his face no more. Then they accompanied him to the ship.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had departed from them and had set sail, we came with a straight course to Cos, and the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, we went aboard, and set sail.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had come in sight of Cyprus, leaving it on the left hand, we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, for the ship was there to unload her cargo.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having found disciples, we stayed there seven days. These said to Paul through the Spirit that he should not go up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When those days were over, we departed and went on our journey. They all, with wives and children, brought us on our way until we were out of the city. Kneeling down on the beach, we prayed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After saying goodbye to each other, we went on board the ship, and they returned home again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had finished the voyage from Tyre, we arrived at Ptolemais. We greeted the brothers and stayed with them one day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the next day, we who were Paul’s companions departed, and came to Caesarea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As we stayed there some days, a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Coming to us and taking Paul’s belt, he bound his own feet and hands, and said, “The Holy Spirit says: ‘So the Jews at Jerusalem will bind the man who owns this belt, and will deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we heard these things, both we and the people of that place begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Paul answered, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, “The Lord’s will be done.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these days we took up our baggage and went up to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of the disciples from Caesarea also went with us, bringing one Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we would stay.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had come to Jerusalem, the brothers received us gladly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The day following, Paul went in with us to James; and all the elders were present.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had greeted them, he reported one by one the things which God had worked among the Gentiles through his ministry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, when they heard it, glorified God. They said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews of those who have believed, and they are all zealous for the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They have been informed about you, that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children and not to walk after the customs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then? The assembly must certainly meet, for they will hear that you have come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore do what we tell you. We have four men who have taken a vow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take them and purify yourself with them, and pay their expenses for them, that they may shave their heads. Then all will know that there is no truth in the things that they have been informed about you, but that you yourself also walk keeping the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But concerning the Gentiles who believe, we have written our decision that they should observe no such thing, except that they should keep themselves from food offered to idols, from blood, from strangled things, and from sexual immorality.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Paul took the men, and the next day purified himself and went with them into the temple, declaring the fulfillment of the days of purification, until the offering was offered for every one of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, when they saw him in the temple, stirred up all the multitude and laid hands on him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: crying out, “Men of Israel, help! This is the man who teaches all men everywhere against the people, and the law, and this place. Moreover, he also brought Greeks into the temple, and has defiled this holy place!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they had seen Trophimus, the Ephesian, with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the city was moved and the people ran together. They seized Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Immediately the doors were shut.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they were trying to kill him, news came up to the commanding officer of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in an uproar.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately he took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them. They, when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers, stopped beating Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the commanding officer came near, arrested him, commanded him to be bound with two chains, and inquired who he was and what he had done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some shouted one thing, and some another, among the crowd. When he couldn’t find out the truth because of the noise, he commanded him to be brought into the barracks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he came to the stairs, he was carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the multitude of the people followed after, crying out, “Away with him!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Paul was about to be brought into the barracks, he asked the commanding officer, “May I speak to you?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Do you know Greek?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Aren’t you then the Egyptian, who before these days stirred up to sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the Assassins?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul said, “I am a Jew, from Tarsus in Cilicia, a citizen of no insignificant city. I beg you, allow me to speak to the people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had given him permission, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people. When there was a great silence, he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense which I now make to you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they were even more quiet. He said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are today.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As I made my journey, and came close to Damascus, about noon, suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I fell to the ground, and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn’t understand the voice of him who spoke to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Arise, and go into Damascus. There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I couldn’t see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ In that very hour I looked up at him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “When I had returned to Jerusalem, and while I prayed in the temple, I fell into a trance,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saw him saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said to me, ‘Depart, for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles.’”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice and said, “Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn’t fit to live!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As they cried out, threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks, ordering him to be examined by scourging, that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had tied him up with thongs, Paul asked the centurion who stood by, “Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and not found guilty?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the centurion heard it, he went to the commanding officer and told him, “Watch what you are about to do, for this man is a Roman!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The commanding officer came and asked him, “Tell me, are you a Roman?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “Yes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The commanding officer answered, “I bought my citizenship for a great price.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul said, “But I was born a Roman.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him, and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman, because he had bound him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But on the next day, desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews, he freed him from the bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, looking steadfastly at the council, said, “Brothers, I have lived before God in all good conscience until today.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest, Ananias, commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! Do you sit to judge me according to the law, and command me to be struck contrary to the law?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who stood by said, “Do you malign God’s high priest?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul said, “I didn’t know, brothers, that he was high priest. For it is written, ‘You shall not speak evil of a ruler of your people.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, “Men and brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. Concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, an argument arose between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the crowd was divided.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit; but the Pharisees confess all of these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A great clamor arose, and some of the scribes of the Pharisees’ part stood up, and contended, saying, “We find no evil in this man. But if a spirit or angel has spoken to him, let’s not fight against God!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When a great argument arose, the commanding officer, fearing that Paul would be torn in pieces by them, commanded the soldiers to go down and take him by force from among them, and bring him into the barracks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The following night, the Lord stood by him and said, “Cheer up, Paul, for as you have testified about me at Jerusalem, so you must testify also at Rome.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was day, some of the Jews banded together, and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink until they had killed Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were more than forty people who had made this conspiracy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They came to the chief priests and the elders, and said, “We have bound ourselves under a great curse to taste nothing until we have killed Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now therefore, you with the council inform the commanding officer that he should bring him down to you tomorrow, as though you were going to judge his case more exactly. We are ready to kill him before he comes near.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul’s sister’s son heard they were lying in wait, and he came and entered into the barracks and told Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul summoned one of the centurions, and said, “Bring this young man to the commanding officer, for he has something to tell him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So he took him, and brought him to the commanding officer, and said, “Paul, the prisoner, summoned me and asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The commanding officer took him by the hand, and going aside, asked him privately, “What is it that you have to tell me?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though intending to inquire somewhat more accurately concerning him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t yield to them, for more than forty men lie in wait for him, who have bound themselves under a curse to neither eat nor drink until they have killed him. Now they are ready, looking for the promise from you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the commanding officer let the young man go, charging him, “Tell no one that you have revealed these things to me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He called to himself two of the centurions, and said, “Prepare two hundred soldiers to go as far as Caesarea, with seventy horsemen, and two hundred men armed with spears, at the third hour of the night.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He asked them to provide animals, that they might set Paul on one, and bring him safely to Felix the governor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He wrote a letter like this:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix: Greetings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “This man was seized by the Jews, and was about to be killed by them, when I came with the soldiers and rescued him, having learned that he was a Roman.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Desiring to know the cause why they accused him, I brought him down to their council.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I found him to be accused about questions of their law, but not to be charged with anything worthy of death or of imprisonment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I was told that the Jews lay in wait for the man, I sent him to you immediately, charging his accusers also to bring their accusations against him before you. Farewell.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the soldiers, carrying out their orders, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But on the next day they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the barracks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they came to Caesarea and delivered the letter to the governor, they also presented Paul to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the governor had read it, he asked what province he was from. When he understood that he was from Cilicia, he said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I will hear you fully when your accusers also arrive.” He commanded that he be kept in Herod’s palace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After five days, the high priest, Ananias, came down with certain elders and an orator, one Tertullus. They informed the governor against Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was called, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, “Seeing that by you we enjoy much peace, and that prosperity is coming to this nation by your foresight,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: we accept it in all ways and in all places, most excellent Felix, with all thankfulness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that I don’t delay you, I entreat you to bear with us and hear a few words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we have found this man to be a plague, an instigator of insurrections among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He even tried to profane the temple, and we arrested him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By examining him yourself you may ascertain all these things of which we accuse him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Jews also joined in the attack, affirming that these things were so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the governor had beckoned to him to speak, Paul answered, “Because I know that you have been a judge of this nation for many years, I cheerfully make my defense,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing that you can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the temple they didn’t find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the synagogues, or in the city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nor can they prove to you the things of which they now accuse me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this I confess to you, that after the Way, which they call a sect, so I serve the God of our fathers, believing all things which are according to the law, and which are written in the prophets;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having hope toward God, which these also themselves look for, that there will be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this I also practice always having a conscience void of offense toward God and men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now after some years, I came to bring gifts for the needy to my nation, and offerings;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: amid which certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, not with a mob, nor with turmoil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They ought to have been here before you, and to make accusation, if they had anything against me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or else let these men themselves say what injustice they found in me when I stood before the council,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: unless it is for this one thing that I cried standing among them, ‘Concerning the resurrection of the dead I am being judged before you today!’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Felix, having more exact knowledge concerning the Way, deferred them, saying, “When Lysias, the commanding officer, comes down, I will decide your case.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He ordered the centurion that Paul should be kept in custody, and should have some privileges, and not to forbid any of his friends to serve him or to visit him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But after some days, Felix came with Drusilla, his wife, who was a Jewess, and sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he reasoned about righteousness, self-control, and the judgment to come, Felix was terrified, and answered, “Go your way for this time, and when it is convenient for me, I will summon you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Meanwhile, he also hoped that money would be given to him by Paul, that he might release him. Therefore also he sent for him more often and talked with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when two years were fulfilled, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus, and desiring to gain favor with the Jews, Felix left Paul in bonds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Festus therefore, having come into the province, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the high priest and the principal men of the Jews informed him against Paul, and they begged him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: asking a favor against him, that he would summon him to Jerusalem; plotting to kill him on the way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However Festus answered that Paul should be kept in custody at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to depart shortly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Let them therefore”, he said, “that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man, let them accuse him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had stayed among them more than ten days, he went down to Caesarea, and on the next day he sat on the judgment seat, and commanded Paul to be brought.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had come, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing against him many and grievous charges which they could not prove,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: while he said in his defense, “Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar, have I sinned at all.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Festus, desiring to gain favor with the Jews, answered Paul and said, “Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem, and be judged by me there concerning these things?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Paul said, “I am standing before Caesar’s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried. I have done no wrong to the Jews, as you also know very well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I have done wrong and have committed anything worthy of death, I don’t refuse to die; but if none of those things is true that they accuse me of, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, “You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you shall go.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when some days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived at Caesarea, and greeted Festus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he stayed there many days, Festus laid Paul’s case before the king, saying, “There is a certain man left a prisoner by Felix;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: about whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, asking for a sentence against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I answered them that it is not the custom of the Romans to give up any man to destruction before the accused has met the accusers face to face and has had opportunity to make his defense concerning the matter laid against him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore they had come together here, I didn’t delay, but on the next day sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the accusers stood up, they brought no charges against him of such things as I supposed;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but had certain questions against him about their own religion, and about one Jesus, who was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being perplexed how to inquire concerning these things, I asked whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem and there be judged concerning these matters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Paul had appealed to be kept for the decision of the emperor, I commanded him to be kept until I could send him to Caesar.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Agrippa said to Festus, “I also would like to hear the man myself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Tomorrow,” he said, “you shall hear him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So on the next day, when Agrippa and Bernice had come with great pomp, and they had entered into the place of hearing with the commanding officers and the principal men of the city, at the command of Festus, Paul was brought in.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Festus said, “King Agrippa, and all men who are here present with us, you see this man about whom all the multitude of the Jews petitioned me, both at Jerusalem and here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and as he himself appealed to the emperor I determined to send him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of whom I have no certain thing to write to my lord. Therefore I have brought him out before you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, that, after examination, I may have something to write.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner, not to also specify the charges against him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Agrippa said to Paul, “You may speak for yourself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then Paul stretched out his hand, and made his defense.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I think myself happy, King Agrippa, that I am to make my defense before you today concerning all the things that I am accused by the Jews,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: especially because you are expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews. Therefore I beg you to hear me patiently.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Indeed, all the Jews know my way of life from my youth up, which was from the beginning among my own nation and at Jerusalem;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having known me from the first, if they are willing to testify, that after the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I stand here to be judged for the hope of the promise made by God to our fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which our twelve tribes, earnestly serving night and day, hope to attain. Concerning this hope I am accused by the Jews, King Agrippa!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why is it judged incredible with you, if God does raise the dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I myself most certainly thought that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I also did this in Jerusalem. I both shut up many of the saints in prisons, having received authority from the chief priests, and when they were put to death I gave my vote against them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Punishing them often in all the synagogues, I tried to make them blaspheme. Being exceedingly enraged against them, I persecuted them even to foreign cities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Whereupon as I traveled to Damascus with the authority and commission from the chief priests,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: at noon, O king, I saw on the way a light from the sky, brighter than the sun, shining around me and those who traveled with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice saying to me in the Hebrew language, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I said, ‘Who are you, Lord?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He said, ‘I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But arise, and stand on your feet, for I have appeared to you for this purpose: to appoint you a servant and a witness both of the things which you have seen, and of the things which I will reveal to you;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: delivering you from the people, and from the Gentiles, to whom I send you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive remission of sins and an inheritance among those who are sanctified by faith in me.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Therefore, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but declared first to them of Damascus, at Jerusalem, and throughout all the country of Judea, and also to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, doing works worthy of repentance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having therefore obtained the help that is from God, I stand to this day testifying both to small and great, saying nothing but what the prophets and Moses said would happen,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how the Christ must suffer, and how, by the resurrection of the dead, he would be first to proclaim light both to these people and to the Gentiles.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he thus made his defense, Festus said with a loud voice, “Paul, you are crazy! Your great learning is driving you insane!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he said, “I am not crazy, most excellent Festus, but boldly declare words of truth and reasonableness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the king knows of these things, to whom also I speak freely. For I am persuaded that none of these things is hidden from him, for this has not been done in a corner.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you believe.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Agrippa said to Paul, “With a little persuasion are you trying to make me a Christian?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul said, “I pray to God, that whether with little or with much, not only you, but also all that hear me today, might become such as I am, except for these bonds.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The king rose up with the governor, and Bernice, and those who sat with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had withdrawn, they spoke to one another, saying, “This man does nothing worthy of death or of bonds.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Agrippa said to Festus, “This man might have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was determined that we should sail for Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners to a centurion named Julius, of the Augustan band.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Embarking in a ship of Adramyttium, which was about to sail to places on the coast of Asia, we put to sea, Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The next day, we touched at Sidon. Julius treated Paul kindly, and gave him permission to go to his friends and refresh himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Putting to sea from there, we sailed under the lee of Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had sailed across the sea which is off Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra, a city of Lycia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy, and he put us on board.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had sailed slowly many days, and had come with difficulty opposite Cnidus, the wind not allowing us further, we sailed under the lee of Crete, opposite Salmone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: With difficulty sailing along it we came to a certain place called Fair Havens, near the city of Lasea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When much time had passed and the voyage was now dangerous, because the Fast had now already gone by, Paul admonished them

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and said to them, “Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury and much loss, not only of the cargo and the ship, but also of our lives.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the centurion gave more heed to the master and to the owner of the ship than to those things which were spoken by Paul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because the haven was not suitable to winter in, the majority advised going to sea from there, if by any means they could reach Phoenix, and winter there, which is a port of Crete, looking southwest and northwest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the south wind blew softly, supposing that they had obtained their purpose, they weighed anchor and sailed along Crete, close to shore.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But before long, a stormy wind beat down from shore, which is called Euroclydon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the ship was caught and couldn’t face the wind, we gave way to it and were driven along.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Running under the lee of a small island called Clauda, we were able, with difficulty, to secure the boat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After they had hoisted it up, they used cables to help reinforce the ship. Fearing that they would run aground on the Syrtis sand bars, they lowered the sea anchor, and so were driven along.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As we labored exceedingly with the storm, the next day they began to throw things overboard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the third day, they threw out the ship’s tackle with their own hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When neither sun nor stars shone on us for many days, and no small storm pressed on us, all hope that we would be saved was now taken away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had been long without food, Paul stood up in the middle of them, and said, “Sirs, you should have listened to me, and not have set sail from Crete and have gotten this injury and loss.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I exhort you to cheer up, for there will be no loss of life among you, but only of the ship.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there stood by me this night an angel, belonging to the God whose I am and whom I serve,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, ‘Don’t be afraid, Paul. You must stand before Caesar. Behold, God has granted you all those who sail with you.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, sirs, cheer up! For I believe God, that it will be just as it has been spoken to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we must run aground on a certain island.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the fourteenth night had come, as we were driven back and forth in the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors surmised that they were drawing near to some land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They took soundings, and found twenty fathoms. After a little while, they took soundings again, and found fifteen fathoms.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fearing that we would run aground on rocky ground, they let go four anchors from the stern, and wished for daylight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As the sailors were trying to flee out of the ship, and had lowered the boat into the sea, pretending that they would lay out anchors from the bow,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, “Unless these stay in the ship, you can’t be saved.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat, and let it fall off.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: While the day was coming on, Paul begged them all to take some food, saying, “Today is the fourteenth day that you wait and continue fasting, having taken nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I beg you to take some food; for this is for your safety; for not a hair will perish from any of your heads.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said this, and had taken bread, he gave thanks to God in the presence of all, then he broke it and began to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they all cheered up, and they also took food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In all, we were two hundred seventy-six souls on the ship.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, throwing out the wheat into the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When it was day, they didn’t recognize the land, but they noticed a certain bay with a beach, and they decided to try to drive the ship onto it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Casting off the anchors, they left them in the sea, at the same time untying the rudder ropes. Hoisting up the foresail to the wind, they made for the beach.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But coming to a place where two seas met, they ran the vessel aground. The bow struck and remained immovable, but the stern began to break up by the violence of the waves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The soldiers’ counsel was to kill the prisoners, so that none of them would swim out and escape.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the centurion, desiring to save Paul, stopped them from their purpose, and commanded that those who could swim should throw themselves overboard first to go toward the land;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the rest should follow, some on planks, and some on other things from the ship. So they all escaped safely to the land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we had escaped, then they learned that the island was called Malta.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The natives showed us uncommon kindness; for they kindled a fire and received us all, because of the present rain and because of the cold.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire, a viper came out because of the heat, and fastened on his hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand, they said to one another, “No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he has escaped from the sea, yet Justice has not allowed to live.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However he shook off the creature into the fire, and wasn’t harmed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they expected that he would have swollen or fallen down dead suddenly, but when they watched for a long time and saw nothing bad happen to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island, named Publius, who received us, and courteously entertained us for three days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The father of Publius lay sick of fever and dysentery. Paul entered in to him, prayed, and laying his hands on him, healed him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then when this was done, the rest also who had diseases in the island came and were cured.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They also honored us with many honors, and when we sailed, they put on board the things that we needed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After three months, we set sail in a ship of Alexandria which had wintered in the island, whose sign was “The Twin Brothers.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Touching at Syracuse, we stayed there three days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From there we circled around and arrived at Rhegium. After one day, a south wind sprang up, and on the second day we came to Puteoli,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: where we found brothers, and were entreated to stay with them for seven days. So we came to Rome.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From there the brothers, when they heard of us, came to meet us as far as The Market of Appius and The Three Taverns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and took courage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When we entered into Rome, the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard, but Paul was allowed to stay by himself with the soldier who guarded him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After three days Paul called together those who were the leaders of the Jews. When they had come together, he said to them, “I, brothers, though I had done nothing against the people or the customs of our fathers, still was delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, when they had examined me, desired to set me free, because there was no cause of death in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the Jews spoke against it, I was constrained to appeal to Caesar, not that I had anything about which to accuse my nation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause therefore I asked to see you and to speak with you. For because of the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to him, “We neither received letters from Judea concerning you, nor did any of the brothers come here and report or speak any evil of you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we desire to hear from you what you think. For, as concerning this sect, it is known to us that everywhere it is spoken against.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they had appointed him a day, many people came to him at his lodging. He explained to them, testifying about God’s Kingdom, and persuading them concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses and from the prophets, from morning until evening.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some believed the things which were spoken, and some disbelieved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they didn’t agree among themselves, they departed after Paul had spoken one word, “The Holy Spirit spoke rightly through Isaiah the prophet to our fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Be it known therefore to you, that the salvation of God is sent to the nations, and they will listen.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had said these words, the Jews departed, having a great dispute among themselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul stayed two whole years in his own rented house and received all who were coming to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: preaching God’s Kingdom, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness, without hindrance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, a servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, set apart for the Good News of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which he promised before through his prophets in the holy Scriptures,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: concerning his Son, who was born of the offspring of David according to the flesh,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who was declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the Spirit of holiness, by the resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: through whom we received grace and apostleship for obedience of faith among all the nations for his name’s sake;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: among whom you are also called to belong to Jesus Christ;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to all who are in Rome, beloved of God, called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, that your faith is proclaimed throughout the whole world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God is my witness, whom I serve in my spirit in the Good News of his Son, how unceasingly I make mention of you always in my prayers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: requesting, if by any means now at last I may be prospered by the will of God to come to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift, to the end that you may be established;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that is, that I with you may be encouraged in you, each of us by the other’s faith, both yours and mine.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I don’t desire to have you unaware, brothers, that I often planned to come to you, and was hindered so far, that I might have some fruit among you also, even as among the rest of the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
