sources.text: I am debtor both to Greeks and to foreigners, both to the wise and to the foolish.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So as much as is in me, I am eager to preach the Good News to you also who are in Rome.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am not ashamed of the Good News of Christ, because it is the power of God for salvation for everyone who believes, for the Jew first, and also for the Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in it is revealed God’s righteousness from faith to faith. As it is written, “But the righteous shall live by faith.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who suppress the truth in unrighteousness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because that which is known of God is revealed in them, for God revealed it to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the invisible things of him since the creation of the world are clearly seen, being perceived through the things that are made, even his everlasting power and divinity, that they may be without excuse.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because knowing God, they didn’t glorify him as God, and didn’t give thanks, but became vain in their reasoning, and their senseless heart was darkened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and traded the glory of the incorruptible God for the likeness of an image of corruptible man, and of birds, four-footed animals, and creeping things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore God also gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to uncleanness, that their bodies should be dishonored among themselves;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this reason, God gave them up to vile passions. For their women changed the natural function into that which is against nature.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise also the men, leaving the natural function of the woman, burned in their lust toward one another, men doing what is inappropriate with men, and receiving in themselves the due penalty of their error.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as they refused to have God in their knowledge, God gave them up to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not fitting;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being filled with all unrighteousness, sexual immorality, wickedness, covetousness, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, evil habits, secret slanderers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: backbiters, hateful to God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors of evil things, disobedient to parents,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: without understanding, covenant breakers, without natural affection, unforgiving, unmerciful;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, knowing the ordinance of God, that those who practice such things are worthy of death, not only do the same, but also approve of those who practice them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore you are without excuse, O man, whoever you are who judge. For in that which you judge another, you condemn yourself. For you who judge practice the same things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that the judgment of God is according to truth against those who practice such things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do you think this, O man who judges those who practice such things, and do the same, that you will escape the judgment of God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or do you despise the riches of his goodness, forbearance, and patience, not knowing that the goodness of God leads you to repentance?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But according to your hardness and unrepentant heart you are treasuring up for yourself wrath in the day of wrath, revelation, and of the righteous judgment of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who “will pay back to everyone according to their works:”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to those who by perseverance in well-doing seek for glory, honor, and incorruptibility, eternal life;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but to those who are self-seeking, and don’t obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, will be wrath, indignation,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: oppression, and anguish on every soul of man who does evil, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But glory, honor, and peace go to every man who does good, to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is no partiality with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as many as have sinned without the law will also perish without the law. As many as have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it isn’t the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law will be justified

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (for when Gentiles who don’t have the law do by nature the things of the law, these, not having the law, are a law to themselves,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts, their conscience testifying with them, and their thoughts among themselves accusing or else excusing them)

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in the day when God will judge the secrets of men, according to my Good News, by Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Indeed you bear the name of a Jew, rest on the law, glory in God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: know his will, and approve the things that are excellent, being instructed out of the law,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and are confident that you yourself are a guide of the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: a corrector of the foolish, a teacher of babies, having in the law the form of knowledge and of the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You therefore who teach another, don’t you teach yourself? You who preach that a man shouldn’t steal, do you steal?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You who say a man shouldn’t commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You who glory in the law, do you dishonor God by disobeying the law?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For “the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you,”  just as it is written.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For circumcision indeed profits, if you are a doer of the law, but if you are a transgressor of the law, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore the uncircumcised keep the ordinances of the law, won’t his uncircumcision be accounted as circumcision?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Won’t the uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfills the law, judge you, who with the letter and circumcision are a transgressor of the law?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he is a Jew who is one inwardly, and circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit not in the letter; whose praise is not from men, but from God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then what advantage does the Jew have? Or what is the profit of circumcision?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Much in every way! Because first of all, they were entrusted with the revelations of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what if some were without faith? Will their lack of faith nullify the faithfulness of God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May it never be! Yes, let God be found true, but every man a liar. As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if our unrighteousness commends the righteousness of God, what will we say? Is God unrighteous who inflicts wrath? I speak like men do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May it never be! For then how will God judge the world?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the truth of God through my lie abounded to his glory, why am I also still judged as a sinner?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why not (as we are slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say), “Let’s do evil, that good may come?” Those who say so are justly condemned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then? Are we better than they? No, in no way. For we previously warned both Jews and Greeks that they are all under sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now we know that whatever things the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, that every mouth may be closed, and all the world may be brought under the judgment of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because by the works of the law, no flesh will be justified in his sight; for through the law comes the knowledge of sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now apart from the law, a righteousness of God has been revealed, being testified by the law and the prophets;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all and on all those who believe. For there is no distinction,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for all have sinned, and fall short of the glory of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom God sent to be an atoning sacrifice, through faith in his blood, for a demonstration of his righteousness through the passing over of prior sins, in God’s forbearance;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to demonstrate his righteousness at this present time; that he might himself be just, and the justifier of him who has faith in Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Where then is the boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? Of works? No, but by a law of faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We maintain therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the works of the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or is God the God of Jews only? Isn’t he the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: since indeed there is one God who will justify the circumcised by faith, and the uncircumcised through faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do we then nullify the law through faith? May it never be! No, we establish the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then will we say that Abraham, our forefather, has found according to the flesh?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not toward God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to him who works, the reward is not counted as grace, but as something owed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to him who doesn’t work, but believes in him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is accounted for righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as David also pronounces blessing on the man to whom God counts righteousness apart from works,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is this blessing then pronounced on the circumcised, or on the uncircumcised also? For we say that faith was accounted to Abraham for righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How then was it counted? When he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He received the sign of circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had while he was in uncircumcision, that he might be the father of all those who believe, though they might be in uncircumcision, that righteousness might also be accounted to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is the father of circumcision to those who not only are of the circumcision, but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had in uncircumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the promise to Abraham and to his offspring that he should be heir of the world wasn’t through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if those who are of the law are heirs, faith is made void, and the promise is made of no effect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the law produces wrath, for where there is no law, neither is there disobedience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause it is of faith, that it may be according to grace, to the end that the promise may be sure to all the offspring, not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written, “I have made you a father of many nations.” This is in the presence of him whom he believed: God, who gives life to the dead, and calls the things that are not, as though they were.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Besides hope, Abraham in hope believed, to the end that he might become a father of many nations, according to that which had been spoken, “So will your offspring be.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Without being weakened in faith, he didn’t consider his own body, already having been worn out, (he being about a hundred years old), and the deadness of Sarah’s womb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet, looking to the promise of God, he didn’t waver through unbelief, but grew strong through faith, giving glory to God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and being fully assured that what he had promised, he was also able to perform.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore it also was “credited to him for righteousness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now it was not written that it was accounted to him for his sake alone,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but for our sake also, to whom it will be accounted, who believe in him who raised Jesus, our Lord, from the dead,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who was delivered up for our trespasses, and was raised for our justification.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being therefore justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: through whom we also have our access by faith into this grace in which we stand. We rejoice in hope of the glory of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only this, but we also rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces perseverance;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and perseverance, proven character; and proven character, hope:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and hope doesn’t disappoint us, because God’s love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who was given to us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For while we were yet weak, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For one will hardly die for a righteous man. Yet perhaps for a righteous person someone would even dare to die.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God commends his own love toward us, in that while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Much more then, being now justified by his blood, we will be saved from God’s wrath through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if while we were enemies, we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we will be saved by his life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only so, but we also rejoice in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received the reconciliation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore as sin entered into the world through one man, and death through sin; so death passed to all men, because all sinned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For until the law, sin was in the world; but sin is not charged when there is no law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless death reigned from Adam until Moses, even over those whose sins weren’t like Adam’s disobedience, who is a foreshadowing of him who was to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the free gift isn’t like the trespass. For if by the trespass of the one the many died, much more did the grace of God, and the gift by the grace of the one man, Jesus Christ, abound to the many.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The gift is not as through one who sinned; for the judgment came by one to condemnation, but the free gift came of many trespasses to justification.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if by the trespass of the one, death reigned through the one; so much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one, Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then as through one trespass, all men were condemned; even so through one act of righteousness, all men were justified to life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as through the one man’s disobedience many were made sinners, even so through the obedience of the one, many will be made righteous.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The law came in that the trespass might abound; but where sin abounded, grace abounded more exceedingly;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that as sin reigned in death, even so grace might reign through righteousness to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin, that grace may abound?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May it never be! We who died to sin, how could we live in it any longer?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or don’t you know that all we who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We were buried therefore with him through baptism into death, that just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, so we also might walk in newness of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death, we will also be part of his resurrection;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing this, that our old man was crucified with him, that the body of sin might be done away with, so that we would no longer be in bondage to sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who has died has been freed from sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with him;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that Christ, being raised from the dead, dies no more. Death no longer has dominion over him!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the death that he died, he died to sin one time; but the life that he lives, he lives to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thus consider yourselves also to be dead to sin, but alive to God in Christ Jesus our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t let sin reign in your mortal body, that you should obey it in its lusts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Also, do not present your members to sin as instruments of unrighteousness, but present yourselves to God as alive from the dead, and your members as instruments of righteousness to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For sin will not have dominion over you. For you are not under law, but under grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then? Shall we sin, because we are not under law, but under grace? May it never be!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that when you present yourselves as servants and obey someone, you are the servants of whomever you obey; whether of sin to death, or of obedience to righteousness?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But thanks be to God, that, whereas you were bondservants of sin, you became obedient from the heart to that form of teaching to which you were delivered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being made free from sin, you became bondservants of righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I speak in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh, for as you presented your members as servants to uncleanness and to wickedness upon wickedness, even so now present your members as servants to righteousness for sanctification.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when you were servants of sin, you were free from righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What fruit then did you have at that time in the things of which you are now ashamed? For the end of those things is death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now, being made free from sin and having become servants of God, you have your fruit of sanctification and the result of eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or don’t you know, brothers (for I speak to men who know the law), that the law has dominion over a man for as long as he lives?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the woman that has a husband is bound by law to the husband while he lives, but if the husband dies, she is discharged from the law of the husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then if, while the husband lives, she is joined to another man, she would be called an adulteress. But if the husband dies, she is free from the law, so that she is no adulteress, though she is joined to another man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, my brothers, you also were made dead to the law through the body of Christ, that you would be joined to another, to him who was raised from the dead, that we might produce fruit to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when we were in the flesh, the sinful passions which were through the law worked in our members to bring out fruit to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now we have been discharged from the law, having died to that in which we were held; so that we serve in newness of the spirit, and not in oldness of the letter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What shall we say then? Is the law sin? May it never be! However, I wouldn’t have known sin, except through the law. For I wouldn’t have known coveting, unless the law had said, “You shall not covet.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But sin, finding occasion through the commandment, produced in me all kinds of coveting. For apart from the law, sin is dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was alive apart from the law once, but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The commandment which was for life, this I found to be for death;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for sin, finding occasion through the commandment, deceived me, and through it killed me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore the law indeed is holy, and the commandment holy, and righteous, and good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Did then that which is good become death to me? May it never be! But sin, that it might be shown to be sin, was producing death in me through that which is good; that through the commandment sin might become exceedingly sinful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we know that the law is spiritual, but I am fleshly, sold under sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I don’t know what I am doing. For I don’t practice what I desire to do; but what I hate, that I do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if what I don’t desire, that I do, I consent to the law that it is good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So now it is no more I that do it, but sin which dwells in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I know that in me, that is, in my flesh, dwells no good thing. For desire is present with me, but I don’t find it doing that which is good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the good which I desire, I don’t do; but the evil which I don’t desire, that I practice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if what I don’t desire, that I do, it is no more I that do it, but sin which dwells in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I find then the law that, to me, while I desire to do good, evil is present.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I delight in God’s law after the inward man,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I see a different law in my members, warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into captivity under the law of sin which is in my members.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What a wretched man I am! Who will deliver me out of the body of this death?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank God through Jesus Christ, our Lord! So then with the mind, I myself serve God’s law, but with the flesh, sin’s law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is therefore now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus, who don’t walk according to the flesh, but according to the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what the law couldn’t do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God did, sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin, he condemned sin in the flesh;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit, the things of the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the mind of the flesh is death, but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because the mind of the flesh is hostile toward God; for it is not subject to God’s law, neither indeed can it be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who are in the flesh can’t please God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if it is so that the Spirit of God dwells in you. But if any man doesn’t have the Spirit of Christ, he is not his.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Christ is in you, the body is dead because of sin, but the spirit is alive because of righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if the Spirit of him who raised up Jesus from the dead dwells in you, he who raised up Christ Jesus from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through his Spirit who dwells in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, brothers, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if you live after the flesh, you must die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are children of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you didn’t receive the spirit of bondage again to fear, but you received the Spirit of adoption, by whom we cry, “Abba! Father!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are children of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and if children, then heirs: heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with him, that we may also be glorified with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which will be revealed toward us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the creation waits with eager expectation for the children of God to be revealed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the creation was subjected to vanity, not of its own will, but because of him who subjected it, in hope

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the creation itself also will be delivered from the bondage of decay into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we know that the whole creation groans and travails in pain together until now.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only so, but ourselves also, who have the first fruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for adoption, the redemption of our body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we were saved in hope, but hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for that which he sees?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we hope for that which we don’t see, we wait for it with patience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, the Spirit also helps our weaknesses, for we don’t know how to pray as we ought. But the Spirit himself makes intercession for us with groanings which can’t be uttered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who searches the hearts knows what is on the Spirit’s mind, because he makes intercession for the saints according to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, for those who are called according to his purpose.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whom he foreknew, he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whom he predestined, those he also called. Whom he called, those he also justified. Whom he justified, those he also glorified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who didn’t spare his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how would he not also with him freely give us all things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who could bring a charge against God’s chosen ones? It is God who justifies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who is he who condemns? It is Christ who died, yes rather, who was raised from the dead, who is at the right hand of God, who also makes intercession for us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Could oppression, or anguish, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I tell the truth in Christ. I am not lying, my conscience testifying with me in the Holy Spirit

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I have great sorrow and unceasing pain in my heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I could wish that I myself were accursed from Christ for my brothers’ sake, my relatives according to the flesh

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who are Israelites; whose is the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the service, and the promises;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of whom are the fathers, and from whom is Christ as concerning the flesh, who is over all, God, blessed forever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it is not as though the word of God has come to nothing. For they are not all Israel that are of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Neither, because they are Abraham’s offspring, are they all children. But, “your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That is, it is not the children of the flesh who are children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as heirs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is a word of promise, “At the appointed time I will come, and Sarah will have a son.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only so, but Rebekah also conceived by one, by our father Isaac.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For being not yet born, neither having done anything good or bad, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him who calls,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: it was said to her, “The elder will serve the younger.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as it is written, “Jacob I loved, but Esau I hated.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What shall we say then? Is there unrighteousness with God? May it never be!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he said to Moses, “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then it is not of him who wills, nor of him who runs, but of God who has mercy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Scripture says to Pharaoh, “For this very purpose I caused you to be raised up, that I might show in you my power, and that my name might be proclaimed in all the earth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, he has mercy on whom he desires, and he hardens whom he desires.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will say then to me, “Why does he still find fault? For who withstands his will?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But indeed, O man, who are you to reply against God? Will the thing formed ask him who formed it, “Why did you make me like this?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or hasn’t the potter a right over the clay, from the same lump to make one part a vessel for honor, and another for dishonor?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What if God, willing to show his wrath and to make his power known, endured with much patience vessels of wrath prepared for destruction,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that he might make known the riches of his glory on vessels of mercy, which he prepared beforehand for glory,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: us, whom he also called, not from the Jews only, but also from the Gentiles?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he says also in Hosea,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isaiah cries concerning Israel,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As Isaiah has said before,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, who didn’t follow after righteousness, attained to righteousness, even the righteousness which is of faith;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Israel, following after a law of righteousness, didn’t arrive at the law of righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why? Because they didn’t seek it by faith, but as it were by works of the law. They stumbled over the stumbling stone;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, my heart’s desire and my prayer to God is for Israel, that they may be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I testify about them that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and seeking to establish their own righteousness, they didn’t subject themselves to the righteousness of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Christ is the fulfillment of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Moses writes about the righteousness of the law, “The one who does them will live by them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the righteousness which is of faith says this, “Don’t say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’ (that is, to bring Christ down);

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or, ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’ (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead.)”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth, and in your heart;” that is, the word of faith which we preach:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that if you will confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For with the heart, one believes resulting in righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made resulting in salvation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Scripture says, “Whoever believes in him will not be disappointed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, and is rich to all who call on him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For, “Whoever will call on the name of the Lord will be saved.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How then will they call on him in whom they have not believed? How will they believe in him whom they have not heard? How will they hear without a preacher?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And how will they preach unless they are sent? As it is written:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they didn’t all listen to the glad news. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our report?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say, didn’t they hear? Yes, most certainly,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I ask, didn’t Israel know? First Moses says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Isaiah is very bold and says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But about Israel he says, “All day long I stretched out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I ask then, did God reject his people? May it never be! For I also am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God didn’t reject his people, which he foreknew. Or don’t you know what the Scripture says about Elijah? How he pleads with God against Israel:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Lord, they have killed your prophets, they have broken down your altars. I am left alone, and they seek my life.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But how does God answer him? “I have reserved for myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to Baal.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And if by grace, then it is no longer of works; otherwise grace is no longer grace. But if it is of works, it is no longer grace; otherwise work is no longer work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then? That which Israel seeks for, that he didn’t obtain, but the chosen ones obtained it, and the rest were hardened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: According as it is written, “God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear, to this very day.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: David says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I ask then, did they stumble that they might fall? May it never be! But by their fall salvation has come to the Gentiles, to provoke them to jealousy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now if their fall is the riches of the world, and their loss the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fullness?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I speak to you who are Gentiles. Since then as I am an apostle to Gentiles, I glorify my ministry;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if by any means I may provoke to jealousy those who are my flesh, and may save some of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the rejection of them is the reconciling of the world, what would their acceptance be, but life from the dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the first fruit is holy, so is the lump. If the root is holy, so are the branches.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, being a wild olive, were grafted in among them and became partaker with them of the root and of the richness of the olive tree,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: don’t boast over the branches. But if you boast, it is not you who support the root, but the root supports you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You will say then, “Branches were broken off, that I might be grafted in.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: True; by their unbelief they were broken off, and you stand by your faith. Don’t be conceited, but fear;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for if God didn’t spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See then the goodness and severity of God. Toward those who fell, severity; but toward you, goodness, if you continue in his goodness; otherwise you also will be cut off.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They also, if they don’t continue in their unbelief, will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if you were cut out of that which is by nature a wild olive tree, and were grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree, how much more will these, which are the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I don’t desire you to be ignorant, brothers, of this mystery, so that you won’t be wise in your own conceits, that a partial hardening has happened to Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and so all Israel will be saved. Even as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Concerning the Good News, they are enemies for your sake. But concerning the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as you in time past were disobedient to God, but now have obtained mercy by their disobedience,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even so these also have now been disobedient, that by the mercy shown to you they may also obtain mercy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God has bound all to disobedience, that he might have mercy on all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Oh the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and the knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past tracing out!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For of him, and through him, and to him are all things. To him be the glory for ever! Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I urge you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I say through the grace that was given me, to every man who is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think reasonably, as God has apportioned to each person a measure of faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even as we have many members in one body, and all the members don’t have the same function,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having gifts differing according to the grace that was given to us: if prophecy, let’s prophesy according to the proportion of our faith;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or service, let’s give ourselves to service; or he who teaches, to his teaching;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or he who exhorts, to his exhorting; he who gives, let him do it with generosity; he who rules, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor that which is evil. Cling to that which is good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor preferring one another;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not lagging in diligence; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: rejoicing in hope; enduring in troubles; continuing steadfastly in prayer;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: contributing to the needs of the saints; given to hospitality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Bless those who persecute you; bless, and don’t curse.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Rejoice with those who rejoice. Weep with those who weep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be of the same mind one toward another. Don’t set your mind on high things, but associate with the humble. Don’t be wise in your own conceits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Repay no one evil for evil. Respect what is honorable in the sight of all men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t seek revenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to God’s wrath. For it is written, “Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, says the Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let every soul be in subjection to the higher authorities, for there is no authority except from God, and those who exist are ordained by God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he who resists the authority withstands the ordinance of God; and those who withstand will receive to themselves judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For rulers are not a terror to the good work, but to the evil. Do you desire to have no fear of the authority? Do that which is good, and you will have praise from the authority,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he is a servant of God to you for good. But if you do that which is evil, be afraid, for he doesn’t bear the sword in vain; for he is a servant of God, an avenger for wrath to him who does evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore you need to be in subjection, not only because of the wrath, but also for conscience’ sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this reason you also pay taxes, for they are servants of God’s service, continually doing this very thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore give everyone what you owe: if you owe taxes, pay taxes; if customs, then customs; if respect, then respect; if honor, then honor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Owe no one anything, except to love one another; for he who loves his neighbor has fulfilled the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery,” “You shall not murder,” “You shall not steal,” “You shall not covet,”  and whatever other commandments there are, are all summed up in this saying, namely, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Love doesn’t harm a neighbor. Love therefore is the fulfillment of the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do this, knowing the time, that it is already time for you to awaken out of sleep, for salvation is now nearer to us than when we first believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The night is far gone, and the day is near. Let’s therefore throw off the deeds of darkness, and let’s put on the armor of light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s walk properly, as in the day; not in reveling and drunkenness, not in sexual promiscuity and lustful acts, and not in strife and jealousy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, for its lusts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now accept one who is weak in faith, but not for disputes over opinions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One man has faith to eat all things, but he who is weak eats only vegetables.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t let him who eats despise him who doesn’t eat. Don’t let him who doesn’t eat judge him who eats, for God has accepted him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who are you who judge another’s servant? To his own lord he stands or falls. Yes, he will be made to stand, for God has power to make him stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One man esteems one day as more important. Another esteems every day alike. Let each man be fully assured in his own mind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who observes the day, observes it to the Lord; and he who does not observe the day, to the Lord he does not observe it. He who eats, eats to the Lord, for he gives God thanks. He who doesn’t eat, to the Lord he doesn’t eat, and gives God thanks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For none of us lives to himself, and none dies to himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we live, we live to the Lord. Or if we die, we die to the Lord. If therefore we live or die, we are the Lord’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to this end Christ died, rose, and lived again, that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, why do you judge your brother? Or you again, why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then each one of us will give account of himself to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let’s not judge one another any more, but judge this rather, that no man put a stumbling block in his brother’s way, or an occasion for falling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know, and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus, that nothing is unclean of itself; except that to him who considers anything to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet if because of food your brother is grieved, you walk no longer in love. Don’t destroy with your food him for whom Christ died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then don’t let your good be slandered,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for God’s Kingdom is not eating and drinking, but righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who serves Christ in these things is acceptable to God and approved by men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, let’s follow after things which make for peace, and things by which we may build one another up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t overthrow God’s work for food’s sake. All things indeed are clean, however it is evil for that man who creates a stumbling block by eating.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is good to not eat meat, drink wine, nor do anything by which your brother stumbles, is offended, or is made weak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do you have faith? Have it to yourself before God. Happy is he who doesn’t judge himself in that which he approves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who doubts is condemned if he eats, because it isn’t of faith; and whatever is not of faith is sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to him who is able to establish you according to my Good News and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery which has been kept secret through long ages,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but now is revealed, and by the Scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the eternal God, is made known for obedience of faith to all the nations;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now we who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak, and not to please ourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let each one of us please his neighbor for that which is good, to be building him up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even Christ didn’t please himself. But, as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whatever things were written before were written for our learning, that through perseverance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the God of perseverance and of encouragement grant you to be of the same mind with one another according to Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that with one accord you may with one mouth glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore accept one another, even as Christ also accepted you, to the glory of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I say that Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision for the truth of God, that he might confirm the promises given to the fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, Isaiah says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I myself am also persuaded about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish others.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I write the more boldly to you in part, as reminding you, because of the grace that was given to me by God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the Good News of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be made acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have therefore my boasting in Christ Jesus in things pertaining to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I will not dare to speak of any things except those which Christ worked through me, for the obedience of the Gentiles, by word and deed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in the power of signs and wonders, in the power of God’s Spirit; so that from Jerusalem, and around as far as to Illyricum, I have fully preached the Good News of Christ;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yes, making it my aim to preach the Good News, not where Christ was already named, that I might not build on another’s foundation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But, as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore also I was hindered these many times from coming to you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but now, no longer having any place in these regions, and having these many years a longing to come to you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whenever I travel to Spain, I will come to you. For I hope to see you on my journey, and to be helped on my way there by you, if first I may enjoy your company for a while.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now, I say, I am going to Jerusalem, serving the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it has been the good pleasure of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor among the saints who are at Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, it has been their good pleasure, and they are their debtors. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, they owe it to them also to serve them in fleshly things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore I have accomplished this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will go on by way of you to Spain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of the Good News of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I beg you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, that you strive together with me in your prayers to God for me,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I may be delivered from those who are disobedient in Judea, and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I may come to you in joy through the will of God, and together with you, find rest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I commend to you Phoebe, our sister, who is a servant of the assembly that is at Cenchreae,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you receive her in the Lord, in a way worthy of the saints, and that you assist her in whatever matter she may need from you, for she herself also has been a helper of many, and of my own self.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who risked their own necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet the assembly that is in their house. Greet Epaenetus, my beloved, who is the first fruits of Achaia to Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Mary, who labored much for us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners, who are notable among the apostles, who were also in Christ before me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the household of Aristobulus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet them of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis, the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Rufus, the chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers  who are with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The assemblies of Christ greet you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I beg you, brothers, look out for those who are causing the divisions and occasions of stumbling, contrary to the doctrine which you learned, and turn away from them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who are such don’t serve our Lord, Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by their smooth and flattering speech, they deceive the hearts of the innocent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For your obedience has become known to all. I rejoice therefore over you. But I desire to have you wise in that which is good, but innocent in that which is evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I, Tertius, who write the letter, greet you in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Gaius, my host and host of the whole assembly, greets you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you, as does Quartus, the brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all! Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and our brother Sosthenes,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the assembly of God which is at Corinth—those who are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called saints, with all who call on the name of our Lord Jesus Christ in every place, both theirs and ours:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I always thank my God concerning you, for the grace of God which was given you in Christ Jesus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that in everything you were enriched in him, in all speech and all knowledge;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that you come behind in no gift; waiting for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who will also confirm you until the end, blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God is faithful, through whom you were called into the fellowship of his Son, Jesus Christ, our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I beg you, brothers, through the name of our Lord, Jesus Christ, that you all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you, but that you be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it has been reported to me concerning you, my brothers, by those who are from Chloe’s household, that there are contentions among you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I mean this, that each one of you says, “I follow Paul,” “I follow Apollos,” “I follow Cephas,” and, “I follow Christ.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Or were you baptized into the name of Paul?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank God that I baptized none of you, except Crispus and Gaius,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that no one should say that I had baptized you into my own name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (I also baptized the household of Stephanas; besides them, I don’t know whether I baptized any other.)

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the Good News—not in wisdom of words, so that the cross of Christ wouldn’t be made void.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are being saved it is the power of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the lawyer of this world? Hasn’t God made foolish the wisdom of this world?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For seeing that in the wisdom of God, the world through its wisdom didn’t know God, it was God’s good pleasure through the foolishness of the preaching to save those who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Jews ask for signs, Greeks seek after wisdom,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but we preach Christ crucified: a stumbling block to Jews, and foolishness to Greeks,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but to those who are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ is the power of God and the wisdom of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because the foolishness of God is wiser than men, and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you see your calling, brothers, that not many are wise according to the flesh, not many mighty, and not many noble;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but God chose the foolish things of the world that he might put to shame those who are wise. God chose the weak things of the world that he might put to shame the things that are strong.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God chose the lowly things of the world, and the things that are despised, and the things that don’t exist, that he might bring to nothing the things that exist,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that no flesh should boast before God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because of him, you are in Christ Jesus, who was made to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification, and redemption:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that, as it is written, “He who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I came to you, brothers, I didn’t come with excellence of speech or of wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I determined not to know anything among you except Jesus Christ and him crucified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was with you in weakness, in fear, and in much trembling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My speech and my preaching were not in persuasive words of human wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that your faith wouldn’t stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We speak wisdom, however, among those who are full grown, yet a wisdom not of this world nor of the rulers of this world who are coming to nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we speak God’s wisdom in a mystery, the wisdom that has been hidden, which God foreordained before the worlds for our glory,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which none of the rulers of this world has known. For had they known it, they wouldn’t have crucified the Lord of glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to us, God revealed them through the Spirit. For the Spirit searches all things, yes, the deep things of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For who among men knows the things of a man, except the spirit of the man, which is in him? Even so, no one knows the things of God, except God’s Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we received not the spirit of the world, but the Spirit which is from God, that we might know the things that were freely given to us by God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We also speak these things, not in words which man’s wisdom teaches, but which the Holy Spirit teaches, comparing spiritual things with spiritual things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the natural man doesn’t receive the things of God’s Spirit, for they are foolishness to him, and he can’t know them, because they are spiritually discerned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who is spiritual discerns all things, and he himself is judged by no one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For who has known the mind of the Lord, that he should instruct him?”  But we have Christ’s mind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, I couldn’t speak to you as to spiritual, but as to fleshly, as to babies in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I fed you with milk, not with meat; for you weren’t yet ready. Indeed, you aren’t ready even now,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for you are still fleshly. For insofar as there is jealousy, strife, and factions among you, aren’t you fleshly, and don’t you walk in the ways of men?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when one says, “I follow Paul,” and another, “I follow Apollos,” aren’t you fleshly?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who then is Apollos, and who is Paul, but servants through whom you believed, and each as the Lord gave to him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I planted. Apollos watered. But God gave the increase.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then neither he who plants is anything, nor he who waters, but God who gives the increase.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he who plants and he who waters are the same, but each will receive his own reward according to his own labor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are God’s fellow workers. You are God’s farming, God’s building.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: According to the grace of God which was given to me, as a wise master builder I laid a foundation, and another builds on it. But let each man be careful how he builds on it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For no one can lay any other foundation than that which has been laid, which is Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone builds on the foundation with gold, silver, costly stones, wood, hay, or stubble,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: each man’s work will be revealed. For the Day will declare it, because it is revealed in fire; and the fire itself will test what sort of work each man’s work is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man’s work remains which he built on it, he will receive a reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man’s work is burned, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, but as through fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that you are a temple of God, and that God’s Spirit lives in you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone destroys God’s temple, God will destroy him; for God’s temple is holy, which you are.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one deceive himself. If anyone thinks that he is wise among you in this world, let him become a fool, that he may become wise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the wisdom of this world is foolishness with God. For it is written, “He has taken the wise in their craftiness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And again, “The Lord knows the reasoning of the wise, that it is worthless.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let no one boast in men. For all things are yours,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whether Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come. All are yours,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and you are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So let a man think of us as Christ’s servants, and stewards of God’s mysteries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here, moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you, or by man’s judgment. Yes, I don’t judge my own self.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this, but he who judges me is the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each man will get his praise from God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now these things, brothers, I have in a figure transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that in us you might learn not to think beyond the things which are written, that none of you be puffed up against one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For who makes you different? And what do you have that you didn’t receive? But if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are already filled. You have already become rich. You have come to reign without us. Yes, and I wish that you did reign, that we also might reign with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For, I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last of all, like men sentenced to death. For we are made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You have honor, but we have dishonor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even to this present hour we hunger, thirst, are naked, are beaten, and have no certain dwelling place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We toil, working with our own hands. When people curse us, we bless. Being persecuted, we endure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I don’t write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though you have ten thousand tutors in Christ, you don’t have many fathers. For in Christ Jesus, I became your father through the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I beg you therefore, be imitators of me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord is willing. And I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is actually reported that there is sexual immorality among you, and such sexual immorality as is not even named among the Gentiles, that one has his father’s wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are arrogant, and didn’t mourn instead, that he who had done this deed might be removed from among you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I most certainly, as being absent in body but present in spirit, have already, as though I were present, judged him who has done this thing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, you being gathered together, and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: are to deliver such a one to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Your boasting is not good. Don’t you know that a little yeast leavens the whole lump?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Purge out the old yeast, that you may be a new lump, even as you are unleavened. For indeed Christ, our Passover, has been sacrificed in our place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let’s keep the feast, not with old yeast, neither with the yeast of malice and wickedness, but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I wrote to you in my letter to have no company with sexual sinners;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet not at all meaning with the sexual sinners of this world, or with the covetous and extortionists, or with idolaters; for then you would have to leave the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as it is, I wrote to you not to associate with anyone who is called a brother who is a sexual sinner, or covetous, or an idolater, or a slanderer, or a drunkard, or an extortionist. Don’t even eat with such a person.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what do I have to do with also judging those who are outside? Don’t you judge those who are within?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But those who are outside, God judges. “Put away the wicked man from among yourselves.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Dare any of you, having a matter against his neighbor, go to law before the unrighteous, and not before the saints?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that the saints will judge the world? And if the world is judged by you, are you unworthy to judge the smallest matters?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that we will judge angels? How much more, things that pertain to this life?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then you have to judge things pertaining to this life, do you set them to judge who are of no account in the assembly?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I say this to move you to shame. Isn’t there even one wise man among you who would be able to decide between his brothers?

sources.label: New Testament
