sources.text: But brother goes to law with brother, and that before unbelievers!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore it is already altogether a defect in you, that you have lawsuits one with another. Why not rather be wronged? Why not rather be defrauded?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No, but you yourselves do wrong and defraud, and that against your brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or don’t you know that the unrighteous will not inherit God’s Kingdom? Don’t be deceived. Neither the sexually immoral, nor idolaters, nor adulterers, nor male prostitutes, nor homosexuals,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor thieves, nor covetous, nor drunkards, nor slanderers, nor extortionists, will inherit God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some of you were such, but you were washed. But you were sanctified. But you were justified in the name of the Lord Jesus, and in the Spirit of our God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are expedient. “All things are lawful for me,” but I will not be brought under the power of anything.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Foods for the belly, and the belly for foods,” but God will bring to nothing both it and them. But the body is not for sexual immorality, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now God raised up the Lord, and will also raise us up by his power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that your bodies are members of Christ? Shall I then take the members of Christ and make them members of a prostitute? May it never be!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or don’t you know that he who is joined to a prostitute is one body? For, “The two”, he says, “will become one flesh.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who is joined to the Lord is one spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Flee sexual immorality! “Every sin that a man does is outside the body,” but he who commits sexual immorality sins against his own body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or don’t you know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit who is in you, whom you have from God? You are not your own,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body and in your spirit, which are God’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning the things about which you wrote to me: it is good for a man not to touch a woman.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But, because of sexual immoralities, let each man have his own wife, and let each woman have her own husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the husband give his wife the affection owed her, and likewise also the wife her husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The wife doesn’t have authority over her own body, but the husband. Likewise also the husband doesn’t have authority over his own body, but the wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t deprive one another, unless it is by consent for a season, that you may give yourselves to fasting and prayer, and may be together again, that Satan doesn’t tempt you because of your lack of self-control.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this I say by way of concession, not of commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet I wish that all men were like me. However each man has his own gift from God, one of this kind, and another of that kind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say to the unmarried and to widows, it is good for them if they remain even as I am.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if they don’t have self-control, let them marry. For it’s better to marry than to burn.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to the married I command—not I, but the Lord—that the wife not leave her husband

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (but if she departs, let her remain unmarried, or else be reconciled to her husband), and that the husband not leave his wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to the rest I—not the Lord—say, if any brother has an unbelieving wife, and she is content to live with him, let him not leave her.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman who has an unbelieving husband, and he is content to live with her, let her not leave her husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the unbelieving husband is sanctified in the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified in the husband. Otherwise your children would be unclean, but now they are holy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet if the unbeliever departs, let there be separation. The brother or the sister is not under bondage in such cases, but God has called us in peace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For how do you know, wife, whether you will save your husband? Or how do you know, husband, whether you will save your wife?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Only, as the Lord has distributed to each man, as God has called each, so let him walk. So I command in all the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Was anyone called having been circumcised? Let him not become uncircumcised. Has anyone been called in uncircumcision? Let him not be circumcised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let each man stay in that calling in which he was called.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Were you called being a bondservant? Don’t let that bother you, but if you get an opportunity to become free, use it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who was called in the Lord being a bondservant is the Lord’s free man. Likewise he who was called being free is Christ’s bondservant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You were bought with a price. Don’t become bondservants of men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, let each man, in whatever condition he was called, stay in that condition with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning virgins, I have no commandment from the Lord, but I give my judgment as one who has obtained mercy from the Lord to be trustworthy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I think that because of the distress that is on us, that it’s good for a man to remain as he is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are you bound to a wife? Don’t seek to be freed. Are you free from a wife? Don’t seek a wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you marry, you have not sinned. If a virgin marries, she has not sinned. Yet such will have oppression in the flesh, and I want to spare you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say this, brothers: the time is short, that from now on, both those who have wives may be as though they had none;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and those who weep, as though they didn’t weep; and those who rejoice, as though they didn’t rejoice; and those who buy, as though they didn’t possess;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and those who use the world, as not using it to the fullest. For the mode of this world passes away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I desire to have you to be free from cares. He who is unmarried is concerned for the things of the Lord, how he may please the Lord;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he who is married is concerned about the things of the world, how he may please his wife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is also a difference between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman cares about the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit. But she who is married cares about the things of the world—how she may please her husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This I say for your own profit; not that I may ensnare you, but for that which is appropriate, and that you may attend to the Lord without distraction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if any man thinks that he is behaving inappropriately toward his virgin, if she is past the flower of her age, and if need so requires, let him do what he desires. He doesn’t sin. Let them marry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who stands steadfast in his heart, having no urgency, but has power over his own will, and has determined in his own heart to keep his own virgin, does well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then both he who gives his own virgin in marriage does well, and he who doesn’t give her in marriage does better.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A wife is bound by law for as long as her husband lives; but if the husband is dead, she is free to be married to whomever she desires, only in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But she is happier if she stays as she is, in my judgment, and I think that I also have God’s Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn’t yet know as he ought to know.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though there are things that are called “gods”, whether in the heavens or on earth; as there are many “gods” and many “lords”;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, that knowledge isn’t in all men. But some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don’t eat, are we the worse; nor, if we eat, are we the better.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol’s temple, won’t his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forever more, that I don’t cause my brother to stumble.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren’t you my work in the Lord?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My defense to those who examine me is this:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Have we no right to eat and to drink?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn’t eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn’t drink from the flock’s milk?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn’t the law also say the same thing?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.” Is it for the oxen that God cares,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If others partake of this right over you, don’t we yet more? Nevertheless we didn’t use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Good News of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Good News should live from the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have used none of these things, and I don’t write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I preach the Good News, I have nothing to boast about; for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me if I don’t preach the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What then is my reward? That when I preach the Good News, I may present the Good News of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain those who are under the law;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I do this for the sake of the Good News, that I may be a joint partaker of it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I therefore run like that, not aimlessly. I fight like that, not beating the air,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be rejected.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I would not have you ignorant, brothers, that our fathers were all under the cloud, and all passed through the sea;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and were all baptized into Moses in the cloud and in the sea;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and all ate the same spiritual food;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and all drank the same spiritual drink. For they drank of a spiritual rock that followed them, and the rock was Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However with most of them, God was not well pleased, for they were overthrown in the wilderness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now these things were our examples, to the intent we should not lust after evil things, as they also lusted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be idolaters, as some of them were. As it is written, “The people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s not commit sexual immorality, as some of them committed, and in one day twenty-three thousand fell.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s not test Christ, as some of them tested, and perished by the serpents.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t grumble, as some of them also grumbled, and perished by the destroyer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now all these things happened to them by way of example, and they were written for our admonition, on whom the ends of the ages have come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let him who thinks he stands be careful that he doesn’t fall.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, my beloved, flee from idolatry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I speak as to wise men. Judge what I say.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The cup of blessing which we bless, isn’t it a sharing of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, isn’t it a sharing of the body of Christ?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because there is one loaf of bread, we, who are many, are one body; for we all partake of the one loaf of bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Consider Israel according to the flesh. Don’t those who eat the sacrifices participate in the altar?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What am I saying then? That a thing sacrificed to idols is anything, or that an idol is anything?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, and not to God, and I don’t desire that you would have fellowship with demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You can’t both drink the cup of the Lord and the cup of demons. You can’t both partake of the table of the Lord and of the table of demons.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or do we provoke the Lord to jealousy? Are we stronger than he?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things are profitable. “All things are lawful for me,” but not all things build up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one seek his own, but each one his neighbor’s good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whatever is sold in the butcher shop, eat, asking no question for the sake of conscience,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for “the earth is the Lord’s, and its fullness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if one of those who don’t believe invites you to a meal, and you are inclined to go, eat whatever is set before you, asking no questions for the sake of conscience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone says to you, “This was offered to idols,” don’t eat it for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience. For “the earth is the Lord’s, with all its fullness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Conscience, I say, not your own, but the other’s conscience. For why is my liberty judged by another conscience?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I partake with thankfulness, why am I denounced for something I give thanks for?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whether therefore you eat, or drink, or whatever you do, do all to the glory of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Give no occasion for stumbling, whether to Jews, or to Greeks, or to the assembly of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as I also please all men in all things, not seeking my own profit, but the profit of the many, that they may be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be imitators of me, even as I also am of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I praise you, brothers, that you remember me in all things, and hold firm the traditions, even as I delivered them to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I would have you know that the head of every man is Christ, and the head of the woman is man, and the head of Christ is God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonors his head.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But every woman praying or prophesying with her head uncovered dishonors her head. For it is one and the same thing as if she were shaved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if a woman is not covered, let her hair also be cut off. But if it is shameful for a woman to have her hair cut off or be shaved, let her be covered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a man indeed ought not to have his head covered, because he is the image and glory of God, but the woman is the glory of the man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For man is not from woman, but woman from man;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for man wasn’t created for the woman, but woman for the man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause the woman ought to have authority over her own head, because of the angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless, neither is the woman independent of the man, nor the man independent of the woman, in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as woman came from man, so a man also comes through a woman; but all things are from God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Judge for yourselves. Is it appropriate that a woman pray to God unveiled?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Doesn’t even nature itself teach you that if a man has long hair, it is a dishonor to him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if a woman has long hair, it is a glory to her, for her hair is given to her for a covering.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if any man seems to be contentious, we have no such custom, neither do God’s assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But in giving you this command, I don’t praise you, that you come together not for the better but for the worse.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For first of all, when you come together in the assembly, I hear that divisions exist among you, and I partly believe it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there also must be factions among you, that those who are approved may be revealed among you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When therefore you assemble yourselves together, it is not the Lord’s supper that you eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in your eating each one takes his own supper first. One is hungry, and another is drunken.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What, don’t you have houses to eat and to drink in? Or do you despise God’s assembly and put them to shame who don’t have enough? What shall I tell you? Shall I praise you? In this I don’t praise you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I received from the Lord that which also I delivered to you, that the Lord Jesus on the night in which he was betrayed took bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “Take, eat. This is my body, which is broken for you. Do this in memory of me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way he also took the cup, after supper, saying, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood. Do this, as often as you drink, in memory of me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as often as you eat this bread and drink this cup, you proclaim the Lord’s death until he comes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore whoever eats this bread or drinks the Lord’s cup in a way unworthy of the Lord will be guilty of the body and the blood of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let a man examine himself, and so let him eat of the bread, and drink of the cup.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who eats and drinks in an unworthy way eats and drinks judgment to himself if he doesn’t discern the Lord’s body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause many among you are weak and sickly, and not a few sleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we discerned ourselves, we wouldn’t be judged.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when we are judged, we are punished by the Lord, that we may not be condemned with the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, my brothers, when you come together to eat, wait for one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone is hungry, let him eat at home, lest your coming together be for judgment. The rest I will set in order whenever I come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don’t want you to be ignorant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You know that when you were heathen, you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There are various kinds of workings, but the same God, who works all things in all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to another faith, by the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healings, by the same Spirit;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the interpretation of languages.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the body is one, and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the body is not one member, but many.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the ear would say, “Because I’m not the eye, I’m not part of the body,” it’s not therefore not part of the body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If they were all one member, where would the body be?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now they are many members, but one body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The eye can’t tell the hand, “I have no need for you,” or again the head to the feet, “I have no need for you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant propriety;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whereas our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. When one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God has set some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, and various kinds of languages.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all miracle workers?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with various languages? Do all interpret?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But earnestly desire the best gifts. Moreover, I show a most excellent way to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I speak with the languages of men and of angels, but don’t have love, I have become sounding brass, or a clanging cymbal.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I have the gift of prophecy, and know all mysteries and all knowledge; and if I have all faith, so as to remove mountains, but don’t have love, I am nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I give away all my goods to feed the poor, and if I give my body to be burned, but don’t have love, it profits me nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Love is patient and is kind. Love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, and endures all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Love never fails. But where there are prophecies, they will be done away with. Where there are various languages, they will cease. Where there is knowledge, it will be done away with.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we know in part and we prophesy in part;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but when that which is complete has come, then that which is partial will be done away with.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I felt as a child, I thought as a child. Now that I have become a man, I have put away childish things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I will know fully, even as I was also fully known.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now faith, hope, and love remain—these three. The greatest of these is love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Follow after love and earnestly desire spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who speaks in another language speaks not to men, but to God; for no one understands; but in the Spirit he speaks mysteries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who prophesies speaks to men for their edification, exhortation, and consolation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who speaks in another language edifies himself, but he who prophesies edifies the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I desire to have you all speak with other languages, but rather that you would prophesy. For he is greater who prophesies than he who speaks with other languages, unless he interprets, that the assembly may be built up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now, brothers, if I come to you speaking with other languages, what would I profit you, unless I speak to you either by way of revelation, or of knowledge, or of prophesying, or of teaching?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even things without life, giving a voice, whether pipe or harp, if they didn’t give a distinction in the sounds, how would it be known what is piped or harped?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the trumpet gave an uncertain sound, who would prepare himself for war?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So also you, unless you uttered by the tongue words easy to understand, how would it be known what is spoken? For you would be speaking into the air.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There are, it may be, so many kinds of sounds in the world, and none of them is without meaning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then I don’t know the meaning of the sound, I would be to him who speaks a foreigner, and he who speaks would be a foreigner to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So also you, since you are zealous for spiritual gifts, seek that you may abound to the building up of the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let him who speaks in another language pray that he may interpret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I pray in another language, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also. I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Otherwise if you bless with the spirit, how will he who fills the place of the unlearned say the “Amen” at your giving of thanks, seeing he doesn’t know what you say?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you most certainly give thanks well, but the other person is not built up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank my God, I speak with other languages more than you all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However in the assembly I would rather speak five words with my understanding, that I might instruct others also, than ten thousand words in another language.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, don’t be children in thoughts, yet in malice be babies, but in thoughts be mature.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the law it is written, “By men of strange languages and by the lips of strangers I will speak to this people. They won’t even hear me that way, says the Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore other languages are for a sign, not to those who believe, but to the unbelieving; but prophesying is for a sign, not to the unbelieving, but to those who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore the whole assembly is assembled together and all speak with other languages, and unlearned or unbelieving people come in, won’t they say that you are crazy?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if all prophesy, and someone unbelieving or unlearned comes in, he is reproved by all, and he is judged by all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed. So he will fall down on his face and worship God, declaring that God is among you indeed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What is it then, brothers? When you come together, each one of you has a psalm, has a teaching, has a revelation, has another language, or has an interpretation. Let all things be done to build each other up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man speaks in another language, let it be two, or at the most three, and in turn; and let one interpret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the assembly, and let him speak to himself, and to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the prophets speak, two or three, and let the others discern.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if a revelation is made to another sitting by, let the first keep silent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you all can prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be exhorted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for God is not a God of confusion, but of peace, as in all the assemblies of the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the wives be quiet in the assemblies, for it has not been permitted for them to be talking except in submission, as the law also says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if they desire to learn anything. “Let them ask their own husbands at home, for it is shameful for a wife to be talking in the assembly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What!? Was it from you that the word of God went out? Or did it come to you alone?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man thinks himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him recognize the things which I write to you, that they are the commandment of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone is ignorant, let him be ignorant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, brothers, desire earnestly to prophesy, and don’t forbid speaking with other languages.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let all things be done decently and in order.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I declare to you, brothers, the Good News which I preached to you, which also you received, in which you also stand,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by which also you are saved, if you hold firmly the word which I preached to you—unless you believed in vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I delivered to you first of all that which I also received: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he appeared to over five hundred brothers at once, most of whom remain until now, but some have also fallen asleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and last of all, as to the child born at the wrong time, he appeared to me also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am the least of the apostles, who is not worthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the assembly of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was given to me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whether then it is I or they, so we preach, and so you believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now if Christ is preached, that he has been raised from the dead, how do some among you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, neither has Christ been raised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain, and your faith also is in vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, we are also found false witnesses of God, because we testified about God that he raised up Christ, whom he didn’t raise up, if it is so that the dead are not raised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the dead aren’t raised, neither has Christ been raised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If Christ has not been raised, your faith is vain; you are still in your sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then they also who are fallen asleep in Christ have perished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we have only hoped in Christ in this life, we are of all men most pitiable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now Christ has been raised from the dead. He became the first fruits of those who are asleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For since death came by man, the resurrection of the dead also came by man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all will be made alive.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But each in his own order: Christ the first fruits, then those who are Christ’s, at his coming.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the end comes, when he will deliver up the Kingdom to God, even the Father, when he will have abolished all rule and all authority and power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The last enemy that will be abolished is death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For, “He put all things in subjection under his feet.” But when he says, “All things are put in subjection”, it is evident that he is excepted who subjected all things to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When all things have been subjected to him, then the Son will also himself be subjected to him who subjected all things to him, that God may be all in all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or else what will they do who are baptized for the dead? If the dead aren’t raised at all, why then are they baptized for the dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I affirm, by the boasting in you which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I fought with animals at Ephesus for human purposes, what does it profit me? If the dead are not raised, then “let’s eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be deceived! “Evil companionships corrupt good morals.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wake up righteously, and don’t sin, for some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But someone will say, “How are the dead raised?” and, “With what kind of body do they come?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You foolish one, that which you yourself sow is not made alive unless it dies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which you sow, you don’t sow the body that will be, but a bare grain, maybe of wheat, or of some other kind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God gives it a body even as it pleased him, and to each seed a body of its own.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All flesh is not the same flesh, but there is one flesh of men, another flesh of animals, another of fish, and another of birds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There are also celestial bodies and terrestrial bodies; but the glory of the celestial differs from that of the terrestrial.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is one glory of the sun, another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for one star differs from another star in glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So also is the resurrection of the dead. The body is sown perishable; it is raised imperishable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. There is a natural body and there is also a spiritual body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So also it is written, “The first man, Adam, became a living soul.”  The last Adam became a life-giving spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However that which is spiritual isn’t first, but that which is natural, then that which is spiritual.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first man is of the earth, made of dust. The second man is the Lord from heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As is the one made of dust, such are those who are also made of dust; and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As we have borne the image of those made of dust, let’s also bear the image of the heavenly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I say this, brothers, that flesh and blood can’t inherit God’s Kingdom; neither does the perishable inherit imperishable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I tell you a mystery. We will not all sleep, but we will all be changed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound and the dead will be raised incorruptible, and we will be changed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this perishable body must become imperishable, and this mortal must put on immortality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when this perishable body will have become imperishable, and this mortal will have put on immortality, then what is written will happen: “Death is swallowed up in victory.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the Lord’s work, because you know that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I commanded the assemblies of Galatia, you do likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the first day of every week, let each one of you save, as he may prosper, that no collections are made when I come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I arrive, I will send whoever you approve with letters to carry your gracious gift to Jerusalem.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If it is appropriate for me to go also, they will go with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I will come to you when I have passed through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But with you it may be that I will stay, or even winter, that you may send me on my journey wherever I go.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I do not wish to see you now in passing, but I hope to stay a while with you, if the Lord permits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I will stay at Ephesus until Pentecost,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for a great and effective door has opened to me, and there are many adversaries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now if Timothy comes, see that he is with you without fear, for he does the work of the Lord, as I also do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let no one despise him. But set him forward on his journey in peace, that he may come to me; for I expect him with the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now concerning Apollos, the brother, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; and it was not at all his desire to come now; but he will come when he has an opportunity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch! Stand firm in the faith! Be courageous! Be strong!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let all that you do be done in love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I beg you, brothers—you know the house of Stephanas, that it is the first fruits of Achaia, and that they have set themselves to serve the saints—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you also be in subjection to such, and to everyone who helps in the work and labors.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas, Fortunatus, and Achaicus; for that which was lacking on your part, they supplied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they refreshed my spirit and yours. Therefore acknowledge those who are like that.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The assemblies of Asia greet you. Aquila and Priscilla greet you much in the Lord, together with the assembly that is in their house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the brothers greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This greeting is by me, Paul, with my own hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man doesn’t love the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be cursed.  Come, Lord!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My love to all of you in Christ Jesus. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, even so our comfort also abounds through Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Our hope for you is steadfast, knowing that, since you are partakers of the sufferings, so you are also of the comfort.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we don’t desire to have you uninformed, brothers, concerning our affliction which happened to us in Asia, that we were weighed down exceedingly, beyond our power, so much that we despaired even of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who delivered us out of so great a death, and does deliver; on whom we have set our hope that he will also still deliver us;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: you also helping together on our behalf by your supplication; that, for the gift given to us by means of many, thanks may be given by many persons on your behalf.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we write no other things to you than what you read or even acknowledge, and I hope you will acknowledge to the end,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as also you acknowledged us in part, that we are your boasting, even as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this confidence, I was determined to come first to you, that you might have a second benefit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and by you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be sent forward by you on my journey to Judea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I therefore was thus determined, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as God is faithful, our word toward you was not “Yes and no.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, by me, Silvanus, and Timothy, was not “Yes and no,” but in him is “Yes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For however many are the promises of God, in him is the “Yes.” Therefore also through him is the “Amen”, to the glory of God through us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he who establishes us with you in Christ and anointed us is God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who also sealed us, and gave us the down payment of the Spirit in our hearts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I call God for a witness to my soul, that I didn’t come to Corinth to spare you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We don’t control your faith, but are fellow workers with you for your joy. For you stand firm in faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I determined this for myself, that I would not come to you again in sorrow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I make you grieve, then who will make me glad but he who is made to grieve by me?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And I wrote this very thing to you, so that, when I came, I wouldn’t have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy would be shared by all of you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you with many tears, not that you should be made to grieve, but that you might know the love that I have so abundantly for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if any has caused sorrow, he has caused sorrow, not to me, but in part (that I not press too heavily) to you all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This punishment which was inflicted by the many is sufficient for such a one;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that on the contrary you should rather forgive him and comfort him, lest by any means such a one should be swallowed up with his excessive sorrow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I beg you to confirm your love toward him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to this end I also wrote, that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I also forgive whomever you forgive anything. For if indeed I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven that one for your sakes in the presence of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that no advantage may be gained over us by Satan, for we are not ignorant of his schemes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when I came to Troas for the Good News of Christ, and when a door was opened to me in the Lord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I had no relief for my spirit, because I didn’t find Titus, my brother, but taking my leave of them, I went out into Macedonia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are a sweet aroma of Christ to God, in those who are saved and in those who perish:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the one a stench from death to death, to the other a sweet aroma from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are not as so many, peddling the word of God. But as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God, we speak in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tablets of stone, but in tablets that are hearts of flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Such confidence we have through Christ toward God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not that we are sufficient of ourselves, to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant, not of the letter, but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if the service of death, written engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the children of Israel could not look steadfastly on the face of Moses for the glory of his face, which was passing away,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: won’t service of the Spirit be with much more glory?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the service of condemnation has glory, the service of righteousness exceeds much more in glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly that which has been made glorious has not been made glorious in this respect, by reason of the glory that surpasses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having therefore such a hope, we use great boldness of speech,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and not as Moses, who put a veil on his face, that the children of Israel wouldn’t look steadfastly on the end of that which was passing away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But their minds were hardened, for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains, because in Christ it passes away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But whenever someone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the Lord is the Spirit and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, even as we obtained mercy, we don’t faint.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we have renounced the hidden things of shame, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully, but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even if our Good News is veiled, it is veiled in those who are dying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, that the light of the Good News of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God, should not dawn on them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we don’t preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus’ sake,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing it is God who said, “Light will shine out of darkness,” who has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we have this treasure in clay vessels, that the exceeding greatness of the power may be of God, and not from ourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: pursued, yet not forsaken; struck down, yet not destroyed;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: always carrying in the body the putting to death of the Lord Jesus, that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we who live are always delivered to death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus may be revealed in our mortal flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then death works in us, but life in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But having the same spirit of faith, according to that which is written, “I believed, and therefore I spoke.” We also believe, and therefore we also speak;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus, and will present us with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For all things are for your sakes, that the grace, being multiplied through the many, may cause the thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: while we don’t look at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal, in the heavens.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly in this we groan, longing to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if indeed being clothed, we will not be found naked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For indeed we who are in this tent do groan, being burdened, not that we desire to be unclothed, but that we desire to be clothed, that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now he who made us for this very thing is God, who also gave to us the down payment of the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for we walk by faith, not by sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are courageous, I say, and are willing rather to be absent from the body and to be at home with the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or absent, to be well pleasing to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ that each one may receive the things in the body according to what he has done, whether good or bad.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Knowing therefore the fear of the Lord, we persuade men, but we are revealed to God, and I hope that we are revealed also in your consciences.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are not commending ourselves to you again, but speak as giving you occasion of boasting on our behalf, that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance, and not in heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God. Or if we are of sober mind, it is for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the love of Christ constrains us; because we judge thus, that one died for all, therefore all died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He died for all, that those who live should no longer live to themselves, but to him who for their sakes died and rose again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore we know no one after the flesh from now on. Even though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now we know him so no more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold, all things have become new.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But all things are of God, who reconciled us to himself through Jesus Christ, and gave to us the ministry of reconciliation;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: namely, that God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their trespasses, and having committed to us the word of reconciliation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are therefore ambassadors on behalf of Christ, as though God were entreating by us: we beg you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For him who knew no sin he made to be sin on our behalf; so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Working together, we entreat also that you do not receive the grace of God in vain,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in everything commending ourselves, as servants of God, in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in pureness, in knowledge, in perseverance, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in sincere love,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in the word of truth, in the power of God; by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report; as deceivers, and yet true;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and not killed;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in return, I speak as to my children: you also open your hearts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers, for what fellowship do righteousness and iniquity have? Or what fellowship does light have with darkness?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What agreement does Christ have with Belial? Or what portion does a believer have with an unbeliever?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What agreement does a temple of God have with idols? For you are a temple of the living God. Even as God said, “I will dwell in them and walk in them. I will be their God and they will be my people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: says the Lord Almighty.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having therefore these promises, beloved, let’s cleanse ourselves from all defilement of flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Open your hearts to us. We wronged no one. We corrupted no one. We took advantage of no one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I say this not to condemn you, for I have said before that you are in our hearts to die together and live together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great is my boldness of speech toward you. Great is my boasting on your behalf. I am filled with comfort. I overflow with joy in all our affliction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even when we had come into Macedonia, our flesh had no relief, but we were afflicted on every side. Fightings were outside. Fear was inside.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless, he who comforts the lowly, God, comforted us by the coming of Titus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and not by his coming only, but also by the comfort with which he was comforted in you while he told us of your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though I grieved you with my letter, I do not regret it, though I did regret it. For I see that my letter made you grieve, though just for a while.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I now rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved to repentance. For you were grieved in a godly way, that you might suffer loss by us in nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For godly sorrow produces repentance to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For behold, this same thing, that you were grieved in a godly way, what earnest care it worked in you. Yes, what defense, indignation, fear, longing, zeal, and vengeance! In everything you demonstrated yourselves to be pure in the matter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So although I wrote to you, I wrote not for his cause that did the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered the wrong, but that your earnest care for us might be revealed in you in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore we have been comforted. In our comfort we rejoiced the more exceedingly for the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if in anything I have boasted to him on your behalf, I was not disappointed. But as we spoke all things to you in truth, so our glorying also which I made before Titus was found to be truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His affection is more abundantly toward you, while he remembers all of your obedience, how with fear and trembling you received him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I rejoice that in everything I am confident concerning you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moreover, brothers, we make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the assemblies of Macedonia,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how in much proof of affliction, the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their generosity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For according to their power, I testify, yes and beyond their power, they gave of their own accord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: begging us with much entreaty to receive this grace and the fellowship in the service to the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This was not as we had expected, but first they gave their own selves to the Lord, and to us through the will of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So we urged Titus, that as he had made a beginning before, so he would also complete in you this grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as you abound in everything, in faith, utterance, knowledge, all earnestness, and in your love to us, see that you also abound in this grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I give a judgment in this: for this is expedient for you who were the first to start a year ago, not only to do, but also to be willing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now complete the doing also, that as there was the readiness to be willing, so there may be the completion also out of your ability.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is not that others may be eased and you distressed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but for equality. Your abundance at this present time supplies their lack, that their abundance also may become a supply for your lack; that there may be equality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But thanks be to God, who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he indeed accepted our exhortation, but being himself very earnest, he went out to you of his own accord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have sent together with him the brother whose praise in the Good News is known throughout all the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not only so, but he was also appointed by the assemblies to travel with us in this grace, which is served by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our readiness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are avoiding this, that any man should blame us concerning this abundance which is administered by us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having regard for honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have sent with them our brother, whom we have many times proved earnest in many things, but now much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence which he has in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for you. As for our brothers, they are the apostles of the assemblies, the glory of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore show the proof of your love to them before the assemblies, and of our boasting on your behalf.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is indeed unnecessary for me to write to you concerning the service to the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for I know your readiness, of which I boast on your behalf to those of Macedonia, that Achaia has been prepared for the past year. Your zeal has stirred up very many of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have sent the brothers that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: lest by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) would be disappointed in this confident boasting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thought it necessary therefore to entreat the brothers that they would go before to you and arrange ahead of time the generous gift that you promised before, that the same might be ready as a matter of generosity, and not of greediness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly. He who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart, not grudgingly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now may he who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food, supply and multiply your seed for sowing, and increase the fruits of your righteousness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: you being enriched in everything to all generosity, which produces thanksgiving to God through us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this service of giving that you perform not only makes up for lack among the saints, but abounds also through much giving of thanks to God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing that through the proof given by this service, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the Good News of Christ and for the generosity of your contribution to them and to all,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: while they themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, yearn for you by reason of the exceeding grace of God in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I Paul, myself, entreat you by the humility and gentleness of Christ, I who in your presence am lowly among you, but being absent am bold toward you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, I beg you that I may not, when present, show courage with the confidence with which I intend to be bold against some, who consider us to be walking according to the flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though we walk in the flesh, we don’t wage war according to the flesh;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh, but mighty before God to the throwing down of strongholds,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: throwing down imaginations and every high thing that is exalted against the knowledge of God and bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and being in readiness to avenge all disobedience when your obedience is made full.

sources.label: New Testament
