sources.text: Do you look at things only as they appear in front of your face? If anyone trusts in himself that he is Christ’s, let him consider this again with himself, that even as he is Christ’s, so we also are Christ’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even if I boast somewhat abundantly concerning our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up, and not for casting you down, I will not be ashamed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I may not seem as if I desire to terrify you by my letters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For, “His letters”, they say, “are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech is despised.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let such a person consider this, that what we are in word by letters when we are absent, such are we also in deed when we are present.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are not bold to number or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But they themselves, measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without understanding.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we will not boast beyond proper limits, but within the boundaries with which God appointed to us, which reach even to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we don’t stretch ourselves too much, as though we didn’t reach to you. For we came even as far as to you with the Good News of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not boasting beyond proper limits in other men’s labors, but having hope that as your faith grows, we will be abundantly enlarged by you in our sphere of influence,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so as to preach the Good News even to the parts beyond you, not to boast in what someone else has already done.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But “he who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it isn’t he who commends himself who is approved, but whom the Lord commends.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you do bear with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy. For I married you to one husband, that I might present you as a pure virgin to Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I am afraid that somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in his craftiness, so your minds might be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if he who comes preaches another Jesus, whom we didn’t preach, or if you receive a different spirit, which you didn’t receive, or a different “good news”, which you didn’t accept, you put up with that well enough.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But though I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not unskilled in knowledge. No, in every way we have been revealed to you in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached to you God’s Good News free of charge?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them that I might serve you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from those who desire an occasion, that in which they boast, they may be found even as we.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as Christ’s apostles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is no great thing therefore if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I say again, let no one think me foolish. But if so, yet receive me as foolish, that I also may boast a little.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which I speak, I don’t speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you bear with a man if he brings you into bondage, if he devours you, if he takes you captive, if he exalts himself, or if he strikes you on the face.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I speak by way of disparagement, as though we had been weak. Yet in whatever way anyone is bold (I speak in foolishness), I am bold also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the offspring of Abraham? So am I.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are they servants of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself.) I am more so: in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in stripes above measure, and in deaths often.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Five times I received forty stripes minus one from the Jews.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I suffered shipwreck. I have been a night and a day in the deep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have been in travels often, perils of rivers, perils of robbers, perils from my countrymen, perils from the Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brothers;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in labor and travail, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, and in cold and nakedness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Besides those things that are outside, there is that which presses on me daily: anxiety for all the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I don’t burn with indignation?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, he who is blessed forever more, knows that I don’t lie.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In Damascus the governor under King Aretas guarded the Damascenes’ city, desiring to arrest me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was let down in a basket through a window by the wall, and escaped his hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is doubtless not profitable for me to boast. For I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know a man in Christ, fourteen years ago (whether in the body, I don’t know, or whether out of the body, I don’t know; God knows), such a one caught up into the third heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know such a man (whether in the body, or outside of the body, I don’t know; God knows),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how he was caught up into Paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On behalf of such a one I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except in my weaknesses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I would desire to boast, I will not be foolish; for I will speak the truth. But I refrain, so that no man may think more of me than that which he sees in me or hears from me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By reason of the exceeding greatness of the revelations, that I should not be exalted excessively, a thorn in the flesh was given to me: a messenger of Satan to torment me, that I should not be exalted excessively.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Concerning this thing, I begged the Lord three times that it might depart from me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He has said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in injuries, in necessities, in persecutions, and in distresses, for Christ’s sake. For when I am weak, then am I strong.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have become foolish in boasting. You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you, for I am in no way inferior to the very best apostles, though I am nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Truly the signs of an apostle were worked among you in all perseverance, in signs and wonders and mighty works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what is there in which you were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies, unless it is that I myself was not a burden to you? Forgive me this wrong.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, this is the third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be a burden to you; for I seek not your possessions, but you. For the children ought not to save up for the parents, but the parents for the children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more abundantly, am I loved the less?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, I myself didn’t burden you. “But, being crafty, I caught you with deception.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Did I take advantage of you by anyone of those whom I have sent to you?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I exhorted Titus, and I sent the brother with him. Did Titus take any advantage of you? Didn’t we walk in the same spirit? Didn’t we walk in the same steps?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, do you think that we are excusing ourselves to you? In the sight of God we speak in Christ. But all things, beloved, are for your edifying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am afraid that by any means, when I come, I might find you not the way I want to, and that I might be found by you as you don’t desire, that by any means there would be strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, factions, slander, whisperings, proud thoughts, or riots,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that again when I come my God would humble me before you, and I would mourn for many of those who have sinned before now, and not repented of the uncleanness, sexual immorality, and lustfulness which they committed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the third time I am coming to you. “At the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have said beforehand, and I do say beforehand, as when I was present the second time, so now, being absent, I write to those who have sinned before now, and to all the rest, that, if I come again, I will not spare;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing that you seek a proof of Christ who speaks in me; who toward you is not weak, but is powerful in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he was crucified through weakness, yet he lives through the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we will live with him through the power of God toward you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith. Test your own selves. Or don’t you know about your own selves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I hope that you will know that we aren’t disqualified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I pray to God that you do no evil; not that we may appear approved, but that you may do that which is honorable, though we are as reprobate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. We also pray for this: your becoming perfect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause I write these things while absent, that I may not deal sharply when present, according to the authority which the Lord gave me for building up, and not for tearing down.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, brothers, rejoice! Be perfected. Be comforted. Be of the same mind. Live in peace, and the God of love and peace will be with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet one another with a holy kiss.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the saints greet you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, God’s love, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle—not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and all the brothers who are with me, to the assemblies of Galatia:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you and want to pervert the Good News of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any “good news” other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As we have said before, so I now say again: if any man preaches to you any “good news” other than that which you received, let him be cursed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I didn’t receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came to me through revelation of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you have heard of my way of living in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the assembly of God and ravaged it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when it was the good pleasure of God, who separated me from my mother’s womb and called me through his grace

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I didn’t immediately confer with flesh and blood,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia. Then I returned to Damascus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But of the other apostles I saw no one except James, the Lord’s brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now about the things which I write to you, behold, before God, I’m not lying.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was still unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea which were in Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but they only heard: “He who once persecuted us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So they glorified God in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then after a period of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I went up by revelation, and I laid before them the Good News which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately before those who were respected, for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who stole in to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom we gave no place in the way of subjection, not for an hour, that the truth of the Good News might continue with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But from those who were reputed to be important—whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God doesn’t show partiality to man—they, I say, who were respected imparted nothing to me,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcised, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcised—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he who worked through Peter in the apostleship with the circumcised also worked through me with the Gentiles—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and when they perceived the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, those who were reputed to be pillars, gave to Barnabas and me the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They only asked us to remember the poor—which very thing I was also zealous to do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face, because he stood condemned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For before some people came from James, he ate with the Gentiles. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those who were of the circumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And the rest of the Jews joined him in his hypocrisy, so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when I saw that they didn’t walk uprightly according to the truth of the Good News, I said to Peter before them all, “If you, being a Jew, live as the Gentiles do, and not as the Jews do, why do you compel the Gentiles to live as the Jews do?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “We, being Jews by nature, and not Gentile sinners,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ, and not by the works of the law, because no flesh will be justified by the works of the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if while we sought to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found sinners, is Christ a servant of sin? Certainly not!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if I build up again those things which I destroyed, I prove myself a law-breaker.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I, through the law, died to the law, that I might live to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I don’t reject the grace of God. For if righteousness is through the law, then Christ died for nothing!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I just want to learn this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now completed in the flesh?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Did you suffer so many things in vain, if it is indeed in vain?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him for righteousness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Know therefore that those who are of faith are children of Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, those who are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who doesn’t continue in all things that are written in the book of the law, to do them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now that no man is justified by the law before God is evident, for, “The righteous will live by faith.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The law is not of faith, but, “The man who does them will live by them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, speaking of human terms, though it is only a man’s covenant, yet when it has been confirmed, no one makes it void or adds to it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring. He doesn’t say, “To descendants”, as of many, but as of one, “To your offspring”, which is Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I say this: A covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law, which came four hundred thirty years after, does not annul, so as to make the promise of no effect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then why is there the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise has been made. It was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is the law then against the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could make alive, most certainly righteousness would have been of the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Scripture imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But before faith came, we were kept in custody under the law, confined for the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a tutor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you are Christ’s, then you are Abraham’s offspring and heirs according to promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba, Father!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You observe days, months, seasons, and years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but you know that because of weakness in the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is written,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I, Paul, tell you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will profit you nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, I testify again to every man who receives circumcision that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are alienated from Christ, you who desire to be justified by the law. You have fallen away from grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we, through the Spirit, by faith wait for the hope of righteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision amounts to anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith working through love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A little yeast grows through the whole lump.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have confidence toward you in the Lord that you will think no other way. But he who troubles you will bear his judgment, whoever he is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been removed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I wish that those who disturb you would cut themselves off.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you, brothers, were called for freedom. Only don’t use your freedom for gain to the flesh, but through love be servants to one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you bite and devour one another, be careful that you don’t consume one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the deeds of the flesh are obvious, which are: adultery, sexual immorality, uncleanness, lustfulness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousies, outbursts of anger, rivalries, divisions, heresies,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: envy, murders, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these; of which I forewarn you, even as I also forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit God’s Kingdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and lusts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s not become conceited, provoking one another, and envying one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual must restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness; looking to yourself so that you also aren’t tempted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if a man thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For each man will bear his own burden.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let him who is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, as we have opportunity, let’s do what is good toward all men, and especially toward those who are of the household of the faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See with what large letters I write to you with my own hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As many as desire to make a good impression in the flesh compel you to be circumcised; just so they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, that they may boast in your flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From now on, let no one cause me any trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, to the saints who are at Ephesus, and the faithful in Christ Jesus:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places in Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and without defect before him in love,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the praise of the glory of his grace, by which he freely gave us favor in the Beloved,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom we have our redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of his grace,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which he made to abound toward us in all wisdom and prudence,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure which he purposed in him

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to an administration of the fullness of the times, to sum up all things in Christ, the things in the heavens and the things on the earth, in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We were also assigned an inheritance in him, having been foreordained according to the purpose of him who does all things after the counsel of his will,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the end that we should be to the praise of his glory, we who had before hoped in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In him you also, having heard the word of the truth, the Good News of your salvation—in whom, having also believed, you were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who is a pledge of our inheritance, to the redemption of God’s own possession, to the praise of his glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause I also, having heard of the faith in the Lord Jesus which is among you, and the love which you have toward all the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: don’t cease to give thanks for you, making mention of you in my prayers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give to you a spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope of his calling, and what are the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and what is the exceeding greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to that working of the strength of his might

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which he worked in Christ, when he raised him from the dead and made him to sit at his right hand in the heavenly places,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: far above all rule, authority, power, dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this age, but also in that which is to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He put all things in subjection under his feet, and gave him to be head over all things for the assembly,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You were made alive when you were dead in transgressions and sins,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in which you once walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air, the spirit who now works in the children of disobedience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We also all once lived among them in the lusts of our flesh, doing the desires of the flesh and of the mind, and were by nature children of wrath, even as the rest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God, being rich in mercy, for his great love with which he loved us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even when we were dead through our trespasses, made us alive together with Christ—by grace you have been saved—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and raised us up with him, and made us to sit with him in the heavenly places in Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that in the ages to come he might show the exceeding riches of his grace in kindness toward us in Christ Jesus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for by grace you have been saved through faith, and that not of yourselves; it is the gift of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not of works, that no one would boast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared before that we would walk in them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore remember that once you, the Gentiles in the flesh, who are called “uncircumcision” by that which is called “circumcision” (in the flesh, made by hands),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you were at that time separate from Christ, alienated from the commonwealth of Israel, and strangers from the covenants of the promise, having no hope and without God in the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far off are made near in the blood of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he is our peace, who made both one, and broke down the middle wall of separation,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having abolished in his flesh the hostility, the law of commandments contained in ordinances, that he might create in himself one new man of the two, making peace,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and might reconcile them both in one body to God through the cross, having killed the hostility through it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He came and preached peace to you who were far off and to those who were near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For through him we both have our access in one Spirit to the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then you are no longer strangers and foreigners, but you are fellow citizens with the saints and of the household of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, Christ Jesus himself being the chief cornerstone;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom the whole building, fitted together, grows into a holy temple in the Lord;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom you also are built together for a habitation of God in the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause I, Paul, am the prisoner of Christ Jesus on behalf of you Gentiles,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if it is so that you have heard of the administration of that grace of God which was given me toward you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how that by revelation the mystery was made known to me, as I wrote before in few words,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by which, when you read, you can perceive my understanding in the mystery of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which in other generations was not made known to the children of men, as it has now been revealed to his holy apostles and prophets in the Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the Gentiles are fellow heirs and fellow members of the body, and fellow partakers of his promise in Christ Jesus through the Good News,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of which I was made a servant according to the gift of that grace of God which was given me according to the working of his power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To me, the very least of all saints, was this grace given, to preach to the Gentiles the unsearchable riches of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to make all men see what is the administration of the mystery which for ages has been hidden in God, who created all things through Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the intent that now through the assembly the manifold wisdom of God might be made known to the principalities and the powers in the heavenly places,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: according to the eternal purpose which he accomplished in Christ Jesus our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In him we have boldness and access in confidence through our faith in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I ask that you may not lose heart at my troubles for you, which are your glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause, I bow my knees to the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that he would grant you, according to the riches of his glory, that you may be strengthened with power through his Spirit in the inner person,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that Christ may dwell in your hearts through faith, to the end that you, being rooted and grounded in love,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: may be strengthened to comprehend with all the saints what is the width and length and height and depth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to know Christ’s love which surpasses knowledge, that you may be filled with all the fullness of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to him who is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that works in us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to him be the glory in the assembly and in Christ Jesus to all generations forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I therefore, the prisoner in the Lord, beg you to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being eager to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is one body and one Spirit, even as you also were called in one hope of your calling,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: one God and Father of all, who is over all and through all, and in us all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to each one of us, the grace was given according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He gave some to be apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, shepherds and teachers;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the perfecting of the saints, to the work of serving, to the building up of the body of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a full grown man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that we may no longer be children, tossed back and forth and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in craftiness, after the wiles of error;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but speaking truth in love, we may grow up in all things into him who is the head, Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from whom all the body, being fitted and knit together through that which every joint supplies, according to the working in measure of each individual part, makes the body increase to the building up of itself in love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardening of their hearts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They, having become callous, gave themselves up to lust, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you didn’t learn Christ that way,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if indeed you heard him, and were taught in him, even as truth is in Jesus:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you put away, as concerning your former way of life, the old man that grows corrupt after the lusts of deceit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and put on the new man, who in the likeness of God has been created in righteousness and holiness of truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore putting away falsehood, speak truth each one with his neighbor. For we are members of one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Be angry, and don’t sin.” Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and don’t give place to the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let him who stole steal no more; but rather let him labor, producing with his hands something that is good, that he may have something to give to him who has need.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander be put away from you, with all malice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be therefore imitators of God, as beloved children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Walk in love, even as Christ also loved us and gave himself up for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweet-smelling fragrance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But sexual immorality, and all uncleanness or covetousness, let it not even be mentioned among you, as becomes saints;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor filthiness, nor foolish talking, nor jesting, which are not appropriate, but rather giving of thanks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Know this for sure, that no sexually immoral person, nor unclean person, nor covetous man, who is an idolater, has any inheritance in the Kingdom of Christ and God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one deceive you with empty words. For because of these things, the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be partakers with them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you were once darkness, but are now light in the Lord. Walk as children of light,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the fruit of the Spirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: proving what is well pleasing to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Have no fellowship with the unfruitful deeds of darkness, but rather even reprove them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is a shame even to speak of the things which are done by them in secret.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But all things, when they are reproved, are revealed by the light, for everything that reveals is light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he says, “Awake, you who sleep, and arise from the dead, and Christ will shine on you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore watch carefully how you walk, not as unwise, but as wise,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: redeeming the time, because the days are evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be foolish, but understand what the will of the Lord is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be drunken with wine, in which is dissipation, but be filled with the Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: speaking to one another in psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs; singing and making melody in your heart to the Lord;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: giving thanks always concerning all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to God, even the Father;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: subjecting yourselves to one another in the fear of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wives, be subject to your own husbands, as to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the husband is the head of the wife, as Christ also is the head of the assembly, being himself the savior of the body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But as the assembly is subject to Christ, so let the wives also be to their own husbands in everything.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Husbands, love your wives, even as Christ also loved the assembly, and gave himself up for it;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that he might sanctify it, having cleansed it by the washing of water with the word,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that he might present the assembly to himself gloriously, not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing; but that it should be holy and without defect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so husbands also ought to love their own wives as their own bodies. He who loves his own wife loves himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For no man ever hated his own flesh; but nourishes and cherishes it, even as the Lord also does the assembly;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because we are members of his body, of his flesh and bones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will be joined to his wife. The two will become one flesh.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This mystery is great, but I speak concerning Christ and of the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless each of you must also love his own wife even as himself; and let the wife see that she respects her husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with a promise:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You fathers, don’t provoke your children to wrath, but nurture them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Servants, be obedient to those who according to the flesh are your masters, with fear and trembling, in singleness of your heart, as to Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not in the way of service only when eyes are on you, as men pleasers, but as servants of Christ, doing the will of God from the heart,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: with good will doing service as to the Lord, and not to men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that whatever good thing each one does, he will receive the same good again from the Lord, whether he is bound or free.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You masters, do the same things to them, and give up threatening, knowing that he who is both their Master and yours is in heaven, and there is no partiality with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, be strong in the Lord, and in the strength of his might.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For our wrestling is not against flesh and blood, but against the principalities, against the powers, against the world’s rulers of the darkness of this age, and against the spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Stand therefore, having the utility belt of truth buckled around your waist, and having put on the breastplate of righteousness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and having fitted your feet with the preparation of the Good News of peace,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: above all, taking up the shield of faith, with which you will be able to quench all the fiery darts of the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: with all prayer and requests, praying at all times in the Spirit, and being watchful to this end in all perseverance and requests for all the saints:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: on my behalf, that utterance may be given to me in opening my mouth, to make known with boldness the mystery of the Good News,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for which I am an ambassador in chains; that in it I may speak boldly, as I ought to speak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But that you also may know my affairs, how I am doing, Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make known to you all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have sent him to you for this very purpose, that you may know our state and that he may comfort your hearts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peace be to the brothers, and love with faith, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace be with all those who love our Lord Jesus Christ with incorruptible love. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are at Philippi, with the overseers  and servants:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you, and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank my God whenever I remember you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: always in every request of mine on behalf of you all, making my requests with joy,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for your partnership in furtherance of the Good News from the first day until now;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is even right for me to think this way on behalf of all of you, because I have you in my heart, because both in my bonds and in the defense and confirmation of the Good News, you all are partakers with me of grace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God is my witness, how I long after all of you in the tender mercies of Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and all discernment,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that you may approve the things that are excellent, that you may be sincere and without offense to the day of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I desire to have you know, brothers, that the things which happened to me have turned out rather to the progress of the Good News,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that it became evident to the whole palace guard, and to all the rest, that my bonds are in Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that most of the brothers in the Lord, being confident through my bonds, are more abundantly bold to speak the word of God without fear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some indeed preach Christ even out of envy and strife, and some also out of good will.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The former insincerely preach Christ from selfish ambition, thinking that they add affliction to my chains;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but the latter out of love, knowing that I am appointed for the defense of the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What does it matter? Only that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is proclaimed. I rejoice in this, yes, and will rejoice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I know that this will turn out to my salvation, through your prayers and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: according to my earnest expectation and hope, that I will in no way be disappointed, but with all boldness, as always, now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if I live on in the flesh, this will bring fruit from my work; yet I don’t know what I will choose.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I am hard pressed between the two, having the desire to depart and be with Christ, which is far better.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet to remain in the flesh is more needful for your sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having this confidence, I know that I will remain, yes, and remain with you all for your progress and joy in the faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that your rejoicing may abound in Christ Jesus in me through my presence with you again.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Only let your way of life be worthy of the Good News of Christ, that whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear of your state, that you stand firm in one spirit, with one soul striving for the faith of the Good News;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in nothing frightened by the adversaries, which is for them a proof of destruction, but to you of salvation, and that from God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because it has been granted to you on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in him, but also to suffer on his behalf,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having the same conflict which you saw in me and now hear is in me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If therefore there is any exhortation in Christ, if any consolation of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any tender mercies and compassion,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: make my joy full by being like-minded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Have this in your mind, which was also in Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who, existing in the form of God, didn’t consider equality with God a thing to be grasped,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but emptied himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to the point of death, yes, the death of the cross.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, those on earth, and those under the earth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, my beloved, even as you have always obeyed, not only in my presence, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is God who works in you both to will and to work, for his good pleasure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Do all things without complaining and arguing,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may become blameless and harmless, children of God without defect in the middle of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you are seen as lights in the world,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: holding up the word of life, that I may have something to boast in the day of Christ, that I didn’t run in vain nor labor in vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, and if I am poured out on the sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice, and rejoice with you all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, you also rejoice, and rejoice with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, that I also may be cheered up when I know how you are doing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I have no one else like-minded, who will truly care about you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they all seek their own, not the things of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you know the proof of him, that as a child serves a father, so he served with me in furtherance of the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I hope to send him at once, as soon as I see how it will go with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I trust in the Lord that I myself also will come shortly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I counted it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, fellow worker, fellow soldier, and your apostle and servant of my need,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: since he longed for you all, and was very troubled because you had heard that he was sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For indeed he was sick, nearly to death, but God had mercy on him, and not on him only, but on me also, that I might not have sorrow on sorrow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have sent him therefore the more diligently, that when you see him again, you may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such people in honor,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because for the work of Christ he came near to death, risking his life to supply that which was lacking in your service toward me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord! To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not tiresome, but for you it is safe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beware of the dogs; beware of the evil workers; beware of the false circumcision.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we are the circumcision, who worship God in the Spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: though I myself might have confidence even in the flesh. If any other man thinks that he has confidence in the flesh, I yet more:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; concerning the law, a Pharisee;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: concerning zeal, persecuting the assembly; concerning the righteousness which is in the law, found blameless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, I consider those things that were gain to me as a loss for Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes most certainly, and I count all things to be a loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus, my Lord, for whom I suffered the loss of all things, and count them nothing but refuse, that I may gain Christ

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own, that which is of the law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is from God by faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed to his death,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if by any means I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not that I have already obtained, or am already made perfect; but I press on, that I may take hold of that for which also I was taken hold of by Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do: forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let us therefore, as many as are perfect, think this way. If in anything you think otherwise, God will also reveal that to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless, to the extent that we have already attained, let’s walk by the same rule. Let’s be of the same mind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, be imitators together of me, and note those who walk this way, even as you have us for an example.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many walk, of whom I told you often, and now tell you even weeping, as the enemies of the cross of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whose end is destruction, whose god is the belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who think about earthly things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For our citizenship is in heaven, from where we also wait for a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who will change the body of our humiliation to be conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, my brothers, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord in this way, my beloved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I exhort Euodia, and I exhort Syntyche, to think the same way in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, I beg you also, true partner, help these women, for they labored with me in the Good News with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow workers, whose names are in the book of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let your gentleness be known to all men. The Lord is at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, brothers, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are of good report: if there is any virtue and if there is any praise, think about these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The things which you learned, received, heard, and saw in me: do these things, and the God of peace will be with you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I rejoice in the Lord greatly, that now at length you have revived your thought for me; in which you did indeed take thought, but you lacked opportunity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not that I speak because of lack, for I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I know how to be humbled, and I also know how to abound. In everything and in all things I have learned the secret both to be filled and to be hungry, both to abound and to be in need.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However you did well that you shared in my affliction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You yourselves also know, you Philippians, that in the beginning of the Good News, when I departed from Macedonia, no assembly shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving but you only.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even in Thessalonica you sent once and again to my need.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Not that I seek for the gift, but I seek for the fruit that increases to your account.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have all things and abound. I am filled, having received from Epaphroditus the things that came from you, a sweet-smelling fragrance, an acceptable and well-pleasing sacrifice to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to our God and Father be the glory forever and ever! Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the saints greet you, especially those who are of Caesar’s household.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love which you have toward all the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens, of which you heard before in the word of the truth of the Good News

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which has come to you, even as it is in all the world and is bearing fruit and growing, as it does in you also, since the day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even as you learned of Epaphras our beloved fellow servant, who is a faithful servant of Christ on your behalf,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who also declared to us your love in the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause, we also, since the day we heard this, don’t cease praying and making requests for you, that you may be filled with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may walk worthily of the Lord, to please him in all respects, bearing fruit in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: strengthened with all power, according to the might of his glory, for all endurance and perseverance with joy,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: giving thanks to the Father, who made us fit to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who delivered us out of the power of darkness, and translated us into the Kingdom of the Son of his love,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom we have our redemption, the forgiveness of our sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For by him all things were created in the heavens and on the earth, visible things and invisible things, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers. All things have been created through him and for him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is before all things, and in him all things are held together.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is the head of the body, the assembly, who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in all things he might have the preeminence.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For all the fullness was pleased to dwell in him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and through him to reconcile all things to himself by him, whether things on the earth or things in the heavens, having made peace through the blood of his cross.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You, being in past times alienated and enemies in your mind in your evil deeds,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet now he has reconciled in the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and without defect and blameless before him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if it is so that you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and not moved away from the hope of the Good News which you heard, which is being proclaimed in all creation under heaven, of which I, Paul, was made a servant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake, and fill up on my part that which is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body’s sake, which is the assembly,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of which I was made a servant according to the stewardship of God which was given me toward you to fulfill the word of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the mystery which has been hidden for ages and generations. But now it has been revealed to his saints,

sources.label: New Testament
