sources.text: to whom God was pleased to make known what are the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We proclaim him, admonishing every man and teaching every man in all wisdom, that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for which I also labor, striving according to his working, which works in me mightily.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I desire to have you know how greatly I struggle for you, and for those at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that their hearts may be comforted, they being knit together in love, and gaining all riches of the full assurance of understanding, that they may know the mystery of God, both of the Father and of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I say this that no one may delude you with persuasiveness of speech.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For though I am absent in the flesh, yet I am with you in the spirit, rejoicing and seeing your order, and the steadfastness of your faith in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As therefore you received Christ Jesus, the Lord, walk in him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: rooted and built up in him, and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be careful that you don’t let anyone rob you through his philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the elements of the world, and not after Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in him all the fullness of the Deity dwells bodily,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in him you are made full, who is the head of all principality and power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In him you were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands, in the putting off of the body of the sins of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having been buried with him in baptism, in which you were also raised with him through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You were dead through your trespasses and the uncircumcision of your flesh. He made you alive together with him, having forgiven us all our trespasses,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: wiping out the handwriting in ordinances which was against us. He has taken it out of the way, nailing it to the cross.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having stripped the principalities and the powers, he made a show of them openly, triumphing over them in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one therefore judge you in eating, or in drinking, or with respect to a feast day or a new moon or a Sabbath day,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which are a shadow of the things to come; but the body is Christ’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one rob you of your prize by self-abasement and worshiping of the angels, dwelling in the things which he has not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and not holding firmly to the Head, from whom all the body, being supplied and knit together through the joints and ligaments, grows with God’s growth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you died with Christ from the elements of the world, why, as though living in the world, do you subject yourselves to ordinances,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Don’t handle, nor taste, nor touch”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (all of which perish with use), according to the precepts and doctrines of men?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things indeed appear like wisdom in self-imposed worship, humility, and severity to the body; but aren’t of any value against the indulgence of the flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then you were raised together with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated on the right hand of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you died, and your life is hidden with Christ in God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When Christ, our life, is revealed, then you will also be revealed with him in glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Put to death therefore your members which are on the earth: sexual immorality, uncleanness, depraved passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For these things’ sake the wrath of God comes on the children of disobedience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You also once walked in those, when you lived in them;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but now you also put them all away: anger, wrath, malice, slander, and shameful speaking out of your mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t lie to one another, seeing that you have put off the old man with his doings,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and have put on the new man, who is being renewed in knowledge after the image of his Creator,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: where there can’t be Greek and Jew, circumcision and uncircumcision, barbarian, Scythian, bondservant, or free person; but Christ is all, and in all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to which also you were called in one body, and be thankful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your heart to the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wives, be in subjection to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Husbands, love your wives, and don’t be bitter against them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Servants, obey in all things those who are your masters according to the flesh, not just when they are looking, as men pleasers, but in singleness of heart, fearing God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord, and not for men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that from the Lord you will receive the reward of the inheritance; for you serve the Lord Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who does wrong will receive again for the wrong that he has done, and there is no partiality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Masters, give to your servants that which is just and equal, knowing that you also have a Master in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Continue steadfastly in prayer, watching in it with thanksgiving,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: praying together for us also, that God may open to us a door for the word, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that I may reveal it as I ought to speak.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Walk in wisdom toward those who are outside, redeeming the time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All my affairs will be made known to you by Tychicus, the beloved brother, faithful servant, and fellow bondservant in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am sending him to you for this very purpose, that he may know your circumstances and comfort your hearts,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: together with Onesimus, the faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They will make known to you everything that is going on here.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, greets you, and Mark, the cousin of Barnabas (concerning whom you received commandments, “if he comes to you, receive him”),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and Jesus who is called Justus. These are my only fellow workers for God’s Kingdom who are of the circumcision, men who have been a comfort to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, salutes you, always striving for you in his prayers, that you may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I testify about him that he has great zeal for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for those in Hierapolis.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Luke the beloved physician and Demas greet you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet the brothers who are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the assembly that is in his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When this letter has been read among you, cause it to be read also in the assembly of the Laodiceans, and that you also read the letter from Laodicea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Tell Archippus, “Take heed to the ministry which you have received in the Lord, that you fulfill it.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The salutation of me, Paul, with my own hand: remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We always give thanks to God for all of you, mentioning you in our prayers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and perseverance of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ, before our God and Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know, brothers loved by God, that you are chosen,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that our Good News came to you not in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit, and with much assurance. You know what kind of men we showed ourselves to be among you for your sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You became imitators of us and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with joy of the Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that you became an example to all who believe in Macedonia and in Achaia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For from you the word of the Lord has been declared, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place your faith toward God has gone out, so that we need not to say anything.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of a reception we had from you, and how you turned to God from idols, to serve a living and true God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead: Jesus, who delivers us from the wrath to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you yourselves know, brothers, our visit to you wasn’t in vain,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but having suffered before and been shamefully treated, as you know, at Philippi, we grew bold in our God to tell you the Good News of God in much conflict.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For our exhortation is not of error, nor of uncleanness, nor in deception.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But even as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the Good News, so we speak: not as pleasing men, but God, who tests our hearts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For neither were we at any time found using words of flattery, as you know, nor a cloak of covetousness (God is witness),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor seeking glory from men (neither from you nor from others), when we might have claimed authority as apostles of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother cherishes her own children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you, not the Good News of God only, but also our own souls, because you had become very dear to us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you remember, brothers, our labor and travail; for working night and day, that we might not burden any of you, we preached to you the Good News of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are witnesses with God how holy, righteously, and blamelessly we behaved ourselves toward you who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As you know, we exhorted, comforted, and implored every one of you, as a father does his own children,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the end that you should walk worthily of God, who calls you into his own Kingdom and glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause we also thank God without ceasing, that when you received from us the word of the message of God, you accepted it not as the word of men, but as it is in truth, the word of God, which also works in you who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you, brothers, became imitators of the assemblies of God which are in Judea in Christ Jesus; for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, even as they did from the Jews

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and drove us out, and don’t please God, and are contrary to all men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved, to fill up their sins always. But wrath has come on them to the uttermost.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we, brothers, being bereaved of you for a short season, in presence, not in heart, tried even harder to see your face with great desire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because we wanted to come to you—indeed, I, Paul, once and again—but Satan hindered us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Isn’t it even you, before our Lord Jesus at his coming?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you are our glory and our joy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when we couldn’t stand it any longer, we thought it good to be left behind at Athens alone,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and sent Timothy, our brother and God’s servant in the Good News of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that no one would be moved by these afflictions. For you know that we are appointed to this task.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly, when we were with you, we told you beforehand that we are to suffer affliction, even as it happened, and you know.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause I also, when I couldn’t stand it any longer, sent that I might know your faith, for fear that by any means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor would have been in vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when Timothy came just now to us from you, and brought us glad news of your faith and love, and that you have good memories of us always, longing to see us, even as we also long to see you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for this cause, brothers, we were comforted over you in all our distress and affliction through your faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what thanksgiving can we render again to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sakes before our God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face, and may perfect that which is lacking in your faith?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now may our God and Father himself, and our Lord Jesus Christ, direct our way to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May the Lord make you to increase and abound in love toward one another, and toward all men, even as we also do toward you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the end he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally then, brothers, we beg and exhort you in the Lord Jesus, that as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God, that you abound more and more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is the will of God: your sanctification, that you abstain from sexual immorality,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that each one of you know how to control his own body in sanctification and honor,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not in the passion of lust, even as the Gentiles who don’t know God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that no one should take advantage of and wrong a brother or sister in this matter; because the Lord is an avenger in all these things, as also we forewarned you and testified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God called us not for uncleanness, but in sanctification.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he who rejects this doesn’t reject man, but God, who has also given his Holy Spirit to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But concerning brotherly love, you have no need that one write to you. For you yourselves are taught by God to love one another,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for indeed you do it toward all the brothers who are in all Macedonia. But we exhort you, brothers, that you abound more and more;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that you make it your ambition to lead a quiet life, and to do your own business, and to work with your own hands, even as we instructed you;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and may have need of nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we don’t want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you don’t grieve like the rest, who have no hope.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this we tell you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive, who are left until the coming of the Lord, will in no way precede those who have fallen asleep.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of the archangel and with God’s trumpet. The dead in Christ will rise first,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air. So we will be with the Lord forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore comfort one another with these words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers, you have no need that anything be written to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you yourselves know well that the day of the Lord comes like a thief in the night.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when they are saying, “Peace and safety,” then sudden destruction will come on them, like birth pains on a pregnant woman. Then they will in no way escape.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, brothers, aren’t in darkness, that the day should overtake you like a thief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are all children of light and children of the day. We don’t belong to the night, nor to darkness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so then let’s not sleep, as the rest do, but let’s watch and be sober.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who sleep, sleep in the night; and those who are drunk are drunk in the night.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But since we belong to the day, let’s be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet, the hope of salvation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God didn’t appoint us to wrath, but to the obtaining of salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who died for us, that, whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore exhort one another, and build each other up, even as you also do.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we beg you, brothers, to know those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to respect and honor them in love for their work’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be at peace among yourselves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We exhort you, brothers: Admonish the disorderly; encourage the faint-hearted; support the weak; be patient toward all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See that no one returns evil for evil to anyone, but always follow after that which is good for one another and for all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Always rejoice.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray without ceasing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t quench the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t despise prophecies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Test all things, and hold firmly that which is good.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Abstain from every form of evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May the God of peace himself sanctify you completely. May your whole spirit, soul, and body be preserved blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who calls you is faithful, who will also do it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, pray for us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet all the brothers with a holy kiss.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I solemnly command you by the Lord that this letter be read to all the holy brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the assembly of the Thessalonians in God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers, even as it is appropriate, because your faith grows exceedingly, and the love of each and every one of you toward one another abounds,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so that we ourselves boast about you in the assemblies of God for your perseverance and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions which you endure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is an obvious sign of the righteous judgment of God, to the end that you may be counted worthy of God’s Kingdom, for which you also suffer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Since it is a righteous thing with God to repay affliction to those who afflict you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and to give relief to you who are afflicted with us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: punishing those who don’t know God, and to those who don’t obey the Good News of our Lord Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who will pay the penalty: eternal destruction from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his might,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: when he comes in that day to be glorified in his saints and to be admired among all those who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To this end we also pray always for you, that our God may count you worthy of your calling, and fulfill every desire of goodness and work of faith with power,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, brothers, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to him, we ask you

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not to be quickly shaken in your mind, and not be troubled, either by spirit, or by word, or by letter as if from us, saying that the day of Christ has already come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one deceive you in any way. For it will not be, unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of sin is revealed, the son of destruction,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he who opposes and exalts himself against all that is called God or that is worshiped, so that he sits as God in the temple of God, setting himself up as God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t you remember that, when I was still with you, I told you these things?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now you know what is restraining him, to the end that he may be revealed in his own season.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the mystery of lawlessness already works. Only there is one who restrains now, until he is taken out of the way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord will kill with the breath of his mouth, and destroy by the manifestation of his coming;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: even he whose coming is according to the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and with all deception of wickedness for those who are being lost, because they didn’t receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because of this, God sends them a working of error, that they should believe a lie;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that they all might be judged who didn’t believe the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we are bound to always give thanks to God for you, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you from the beginning for salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief in the truth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to which he called you through our Good News, for the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So then, brothers, stand firm and hold the traditions which you were taught by us, whether by word or by letter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and gave us eternal comfort and good hope through grace,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good work and word.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, brothers, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified, even as also with you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men; for not all have faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you and guard you from the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you both do and will do the things we command.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May the Lord direct your hearts into God’s love, and into the perseverance of Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now we command you, brothers, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother who walks in rebellion, and not after the tradition which they received from us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you know how you ought to imitate us. For we didn’t behave ourselves rebelliously among you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: neither did we eat bread from anyone’s hand without paying for it, but in labor and travail worked night and day, that we might not burden any of you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not because we don’t have the right, but to make ourselves an example to you, that you should imitate us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: “If anyone is not willing to work, don’t let him eat.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we hear of some who walk among you in rebellion, who don’t work at all, but are busybodies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now those who are that way, we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ, that they work with quietness and eat their own bread.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, brothers, don’t be weary in doing what is right.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man doesn’t obey our word in this letter, note that man, that you have no company with him, to the end that he may be ashamed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The greeting of me, Paul, with my own hand, which is the sign in every letter: this is how I write.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ according to the commandment of God our Savior and the Lord Jesus Christ our hope;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to Timothy, my true child in faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As I urged you when I was going into Macedonia, stay at Ephesus that you might command certain men not to teach a different doctrine,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and not to pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which cause disputes, rather than God’s stewardship, which is in faith—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but the goal of this command is love, out of a pure heart and a good conscience and sincere faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from which things some, having missed the mark, have turned away to vain talking,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: desiring to be teachers of the law, though they understand neither what they say, nor about what they strongly affirm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we know that the law is good, if a person uses it lawfully,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as knowing this, that law is not made for a righteous person, but for the lawless and insubordinate, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for slave-traders, for liars, for perjurers, and for any other thing contrary to the sound doctrine,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: according to the Good News of the glory of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank him who enabled me, Christ Jesus our Lord, because he counted me faithful, appointing me to service;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: although I used to be a blasphemer, a persecutor, and insolent. However, I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord abounded exceedingly with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am chief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first, Jesus Christ might display all his patience for an example of those who were going to believe in him for eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, to God who alone is wise, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I commit this instruction to you, my child Timothy, according to the prophecies which were given to you before, that by them you may wage the good warfare,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: holding faith and a good conscience, which some having thrust away made a shipwreck concerning the faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I delivered to Satan, that they might be taught not to blaspheme.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I exhort therefore, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercessions, and givings of thanks be made for all men:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for kings and all who are in high places, that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and reverence.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Savior,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who desires all people to be saved and come to full knowledge of the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who gave himself as a ransom for all, the testimony in its own times,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am telling the truth in Christ, not lying—a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I desire therefore that the men in every place pray, lifting up holy hands without anger and doubting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, that women also adorn themselves in decent clothing, with modesty and propriety, not just with braided hair, gold, pearls, or expensive clothing,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but with good works, which is appropriate for women professing godliness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let a woman learn in quietness with full submission.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I don’t permit a woman to teach, nor to exercise authority over a man, but to be in quietness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Adam wasn’t deceived, but the woman, being deceived, has fallen into disobedience;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but she will be saved through her childbearing, if they continue in faith, love, and sanctification with sobriety.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is a faithful saying: someone who seeks to be an overseer desires a good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The overseer therefore must be without reproach, the husband of one wife, temperate, sensible, modest, hospitable, good at teaching;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not a drinker, not violent, not greedy for money, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not covetous;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: one who rules his own house well, having children in subjection with all reverence;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (but how could someone who doesn’t know how to rule one’s own house take care of God’s assembly?)

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not a new convert, lest being puffed up he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moreover he must have good testimony from those who are outside, to avoid falling into reproach and the snare of the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Servants, in the same way, must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for money,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let them also first be tested; then let them serve if they are blameless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Their wives in the same way must be reverent, not slanderers, temperate, and faithful in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let servants be husbands of one wife, ruling their children and their own houses well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who have served well gain for themselves a good standing, and great boldness in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things I write to you, hoping to come to you shortly;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but if I wait long, that you may know how men ought to behave themselves in God’s house, which is the assembly of the living God, the pillar and ground of the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Without controversy, the mystery of godliness is great:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Spirit says expressly that in later times some will fall away from the faith, paying attention to seducing spirits and doctrines of demons,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: through the hypocrisy of men who speak lies, branded in their own conscience as with a hot iron,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: forbidding marriage and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For every creature of God is good, and nothing is to be rejected, if it is received with thanksgiving.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you instruct the brothers of these things, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, nourished in the words of the faith, and of the good doctrine which you have followed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But refuse profane and old wives’ fables. Exercise yourself toward godliness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For bodily exercise has some value, but godliness has value in all things, having the promise of the life which is now, and of that which is to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This saying is faithful and worthy of all acceptance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to this end we both labor and suffer reproach, because we have set our trust in the living God, who is the Savior of all men, especially of those who believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Command and teach these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no man despise your youth; but be an example to those who believe, in word, in your way of life, in love, in spirit, in faith, and in purity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Until I come, pay attention to reading, to exhortation, and to teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t neglect the gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the elders.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be diligent in these things. Give yourself wholly to them, that your progress may be revealed to all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pay attention to yourself and to your teaching. Continue in these things, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father; the younger men as brothers;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, in all purity.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Honor widows who are widows indeed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them learn first to show piety toward their own family and to repay their parents, for this is  acceptable in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now she who is a widow indeed and desolate, has her hope set on God, and continues in petitions and prayers night and day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But she who gives herself to pleasure is dead while she lives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Also command these things, that they may be without reproach.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if anyone doesn’t provide for his own, and especially his own household, he has denied the faith, and is worse than an unbeliever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no one be enrolled as a widow under sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being approved by good works, if she has brought up children, if she has been hospitable to strangers, if she has washed the saints’ feet, if she has relieved the afflicted, and if she has diligently followed every good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But refuse younger widows, for when they have grown wanton against Christ, they desire to marry,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having condemnation, because they have rejected their first pledge.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Besides, they also learn to be idle, going about from house to house. Not only idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things which they ought not.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I desire therefore that the younger widows marry, bear children, rule the household, and give no occasion to the adversary for insulting.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For already some have turned away after Satan.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If any man or woman who believes has widows, let them relieve them, and don’t let the assembly be burdened, that it might relieve those who are widows indeed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let the elders who rule well be counted worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in the word and in teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle the ox when it treads out the grain.” And, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t receive an accusation against an elder, except at the word of two or three witnesses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who sin, reprove in the sight of all, that the rest also may be in fear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I command you in the sight of God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the chosen angels, that you observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing by partiality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Lay hands hastily on no one. Don’t be a participant in other people’s sins. Keep yourself pure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be no longer a drinker of water only, but use a little wine for your stomach’s sake and your frequent infirmities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Some men’s sins are evident, preceding them to judgment, and some also follow later.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way also there are good works that are obvious, and those that are otherwise can’t be hidden.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let as many as are bondservants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the doctrine not be blasphemed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who have believing masters, let them not despise them because they are brothers, but rather let them serve them, because those who partake of the benefit are believing and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone teaches a different doctrine, and doesn’t consent to sound words, the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which is according to godliness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he is conceited, knowing nothing, but obsessed with arguments, disputes, and word battles, from which come envy, strife, insulting, evil suspicions,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: constant friction of people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain. Withdraw yourself from such.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But godliness with contentment is great gain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But those who are determined to be rich fall into a temptation, a snare, and many foolish and harmful lusts, such as drown men in ruin and destruction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some have been led astray from the faith in their greed, and have pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, man of God, flee these things, and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called, and you confessed the good confession in the sight of many witnesses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I command you before God, who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus, who before Pontius Pilate testified the good confession,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you keep the commandment without spot, blameless, until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which in its own times he will show, who is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings, and Lord of lords.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no man has seen, nor can see: to whom be honor and eternal power. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Charge those who are rich in this present world that they not be arrogant, nor have their hope set on the uncertainty of riches, but on the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that they do good, that they be rich in good works, that they be ready to distribute, willing to share;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold of eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Timothy, guard that which is committed to you, turning away from the empty chatter and oppositions of what is falsely called knowledge,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: which some profess, and thus have wandered from the faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace be with you. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank God, whom I serve as my forefathers did, with a pure conscience. How unceasing is my memory of you in my petitions, night and day

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: longing to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you, which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause, I remind you that you should stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner; but endure hardship for the Good News according to the power of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who abolished death, and brought life and immortality to light through the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this I was appointed as a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this cause I also suffer these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet I am not ashamed, for I know him whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard that which I have committed to him against that day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Hold the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That good thing which was committed to you, guard through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This you know, that all who are in Asia turned away from me, of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: May the Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but when he was in Rome, he sought me diligently and found me

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (the Lord grant to him to find the Lord’s mercy in that day); and in how many things he served at Ephesus, you know very well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The things which you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit the same things to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You therefore must endure hardship as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No soldier on duty entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The farmer who labors must be the first to get a share of the crops.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, of the offspring of David, according to my Good News,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in which I suffer hardship to the point of chains as a criminal. But God’s word isn’t chained.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I endure all things for the chosen ones’ sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This saying is trustworthy:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord, that they don’t argue about words, to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But shun empty chatter, for it will go further in ungodliness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and those words will consume like gangrene, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: men who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past, and overthrowing the faith of some.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal, “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in a large house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of clay. Some are for honor, and some for dishonor.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone therefore purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, and suitable for the master’s use, prepared for every good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But refuse foolish and ignorant questionings, knowing that they generate strife.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord’s servant must not quarrel, but be gentle toward all, able to teach, patient,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in gentleness correcting those who oppose him: perhaps God may give them repentance leading to a full knowledge of the truth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they may recover themselves out of the devil’s snare, having been taken captive by him to his will.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But know this: that in the last days, grievous times will come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, fierce, not lovers of good,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: traitors, headstrong, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: holding a form of godliness, but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For some of these are people who creep into houses and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: always learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these also oppose the truth, men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith are rejected.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But they will proceed no further. For their folly will be evident to all men, as theirs also came to be.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: persecutions, and sufferings: those things that happened to me at Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I endured those persecutions. The Lord delivered me out of them all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you remain in the things which you have learned and have been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that each person who belongs to God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I command you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at his appearing and his Kingdom:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort with all patience and teaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the time will come when they will not listen to the sound doctrine, but having itching ears, will heap up for themselves teachers after their own lusts,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and will turn away their ears from the truth, and turn away to fables.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do the work of an evangelist, and fulfill your ministry.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From now on, the crown of righteousness is stored up for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be diligent to come to me soon,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for Demas left me, having loved this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia; and Titus to Dalmatia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Bring the cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus when you come, and the books, especially the parchments.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Alexander, the coppersmith, did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of whom you also must beware; for he greatly opposed our words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: At my first defense, no one came to help me, but all left me. May it not be held against them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear. So I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Erastus remained at Corinth, but I left Trophimus at Miletus sick.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus salutes you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s chosen ones, and the knowledge of the truth which is according to godliness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in hope of eternal life, which God, who can’t lie, promised before time began;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in his own time revealed his word in the message with which I was entrusted according to the commandment of God our Savior;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to Titus, my true child according to a common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I left you in Crete for this reason, that you would set in order the things that were lacking and appoint elders in every city, as I directed you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if anyone is blameless, the husband of one wife, having children who believe, who are not accused of loose or unruly behavior.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the overseer must be blameless, as God’s steward, not self-pleasing, not easily angered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for dishonest gain;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but given to hospitality, a lover of good, sober minded, fair, holy, self-controlled,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching, that he may be able to exhort in the sound doctrine, and to convict those who contradict him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there are also many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whose mouths must be stopped: men who overthrow whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for dishonest gain’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of them, a prophet of their own, said, “Cretans are always liars, evil beasts, and idle gluttons.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This testimony is true. For this cause, reprove them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not paying attention to Jewish fables and commandments of men who turn away from the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To the pure, all things are pure; but to those who are defiled and unbelieving, nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They profess that they know God, but by their deeds they deny him, being abominable, disobedient, and unfit for any good work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But say the things which fit sound doctrine,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that older men should be temperate, sensible, sober minded, sound in faith, in love, and in perseverance:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that older women likewise be reverent in behavior, not slanderers nor enslaved to much wine, teachers of that which is good,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that they may train the young wives to love their husbands, to love their children,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to be sober minded, chaste, workers at home, kind, being in subjection to their own husbands, that God’s word may not be blasphemed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise, exhort the younger men to be sober minded.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In all things be showing yourself an example of good works. In your teaching, be showing integrity, seriousness, incorruptibility,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and soundness of speech that can’t be condemned, that he who opposes you may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say about us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Exhort servants to be in subjection to their own masters and to be well-pleasing in all things, not contradicting,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not stealing, but showing all good fidelity, that they may adorn the doctrine of God, our Savior, in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present age;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: looking for the blessed hope and appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify for himself a people for his own possession, zealous for good works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Say these things and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one despise you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remind them to be in subjection to rulers and to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to speak evil of no one, not to be contentious, to be gentle, showing all humility toward all men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we were also once foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving various lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But when the kindness of God our Savior and his love toward mankind appeared,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom he poured out on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Savior;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that being justified by his grace, we might be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This saying is faithful, and concerning these things I desire that you affirm confidently, so that those who have believed God may be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable to men;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but shun foolish questionings, genealogies, strife, and disputes about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Avoid a factious man after a first and second warning,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that such a one is perverted and sins, being self-condemned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, be diligent to come to me to Nicopolis, for I have determined to winter there.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Send Zenas, the lawyer, and Apollos on their journey speedily, that nothing may be lacking for them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who are with me greet you. Greet those who love us in faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved fellow worker,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the beloved Apphia, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the assembly in your house:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I thank my God always, making mention of you in my prayers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: hearing of your love and of the faith which you have toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the fellowship of your faith may become effective in the knowledge of every good thing which is in us in Christ Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we have much joy and comfort in your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore though I have all boldness in Christ to command you that which is appropriate,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: yet for love’s sake I rather beg, being such a one as Paul, the aged, but also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I beg you for my child, whom I have become the father of in my chains, Onesimus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who once was useless to you, but now is useful to you and to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am sending him back. Therefore receive him, that is, my own heart,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom I desired to keep with me, that on your behalf he might serve me in my chains for the Good News.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I was willing to do nothing without your consent, that your goodness would not be as of necessity, but of free will.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For perhaps he was therefore separated from you for a while, that you would have him forever,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother, especially to me, but how much rather to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If then you count me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if he has wronged you at all or owes you anything, put that to my account.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I, Paul, write this with my own hand: I will repay it (not to mention to you that you owe to me even your own self besides).

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, brother, let me have joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having confidence in your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even beyond what I say.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Also, prepare a guest room for me, for I hope that through your prayers I will be restored to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having become as much better than the angels as the more excellent name he has inherited is better than theirs.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to which of the angels did he say at any time,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he again brings in the firstborn into the world he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Of the angels he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But of the Son he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But which of the angels has he told at any time,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Aren’t they all serving spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just penalty,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation—which at the first having been spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God also testifying with them, both by signs and wonders, by various works of power and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to his own will?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he didn’t subject the world to come, of which we speak, to angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But one has somewhere testified, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing that is not subject to him. But now we don’t see all things subjected to him, yet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it became him, for whom are all things, and through whom are all things, in bringing many children to glory, to make the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one, for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, “I will put my trust in him.” Again, “Behold, here I am with the children whom God has given me.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in the same way partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and might deliver all of them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For most certainly, he doesn’t give help to angels, but he gives help to the offspring of Abraham.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession: Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also Moses was in all his house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, because he who built the house has more honor than the house.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house. We are his house, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there might be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called “today”, lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence firm to the end,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: while it is said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For who, when they heard, rebelled? Wasn’t it all those who came out of Egypt led by Moses?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To whom did he swear that they wouldn’t enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We see that they weren’t able to enter in because of unbelief.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s fear therefore, lest perhaps anyone of you should seem to have come short of a promise of entering into his rest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For indeed we have had good news preached to us, even as they also did, but the word they heard didn’t profit them, because it wasn’t mixed with faith by those who heard.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest;” although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he has said this somewhere about the seventh day, “God rested on the seventh day from all his works;”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in this place again, “They will not enter into my rest.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing therefore it remains that some should enter into it, and they to whom the good news was preached before failed to enter in because of disobedience,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he again defines a certain day, today, saying through David so long a time afterward (just as has been said),

sources.label: New Testament
