sources.text: For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall after the same example of disobedience.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him to whom we must give an account.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having then a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let’s hold tightly to our confession.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and may find grace for help in time of need.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As he says also in another place,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let’s press on to perfection—not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This will we do, if God permits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it and produces a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you won’t be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherited the promises.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom also Abraham divided a tenth part of all (being first, by interpretation, “king of righteousness”, and then also “king of Salem”, which means “king of peace”,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God), remains a priest continually.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth out of the best plunder.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest’s office have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brothers, though these have come out of the body of Abraham,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has accepted tithes from Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here people who die receive tithes, but there one receives tithes of whom it is testified that he lives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We can say that through Abraham even Levi, who receives tithes, has paid tithes,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he was yet in the body of his father when Melchizedek met him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now if perfection was through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people have received the law), what further need was there for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he of whom these things are said belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is evident that our Lord has sprung out of Judah, about which tribe Moses spoke nothing concerning priesthood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is yet more abundantly evident, if after the likeness of Melchizedek there arises another priest,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who has been made, not after the law of a fleshly commandment, but after the power of an endless life;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for it is testified,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there is an annulling of a foregoing commandment because of its weakness and uselessness

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (for the law made nothing perfect), and a bringing in of a better hope, through which we draw near to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Inasmuch as he was not made priest without the taking of an oath

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (for they indeed have been made priests without an oath), but he with an oath by him that says of him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By so much, Jesus has become the collateral of a better covenant.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many, indeed, have been made priests, because they are hindered from continuing by death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he, because he lives forever, has his priesthood unchangeable.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing that he lives forever to make intercession for them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For such a high priest was fitting for us: holy, guiltless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who doesn’t need, like those high priests, to offer up sacrifices daily, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. For he did this once for all, when he offered up himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath which came after the law appoints a Son forever who has been perfected.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now in the things which we are saying, the main point is this. We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: a servant of the sanctuary and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this high priest also have something to offer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, seeing there are priests who offer the gifts according to the law,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things, even as Moses was warned by God when he was about to make the tabernacle, for he said, “See, you shall make everything according to the pattern that was shown to you on the mountain.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now he has obtained a more excellent ministry, by so much as he is also the mediator of a better covenant, which on better promises has been given as law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if that first covenant had been faultless, then no place would have been sought for a second.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For finding fault with them, he said,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that he says, “A new covenant”, he has made the first old. But that which is becoming old and grows aged is near to vanishing away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service and an earthly sanctuary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a tabernacle was prepared. In the first part were the lamp stand, the table, and the show bread; which is called the Holy Place.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the second veil was the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having a golden altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold, in which was a golden pot holding the manna, Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tablets of the covenant;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat, of which things we can’t speak now in detail.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now these things having been thus prepared, the priests go in continually into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the services,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but into the second the high priest alone, once in the year, not without blood, which he offers for himself, and for the errors of the people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Holy Spirit is indicating this, that the way into the Holy Place wasn’t yet revealed while the first tabernacle was still standing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is a symbol of the present age, where gifts and sacrifices are offered that are incapable, concerning the conscience, of making the worshiper perfect,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: being only (with meats and drinks and various washings) fleshly ordinances, imposed until a time of reformation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Christ having come as a high priest of the coming good things, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor yet through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, entered in once for all into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify to the cleanness of the flesh,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without defect to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant, since a death has occurred for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first covenant, that those who have been called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For where a last will and testament is, there must of necessity be the death of him who made it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For a will is in force where there has been death, for it is never in force while he who made it lives.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore even the first covenant has not been dedicated without blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of the calves and the goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “This is the blood of the covenant which God has commanded you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Moreover he sprinkled the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry in the same way with the blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: According to the law, nearly everything is cleansed with blood, and apart from shedding of blood there is no remission.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these, but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For Christ hasn’t entered into holy places made with hands, which are representations of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy place year by year with blood not his own,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or else he must have suffered often since the foundation of the world. But now once at the end of the ages, he has been revealed to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so Christ also, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, without sin, to those who are eagerly waiting for him for salvation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or else wouldn’t they have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But in those sacrifices there is a yearly reminder of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore when he comes into the world, he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then he has said, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He takes away the first, that he may establish the second,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every priest indeed stands day by day serving and often offering the same sacrifices which can never take away sins,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down on the right hand of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: then he says,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and having a great priest over God’s house,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: let’s draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: let’s hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering; for he who promised is faithful.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another, and so much the more as you see the Day approaching.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A man who disregards Moses’ law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: How much worse punishment do you think he will be judged worthy of who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we know him who said, “Vengeance belongs to me;” says the Lord, “I will repay.” Again, “The Lord will judge his people.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore don’t throw away your boldness, which has a great reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For by this, the elders obtained testimony.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God testifying with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn’t see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared a ship for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to whom it was said, “Your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac,”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: concluding that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel, and gave instructions concerning his bones.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: choosing rather to share ill treatment with God’s people than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: considering the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By faith, Rahab the prostitute didn’t perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and caused foreign armies to flee.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: of whom the world was not worthy—wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn’t receive the promise,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let’s also, seeing we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily entangles us, and let’s run with perseverance the race that is set before us,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For consider him who has endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, that you don’t grow weary, fainting in your souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have not yet resisted to blood, striving against sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have forgotten the exhortation which reasons with you as with children,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn’t discipline?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you are without discipline, of which all have been made partakers, then you are illegitimate, and not children.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Furthermore, we had the fathers of our flesh to chasten us, and we paid them respect. Shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they indeed, for a few days, punished us as seemed good to them; but he for our profit, that we may be partakers of his holiness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been trained by it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and make straight paths for your feet, so what is lame may not be dislocated, but rather be healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man will see the Lord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God, lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and many be defiled by it,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: lest there be any sexually immoral person, or profane person, like Esau, who sold his birthright for one meal.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you know that even when he afterward desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for a change of mind though he sought it diligently with tears.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you have not come to a mountain that might be touched, and that burned with fire, and to blackness, darkness, storm,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which those who heard it begged that not one more word should be spoken to them,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they could not stand that which was commanded, “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned”.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So fearful was the appearance that Moses said, “I am terrified and trembling.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you have come to Mount Zion, and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable multitudes of angels,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to the festal gathering and assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, to God the Judge of all, to the spirits of just men made perfect,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling that speaks better than that of Abel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See that you don’t refuse him who speaks. For if they didn’t escape when they refused him who warned on the earth, how much more will we not escape who turn away from him who warns from heaven,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whose voice shook the earth then, but now he has promised, saying, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This phrase, “Yet once more” signifies the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that have been made, that those things which are not shaken may remain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, receiving a Kingdom that can’t be shaken, let’s have grace, through which we serve God acceptably, with reverence and awe,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for our God is a consuming fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let brotherly love continue.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t forget to show hospitality to strangers, for in doing so, some have entertained angels without knowing it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember those who are in bonds, as bound with them, and those who are ill-treated, since you are also in the body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled; but God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So that with good courage we say,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember your leaders, men who spoke to you the word of God, and considering the results of their conduct, imitate their faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be carried away by various and strange teachings, for it is good that the heart be established by grace, not by food, through which those who were so occupied were not benefited.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have an altar from which those who serve the holy tabernacle have no right to eat.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the bodies of those animals, whose blood is brought into the holy place by the high priest as an offering for sin, are burned outside of the camp.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside of the gate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we don’t have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Through him, then, let’s offer up a sacrifice of praise to God  continually, that is, the fruit of lips which proclaim allegiance to his name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But don’t forget to be doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch on behalf of your souls, as those who will give account, that they may do this with joy, and not with groaning, for that would be unprofitable for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, desiring to live honorably in all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I strongly urge you to do this, that I may be restored to you sooner.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: make you complete in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation; for I have written to you in few words.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Know that our brother Timothy has been freed, with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace be with you all. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes which are in the Dispersion: Greetings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Count it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach, and it will be given to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let him ask in faith, without any doubting, for he who doubts is like a wave of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For that man shouldn’t think that he will receive anything from the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But let the brother in humble circumstances glory in his high position;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the rich, in that he is made humble, because like the flower in the grass, he will pass away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the sun arises with the scorching wind and withers the grass, and the flower in it falls, and the beauty of its appearance perishes. So the rich man will also fade away in his pursuits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is a person who endures temptation, for when he has been approved, he will receive the crown of life, which the Lord promised to those who love him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let no man say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God,” for God can’t be tempted by evil, and he himself tempts no one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own lust and enticed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then the lust, when it has conceived, bears sin. The sin, when it is full grown, produces death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be deceived, my beloved brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Of his own will he gave birth to us by the word of truth, that we should be a kind of first fruits of his creatures.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, putting away all filthiness and overflowing of wickedness, receive with humility the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But be doers of the word, and not only hearers, deluding your own selves.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man looking at his natural face in a mirror;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he sees himself, and goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of man he was.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who looks into the perfect law of freedom and continues, not being a hearer who forgets, but a doer of the work, this man will be blessed in what he does.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone among you thinks himself to be religious while he doesn’t bridle his tongue, but deceives his heart, this man’s religion is worthless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Pure religion and undefiled before our God and Father is this: to visit the fatherless and widows in their affliction, and to keep oneself unstained by the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if a man with a gold ring, in fine clothing, comes into your synagogue, and a poor man in filthy clothing also comes in,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and you pay special attention to him who wears the fine clothing and say, “Sit here in a good place;” and you tell the poor man, “Stand there,” or “Sit by my footstool”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: haven’t you shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Listen, my beloved brothers. Didn’t God choose those who are poor in this world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the Kingdom which he promised to those who love him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you have dishonored the poor man. Don’t the rich oppress you, and personally drag you before the courts?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t they blaspheme the honorable name by which you are called?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However, if you fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you show partiality, you commit sin, being convicted by the law as transgressors.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not commit murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So speak and so do, as men who are to be judged by a law of freedom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For judgment is without mercy to him who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And if a brother or sister is naked and in lack of daily food,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and one of you tells them, “Go in peace. Be warmed and filled;” yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I will show you my faith by my works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wasn’t Abraham our father justified by works, in that he offered up Isaac his son on the altar?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him as righteousness,” and he was called the friend of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You see then that by works, a man is justified, and not only by faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, wasn’t Rahab the prostitute also justified by works, in that she received the messengers and sent them out another way?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let not many of you be teachers, my brothers, knowing that we will receive heavier judgment.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we all stumble in many things. Anyone who doesn’t stumble in word is a perfect person, able to bridle the whole body also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Indeed, we put bits into the horses’ mouths so that they may obey us, and we guide their whole body.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, the ships also, though they are so big and are driven by fierce winds, are yet guided by a very small rudder, wherever the pilot desires.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So the tongue is also a little member, and boasts great things. See how a small fire can spread to a large forest!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And the tongue is a fire. The world of iniquity among our members is the tongue, which defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature, and is set on fire by Gehenna.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For every kind of animal, bird, creeping thing, and sea creature, is tamed, and has been tamed by mankind;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but nobody can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men who are made in the image of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Out of the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Does a spring send out from the same opening fresh and bitter water?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Can a fig tree, my brothers, yield olives, or a vine figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh water.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by his good conduct that his deeds are done in gentleness of wisdom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your heart, don’t boast and don’t lie against the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This wisdom is not that which comes down from above, but is earthly, sensual, and demonic.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For where jealousy and selfish ambition are, there is confusion and every evil deed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Where do wars and fightings among you come from? Don’t they come from your pleasures that war in your members?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You lust, and don’t have. You murder and covet, and can’t obtain. You fight and make war. You don’t have, because you don’t ask.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You ask, and don’t receive, because you ask with wrong motives, so that you may spend it on your pleasures.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You adulterers and adulteresses, don’t you know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who lives in us yearns jealously”?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he gives more grace. Therefore it says, “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Draw near to God, and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you double-minded.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Lament, mourn, and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning, and your joy to gloom.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he will exalt you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t speak against one another, brothers. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law, but a judge.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Only one is the lawgiver, who is able to save and to destroy. But who are you to judge another?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow let’s go into this city, and spend a year there, trade, and make a profit.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whereas you don’t know what your life will be like tomorrow. For what is your life? For you are a vapor that appears for a little time, and then vanishes away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will both live, and do this or that.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But now you glory in your boasting. All such boasting is evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To him therefore who knows to do good, and doesn’t do it, to him it is sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming on you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Your riches are corrupted and your garments are moth-eaten.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Your gold and your silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be for a testimony against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have laid up your treasure in the last days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you have kept back by fraud, cry out, and the cries of those who reaped have entered into the ears of the Lord of Armies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have lived in luxury on the earth, and taken your pleasure. You have nourished your hearts as in a day of slaughter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have condemned and you have murdered the righteous one. He doesn’t resist you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be patient therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient over it, until it receives the early and late rain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You also be patient. Establish your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t grumble, brothers, against one another, so that you won’t be judged. Behold, the judge stands at the door.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Take, brothers, for an example of suffering and of perseverance, the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, we call them blessed who endured. You have heard of the perseverance of Job, and have seen the Lord in the outcome, and how the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But above all things, my brothers, don’t swear— not by heaven, or by the earth, or by any other oath; but let your “yes” be “yes”, and your “no”, “no”, so that you don’t fall into hypocrisy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is any among you suffering? Let him pray. Is any cheerful? Let him sing praises.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain, and it didn’t rain on the earth for three years and six months.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He prayed again, and the sky gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, if any among you wanders from the truth and someone turns him back,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the chosen ones who are living as foreigners in the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, in sanctification of the Spirit, that you may obey Jesus Christ and be sprinkled with his blood: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to his great mercy caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to an incorruptible and undefiled inheritance that doesn’t fade away, reserved in Heaven for you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who by the power of God are guarded through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wherein you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if need be, you have been grieved in various trials,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that the proof of your faith, which is more precious than gold that perishes even though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whom, not having known, you love. In him, though now you don’t see him, yet believing, you rejoice greatly with joy that is unspeakable and full of glory,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: receiving the result of your faith, the salvation of your souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Concerning this salvation, the prophets sought and searched diligently. They prophesied of the grace that would come to you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: searching for who or what kind of time the Spirit of Christ, which was in them, pointed to, when he predicted the sufferings of Christ, and the glories that would follow them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To them it was revealed, that they served not to themselves, but to you, in these things, which now have been announced to you through those who preached the Good News to you by the Holy Spirit sent out from heaven; which things angels desire to look into.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore prepare your minds for action. Be sober, and set your hope fully on the grace that will be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as children of obedience, not conforming yourselves according to your former lusts as in your ignorance,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but just as he who called you is holy, you yourselves also be holy in all of your behavior;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because it is written, “You shall be holy; for I am holy.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you call on him as Father, who without respect of persons judges according to each man’s work, pass the time of your living as foreigners here in reverent fear,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that you were redeemed, not with corruptible things, with silver or gold, from the useless way of life handed down from your fathers,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but with precious blood, as of a lamb without blemish or spot, the blood of Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who was foreknown indeed before the foundation of the world, but was revealed in this last age for your sake,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who through him are believers in God, who raised him from the dead, and gave him glory, so that your faith and hope might be in God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Seeing you have purified your souls in your obedience to the truth through the Spirit in sincere brotherly affection, love one another from the heart fervently,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having been born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, through the word of God, which lives and remains forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the word of Good News which was preached to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Putting away therefore all wickedness, all deceit, hypocrisies, envies, and all evil speaking,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as newborn babies, long for the pure milk of the Word, that with it you may grow,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: if indeed you have tasted that the Lord is gracious:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: coming to him, a living stone, rejected indeed by men, but chosen by God, precious.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You also, as living stones, are built up as a spiritual house, to be a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices, acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because it is contained in Scripture,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you who believe therefore is the honor, but for those who are disobedient,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they stumble at the word, being disobedient, to which also they were appointed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the past, you were not a people, but now are God’s people, who had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, I beg you as foreigners and pilgrims, to abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having good behavior among the nations, so in that of which they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good works, which they see, glorify God in the day of visitation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore subject yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether to the king, as supreme;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: or to governors, as sent by him for vengeance on evildoers and for praise to those who do well.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is the will of God, that by well-doing you should put to silence the ignorance of foolish men:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as free, and not using your freedom for a cloak of wickedness, but as bondservants of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Servants, be in subjection to your masters with all respect: not only to the good and gentle, but also to the wicked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is commendable if someone endures pain, suffering unjustly, because of conscience toward God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For what glory is it if, when you sin, you patiently endure beating? But if, when you do well, you patiently endure suffering, this is commendable with God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you were called to this, because Christ also suffered for us, leaving you an example, that you should follow his steps,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who didn’t sin, “neither was deceit found in his mouth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he was cursed, he didn’t curse back. When he suffered, he didn’t threaten, but committed himself to him who judges righteously.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness. You were healed by his wounds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For you were going astray like sheep; but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In the same way, wives, be in subjection to your own husbands; so that, even if any don’t obey the Word, they may be won by the behavior of their wives without a word,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing your pure behavior in fear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let your beauty be not just the outward adorning of braiding the hair, and of wearing jewels of gold, or of putting on fine clothing;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in the hidden person of the heart, in the incorruptible adornment of a gentle and quiet spirit, which is very precious in the sight of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is how in the past, the holy women who hoped in God also adorned themselves, being in subjection to their own husbands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So Sarah obeyed Abraham, calling him lord, whose children you now are, if you do well, and are not put in fear by any terror.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You husbands, in the same way, live with your wives according to knowledge, giving honor to the woman, as to the weaker vessel, as also being joint heirs of the grace of life, that your prayers may not be hindered.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Finally, all of you be like-minded, compassionate, loving as brothers, tenderhearted, courteous,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not rendering evil for evil, or insult for insult; but instead blessing, knowing that you were called to this, that you may inherit a blessing.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now who will harm you if you become imitators of that which is good?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But even if you should suffer for righteousness’ sake, you are blessed. “Don’t fear what they fear, neither be troubled.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts. Always be ready to give an answer to everyone who asks you a reason concerning the hope that is in you, with humility and fear,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having a good conscience. Thus, while you are spoken against as evildoers, they may be disappointed who curse your good way of life in Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For it is better, if it is God’s will, that you suffer for doing well than for doing evil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because Christ also suffered for sins once, the righteous for the unrighteous, that he might bring you to God, being put to death in the flesh, but made alive in the Spirit,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in whom he also went and preached to the spirits in prison,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who before were disobedient, when God waited patiently in the days of Noah, while the ship was being built. In it, few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is a symbol of baptism, which now saves you—not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience toward God, through the resurrection of Jesus Christ,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who is at the right hand of God, having gone into heaven, angels and authorities and powers being made subject to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, since Christ suffered for us in the flesh, arm yourselves also with the same mind; for he who has suffered in the flesh has ceased from sin,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you no longer should live the rest of your time in the flesh for the lusts of men, but for the will of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we have spent enough of our past time doing the desire of the Gentiles, and having walked in lewdness, lusts, drunken binges, orgies, carousings, and abominable idolatries.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They think it is strange that you don’t run with them into the same excess of riot, blaspheming.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will give account to him who is ready to judge the living and the dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For to this end the Good News was preached even to the dead, that they might be judged indeed as men in the flesh, but live as to God in the spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the end of all things is near. Therefore be of sound mind, self-controlled, and sober in prayer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be hospitable to one another without grumbling.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As each has received a gift, employ it in serving one another, as good managers of the grace of God in its various forms.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone speaks, let it be as it were the very words of God. If anyone serves, let it be as of the strength which God supplies, that in all things God may be glorified through Jesus Christ, to whom belong the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, don’t be astonished at the fiery trial which has come upon you to test you, as though a strange thing happened to you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But because you are partakers of Christ’s sufferings, rejoice, that at the revelation of his glory you also may rejoice with exceeding joy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you are insulted for the name of Christ, you are blessed; because the Spirit of glory and of God rests on you. On their part he is blasphemed, but on your part he is glorified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For let none of you suffer as a murderer, or a thief, or an evil doer, or a meddler in other men’s matters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if one of you suffers for being a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God in this matter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the time has come for judgment to begin with the household of God. If it begins first with us, what will happen to those who don’t obey the Good News of God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “If it is hard for the righteous to be saved, what will happen to the ungodly and the sinner?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore let them also who suffer according to the will of God in doing good entrust their souls to him, as to a faithful Creator.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I exhort the elders among you, as a fellow elder, and a witness of the sufferings of Christ, and who will also share in the glory that will be revealed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Shepherd the flock of God which is among you, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion, but voluntarily, not for dishonest gain, but willingly;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: not as lording it over those entrusted to you, but making yourselves examples to the flock.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the chief Shepherd is revealed, you will receive the crown of glory that doesn’t fade away.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Likewise, you younger ones, be subject to the elder. Yes, all of you clothe yourselves with humility, to subject yourselves to one another; for “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Withstand him steadfast in your faith, knowing that your brothers who are in the world are undergoing the same sufferings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But may the God of all grace, who called you to his eternal glory by Christ Jesus, after you have suffered a little while, perfect, establish, strengthen, and settle you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: To him be the glory and the power forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Through Silvanus, our faithful brother, as I consider him, I have written to you briefly, exhorting, and testifying that this is the true grace of God in which you stand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She who is in Babylon, chosen together with you, greets you. So does Mark, my son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Greet one another with a kiss of love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Peace be to all of you who are in Christ Jesus. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Simon Peter, a servant and apostle of Jesus Christ, to those who have obtained a like precious faith with us in the righteousness of our God and Savior, Jesus Christ:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace to you and peace be multiplied in the knowledge of God and of Jesus our Lord,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: seeing that his divine power has granted to us all things that pertain to life and godliness, through the knowledge of him who called us by his own glory and virtue,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by which he has granted to us his precious and exceedingly great promises; that through these you may become partakers of the divine nature, having escaped from the corruption that is in the world by lust.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, and for this very cause adding on your part all diligence, in your faith supply moral excellence; and in moral excellence, knowledge;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in knowledge, self-control; and in self-control perseverance; and in perseverance godliness;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and in godliness brotherly affection; and in brotherly affection, love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if these things are yours and abound, they make you to not be idle or unfruitful in the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he who lacks these things is blind, seeing only what is near, having forgotten the cleansing from his old sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, brothers, be more diligent to make your calling and election sure. For if you do these things, you will never stumble.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For thus you will be richly supplied with the entrance into the eternal Kingdom of our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore I will not be negligent to remind you of these things, though you know them, and are established in the present truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I think it right, as long as I am in this tent, to stir you up by reminding you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing that the putting off of my tent comes swiftly, even as our Lord Jesus Christ made clear to me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yes, I will make every effort that you may always be able to remember these things even after my departure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For we didn’t follow cunningly devised fables when we made known to you the power and coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, but we were eyewitnesses of his majesty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For he received from God the Father honor and glory when the voice came to him from the Majestic Glory, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We heard this voice come out of heaven when we were with him on the holy mountain.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have the more sure word of prophecy; and you do well that you heed it, as to a lamp shining in a dark place, until the day dawns, and the morning star arises in your hearts:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing this first, that no prophecy of Scripture is of private interpretation.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For no prophecy ever came by the will of man: but holy men of God spoke, being moved by the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But false prophets also arose among the people, as false teachers will also be among you, who will secretly bring in destructive heresies, denying even the Master who bought them, bringing on themselves swift destruction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Many will follow their immoral ways, and as a result, the way of the truth will be maligned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In covetousness they will exploit you with deceptive words: whose sentence now from of old doesn’t linger, and their destruction will not slumber.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if God didn’t spare angels when they sinned, but cast them down to Tartarus, and committed them to pits of darkness to be reserved for judgment;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and didn’t spare the ancient world, but preserved Noah with seven others, a preacher of righteousness, when he brought a flood on the world of the ungodly;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah into ashes, condemned them to destruction, having made them an example to those who would live in an ungodly way;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and delivered righteous Lot, who was very distressed by the lustful life of the wicked

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (for that righteous man dwelling among them was tormented in his righteous soul from day to day with seeing and hearing lawless deeds):

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the Lord knows how to deliver the godly out of temptation and to keep the unrighteous under punishment for the day of judgment,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but chiefly those who walk after the flesh in the lust of defilement and despise authority. Daring, self-willed, they are not afraid to speak evil of dignitaries;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: whereas angels, though greater in might and power, don’t bring a railing judgment against them before the Lord.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But these, as unreasoning creatures, born natural animals to be taken and destroyed, speaking evil in matters about which they are ignorant, will in their destroying surely be destroyed,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: receiving the wages of unrighteousness; people who count it pleasure to revel in the daytime, spots and defects, reveling in their deceit while they feast with you;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having eyes full of adultery, and who can’t cease from sin; enticing unsettled souls; having a heart trained in greed; children of cursing;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: forsaking the right way, they went astray, having followed the way of Balaam the son of Beor, who loved the wages of wrongdoing;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but he was rebuked for his own disobedience. A mute donkey spoke with a man’s voice and stopped the madness of the prophet.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are wells without water, clouds driven by a storm; for whom the blackness of darkness has been reserved forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For, uttering great swelling words of emptiness, they entice in the lusts of the flesh, by licentiousness, those who are indeed escaping from those who live in error;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: promising them liberty, while they themselves are bondservants of corruption; for a man is brought into bondage by whoever overcomes him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For if, after they have escaped the defilement of the world through the knowledge of the Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, they are again entangled in it and overcome, the last state has become worse for them than the first.

sources.label: New Testament
