sources.text: For it would be better for them not to have known the way of righteousness, than after knowing it, to turn back from the holy commandment delivered to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But it has happened to them according to the true proverb, “The dog turns to his own vomit again,” and “the sow that has washed to wallowing in the mire.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is now, beloved, the second letter that I have written to you; and in both of them I stir up your sincere mind by reminding you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you should remember the words which were spoken before by the holy prophets, and the commandment of us, the apostles of the Lord and Savior:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: knowing this first, that in the last days mockers will come, walking after their own lusts

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and saying, “Where is the promise of his coming? For, from the day that the fathers fell asleep, all things continue as they were from the beginning of the creation.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this they willfully forget that there were heavens from of old, and an earth formed out of water and amid water by the word of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: by which means the world that existed then, being overflowed with water, perished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the heavens that exist now and the earth, by the same word have been stored up for fire, being reserved against the day of judgment and destruction of ungodly men.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But don’t forget this one thing, beloved, that one day is with the Lord as a thousand years, and a thousand years as one day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Lord is not slow concerning his promise, as some count slowness; but he is patient with us, not wishing that anyone should perish, but that all should come to repentance.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But the day of the Lord will come as a thief in the night; in which the heavens will pass away with a great noise, and the elements will be dissolved with fervent heat, and the earth and the works that are in it will be burned up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore since all these things will be destroyed like this, what kind of people ought you to be in holy living and godliness,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: looking for and earnestly desiring the coming of the day of God, which will cause the burning heavens to be dissolved, and the elements will melt with fervent heat?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But, according to his promise, we look for new heavens and a new earth, in which righteousness dwells.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, beloved, seeing that you look for these things, be diligent to be found in peace, without defect and blameless in his sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Regard the patience of our Lord as salvation; even as our beloved brother Paul also, according to the wisdom given to him, wrote to you,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: as also in all of his letters, speaking in them of these things. In those, there are some things that are hard to understand, which the ignorant and unsettled twist, as they also do to the other Scriptures, to their own destruction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You therefore, beloved, knowing these things beforehand, beware, lest being carried away with the error of the wicked, you fall from your own steadfastness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and forever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: That which was from the beginning, that which we have heard, that which we have seen with our eyes, that which we saw, and our hands touched, concerning the Word of life

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: (and the life was revealed, and we have seen, and testify, and declare to you the life, the eternal life, which was with the Father, and was revealed to us);

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that which we have seen and heard we declare to you, that you also may have fellowship with us. Yes, and our fellowship is with the Father, and with his Son, Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And we write these things to you, that our joy may be fulfilled.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the message which we have heard from him and announce to you, that God is light, and in him is no darkness at all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we say that we have fellowship with him and walk in the darkness, we lie, and don’t tell the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But if we walk in the light, as he is in the light, we have fellowship with one another, and the blood of Jesus Christ, his Son, cleanses us from all sin.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we say that we haven’t sinned, we make him a liar, and his word is not in us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. If anyone sins, we have a Counselor with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And he is the atoning sacrifice for our sins, and not for ours only, but also for the whole world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is how we know that we know him: if we keep his commandments.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One who says, “I know him,” and doesn’t keep his commandments, is a liar, and the truth isn’t in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But God’s love has most certainly been perfected in whoever keeps his word. This is how we know that we are in him:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he who says he remains in him ought himself also to walk just like he walked.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Brothers, I write no new commandment to you, but an old commandment which you had from the beginning. The old commandment is the word which you heard from the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Again, I write a new commandment to you, which is true in him and in you; because the darkness is passing away and the true light already shines.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who says he is in the light and hates his brother is in the darkness even until now.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who loves his brother remains in the light, and there is no occasion for stumbling in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But he who hates his brother is in the darkness, and walks in the darkness, and doesn’t know where he is going, because the darkness has blinded his eyes.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I write to you, little children, because your sins are forgiven you for his name’s sake.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I write to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I write to you, young men, because you have overcome the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I write to you, little children, because you know the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have written to you, fathers, because you know him who is from the beginning.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have written to you, young men, because you are strong, and the word of God remains in you, and you have overcome the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t love the world or the things that are in the world. If anyone loves the world, the Father’s love isn’t in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For all that is in the world, the lust of the flesh, the lust of the eyes, and the pride of life, isn’t the Father’s, but is the world’s.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The world is passing away with its lusts, but he who does God’s will remains forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Little children, these are the end times, and as you heard that the Antichrist is coming, even now many antichrists have arisen. By this we know that it is the final hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went out from us, but they didn’t belong to us; for if they had belonged to us, they would have continued with us. But they left, that they might be revealed that none of them belong to us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have an anointing from the Holy One, and you all have knowledge.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have not written to you because you don’t know the truth, but because you know it, and because no lie is of the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who is the liar but he who denies that Jesus is the Christ? This is the Antichrist, he who denies the Father and the Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever denies the Son doesn’t have the Father. He who confesses the Son has the Father also.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore, as for you, let that remain in you which you heard from the beginning. If that which you heard from the beginning remains in you, you also will remain in the Son, and in the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the promise which he promised us, the eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things I have written to you concerning those who would lead you astray.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As for you, the anointing which you received from him remains in you, and you don’t need for anyone to teach you. But as his anointing teaches you concerning all things, and is true, and is no lie, and even as it taught you, you will remain in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now, little children, remain in him, that when he appears, we may have boldness, and not be ashamed before him at his coming.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If you know that he is righteous, you know that everyone who practices righteousness has been born of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: See how great a love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God! For this cause the world doesn’t know us, because it didn’t know him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, now we are children of God. It is not yet revealed what we will be; but we know that when he is revealed, we will be like him; for we will see him just as he is.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who has this hope set on him purifies himself, even as he is pure.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Everyone who sins also commits lawlessness. Sin is lawlessness.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You know that he was revealed to take away our sins, and no sin is in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever remains in him doesn’t sin. Whoever sins hasn’t seen him and doesn’t know him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Little children, let no one lead you astray. He who does righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who sins is of the devil, for the devil has been sinning from the beginning. To this end the Son of God was revealed: that he might destroy the works of the devil.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever is born of God doesn’t commit sin, because his seed remains in him, and he can’t sin, because he is born of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this the children of God are revealed, and the children of the devil. Whoever doesn’t do righteousness is not of God, neither is he who doesn’t love his brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is the message which you heard from the beginning, that we should love one another;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: unlike Cain, who was of the evil one, and killed his brother. Why did he kill him? Because his deeds were evil, and his brother’s righteous.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be surprised, my brothers, if the world hates you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that we have passed out of death into life, because we love the brothers. He who doesn’t love his brother remains in death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever hates his brother is a murderer, and you know that no murderer has eternal life remaining in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this we know love, because he laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But whoever has the world’s goods and sees his brother in need, then closes his heart of compassion against him, how does God’s love remain in him?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: My little children, let’s not love in word only, or with the tongue only, but in deed and truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And by this we know that we are of the truth, and persuade our hearts before him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because if our heart condemns us, God is greater than our heart, and knows all things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, if our hearts don’t condemn us, we have boldness toward God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: so whatever we ask, we receive from him, because we keep his commandments and do the things that are pleasing in his sight.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is his commandment, that we should believe in the name of his Son, Jesus Christ, and love one another, even as he commanded.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who keeps his commandments remains in him, and he in him. By this we know that he remains in us, by the Spirit which he gave us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, don’t believe every spirit, but test the spirits, whether they are of God, because many false prophets have gone out into the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this you know the Spirit of God: every spirit who confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and every spirit who doesn’t confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is not of God, and this is the spirit of the Antichrist, of whom you have heard that it comes. Now it is in the world already.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You are of God, little children, and have overcome them; because greater is he who is in you than he who is in the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They are of the world. Therefore they speak of the world, and the world hears them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We are of God. He who knows God listens to us. He who is not of God doesn’t listen to us. By this we know the spirit of truth, and the spirit of error.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, let’s love one another, for love is of God; and everyone who loves has been born of God, and knows God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who doesn’t love doesn’t know God, for God is love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this God’s love was revealed in us, that God has sent his one and only Son into the world that we might live through him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, if God loved us in this way, we also ought to love one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one has seen God at any time. If we love one another, God remains in us, and his love has been perfected in us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this we know that we remain in him and he in us, because he has given us of his Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We have seen and testify that the Father has sent the Son as the Savior of the world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God remains in him, and he in God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know and have believed the love which God has for us. God is love, and he who remains in love remains in God, and God remains in him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In this love has been made perfect among us, that we may have boldness in the day of judgment, because as he is, even so we are in this world.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment. He who fears is not made perfect in love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We love him, because he first loved us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If a man says, “I love God,” and hates his brother, he is a liar; for he who doesn’t love his brother whom he has seen, how can he love God whom he has not seen?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This commandment we have from him, that he who loves God should also love his brother.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God. Whoever loves the Father also loves the child who is born of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By this we know that we love the children of God, when we love God and keep his commandments.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For this is loving God, that we keep his commandments. His commandments are not grievous.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For whatever is born of God overcomes the world. This is the victory that has overcome the world: your faith.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Who is he who overcomes the world, but he who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is he who came by water and blood, Jesus Christ; not with the water only, but with the water and the blood. It is the Spirit who testifies, because the Spirit is the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there are three who testify:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the Spirit, the water, and the blood; and the three agree as one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater; for this is God’s testimony which he has testified concerning his Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. He who doesn’t believe God has made him a liar, because he has not believed in the testimony that God has given concerning his Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The testimony is this, that God gave to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has the Son has the life. He who doesn’t have God’s Son doesn’t have the life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may continue to believe in the name of the Son of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the boldness which we have toward him, that if we ask anything according to his will, he listens to us.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And if we know that he listens to us, whatever we ask, we know that we have the petitions which we have asked of him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not leading to death, he shall ask, and God will give him life for those who sin not leading to death. There is a sin leading to death. I don’t say that he should make a request concerning this.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All unrighteousness is sin, and there is a sin not leading to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that whoever is born of God doesn’t sin, but he who was born of God keeps himself, and the evil one doesn’t touch him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that we are of God, and the whole world lies in the power of the evil one.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We know that the Son of God has come, and has given us an understanding, that we know him who is true, and we are in him who is true, in his Son Jesus Christ. This is the true God and eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Little children, keep yourselves from idols.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The elder, to the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in truth, and not I only, but also all those who know the truth,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the truth’s sake, which remains in us, and it will be with us forever:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us, from God the Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of the Father, in truth and love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I rejoice greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, even as we have been commanded by the Father.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I beg you, dear lady, not as though I wrote to you a new commandment, but that which we had from the beginning, that we love one another.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is love, that we should walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, even as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For many deceivers have gone out into the world, those who don’t confess that Jesus Christ came in the flesh. This is the deceiver and the Antichrist.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Watch yourselves, that we don’t lose the things which we have accomplished, but that we receive a full reward.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Whoever transgresses and doesn’t remain in the teaching of Christ, doesn’t have God. He who remains in the teaching has both the Father and the Son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone comes to you, and doesn’t bring this teaching, don’t receive him into your house, and don’t welcome him,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for he who welcomes him participates in his evil deeds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Having many things to write to you, I don’t want to do so with paper and ink, but I hope to come to you, and to speak face to face, that our joy may be made full.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The children of your chosen sister greet you. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The elder to Gaius the beloved, whom I love in truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in all things and be healthy, even as your soul prospers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For I rejoiced greatly when brothers came and testified about your truth, even as you walk in truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I have no greater joy than this: to hear about my children walking in truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, you do a faithful work in whatever you accomplish for those who are brothers and strangers.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They have testified about your love before the assembly. You will do well to send them forward on their journey in a way worthy of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: because for the sake of the Name they went out, taking nothing from the Gentiles.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: We therefore ought to receive such, that we may be fellow workers for the truth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I wrote to the assembly, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first among them, doesn’t accept what we say.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore if I come, I will call attention to his deeds which he does, unjustly accusing us with wicked words. Not content with this, neither does he himself receive the brothers, and those who would, he forbids and throws out of the assembly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, don’t imitate that which is evil, but that which is good. He who does good is of God. He who does evil hasn’t seen God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Demetrius has the testimony of all, and of the truth itself; yes, we also testify, and you know that our testimony is true.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I had many things to write to you, but I am unwilling to write to you with ink and pen;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but I hope to see you soon. Then we will speak face to face. Peace be to you. The friends greet you. Greet the friends by name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to those who are called, sanctified by God the Father, and kept for Jesus Christ:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Mercy to you and peace and love be multiplied.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Beloved, while I was very eager to write to you about our common salvation, I was constrained to write to you exhorting you to contend earnestly for the faith which was once for all delivered to the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For there are certain men who crept in secretly, even those who were long ago written about for this condemnation: ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into indecency, and denying our only Master, God, and Lord, Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I desire to remind you, though you already know this, that the Lord, having saved a people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed those who didn’t believe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Angels who didn’t keep their first domain, but deserted their own dwelling place, he has kept in everlasting bonds under darkness for the judgment of the great day.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Even as Sodom and Gomorrah and the cities around them, having in the same way as these given themselves over to sexual immorality and gone after strange flesh, are shown as an example, suffering the punishment of eternal fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Yet in the same way, these also in their dreaming defile the flesh, despise authority, and slander celestial beings.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But Michael, the archangel, when contending with the devil and arguing about the body of Moses, dared not bring against him an abusive condemnation, but said, “May the Lord rebuke you!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But these speak evil of whatever things they don’t know. They are destroyed in these things that they understand naturally, like the creatures without reason.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Woe to them! For they went in the way of Cain, and ran riotously in the error of Balaam for hire, and perished in Korah’s rebellion.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are hidden rocky reefs in your love feasts when they feast with you, shepherds who without fear feed themselves; clouds without water, carried along by winds; autumn trees without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: wild waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame; wandering stars, for whom the blackness of darkness has been reserved forever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: About these also Enoch, the seventh from Adam, prophesied, saying, “Behold, the Lord came with ten thousands of his holy ones,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to execute judgment on all, and to convict all the ungodly of all their works of ungodliness which they have done in an ungodly way, and of all the hard things which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are murmurers and complainers, walking after their lusts—and their mouth speaks proud things—showing respect of persons to gain advantage.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, beloved, remember the words which have been spoken before by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They said to you, “In the last time there will be mockers, walking after their own ungodly lusts.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are those who cause divisions and are sensual, not having the Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But you, beloved, keep building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Spirit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Keep yourselves in God’s love, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ to eternal life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On some have compassion, making a distinction,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and some save, snatching them out of the fire with fear, hating even the clothing stained by the flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now to him who is able to keep them from stumbling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory in great joy,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: to God our Savior, who alone is wise, be glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and forever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: This is the Revelation of Jesus Christ, which God gave him to show to his servants the things which must happen soon, which he sent and made known by his angel to his servant, John,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: who testified to God’s word and of the testimony of Jesus Christ, about everything that he saw.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed is he who reads and those who hear the words of the prophecy, and keep the things that are written in it, for the time is at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: John, to the seven assemblies that are in Asia: Grace to you and peace from God, who is and who was and who is to come; and from the seven Spirits who are before his throne;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness, the firstborn of the dead, and the ruler of the kings of the earth. To him who loves us, and washed us from our sins by his blood—

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he made us to be a Kingdom, priests to his God and Father—to him be the glory and the dominion forever and ever. Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, he is coming with the clouds, and every eye will see him, including those who pierced him. All the tribes of the earth will mourn over him. Even so, Amen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I am the Alpha and the Omega,” says the Lord God, “who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I John, your brother and partner with you in the oppression, Kingdom, and perseverance in Christ Jesus, was on the isle that is called Patmos because of God’s Word and the testimony of Jesus Christ.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s day, and I heard behind me a loud voice, like a trumpet

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “What you see, write in a book and send to the seven assemblies:  to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and to Laodicea.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I turned to see the voice that spoke with me. Having turned, I saw seven golden lamp stands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And among the lamp stands was one like a son of man, clothed with a robe reaching down to his feet, and with a golden sash around his chest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His head and his hair were white as white wool, like snow. His eyes were like a flame of fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His feet were like burnished brass, as if it had been refined in a furnace. His voice was like the voice of many waters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He had seven stars in his right hand. Out of his mouth proceeded a sharp two-edged sword. His face was like the sun shining at its brightest.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead man.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He laid his right hand on me, saying, “Don’t be afraid. I am the first and the last,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and the Living one. I was dead, and behold, I am alive forever and ever. Amen. I have the keys of Death and of Hades.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Write therefore the things which you have seen, and the things which are, and the things which will happen hereafter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The mystery of the seven stars which you saw in my right hand, and the seven golden lamp stands is this: The seven stars are the angels of the seven assemblies. The seven lamp stands are seven assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Ephesus write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He who holds the seven stars in his right hand, he who walks among the seven golden lamp stands says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works, and your toil and perseverance, and that you can’t tolerate evil men, and have tested those who call themselves apostles, and they are not, and found them false.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: You have perseverance and have endured for my name’s sake, and have  not grown weary.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have this against you, that you left your first love.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember therefore from where you have fallen, and repent and do the first works; or else I am coming to you swiftly, and will move your lamp stand out of its place, unless you repent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But this you have, that you hate the works of the Nicolaitans, which I also hate.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes I will give to eat from the tree of life, which is in the Paradise of my God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Smyrna write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The first and the last, who was dead, and has come to life says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works, oppression, and your poverty (but you are rich), and the blasphemy of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but are a synagogue of Satan.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Don’t be afraid of the things which you are about to suffer. Behold, the devil is about to throw some of you into prison, that you may be tested; and you will have oppression for ten days. Be faithful to death, and I will give you the crown of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. He who overcomes won’t be harmed by the second death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Pergamum write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He who has the sharp two-edged sword says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works and where you dwell, where Satan’s throne is. You hold firmly to my name, and didn’t deny my faith in the days of Antipas my witness, my faithful one, who was killed among you, where Satan dwells.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have a few things against you, because you have there some who hold the teaching of Balaam, who taught Balak to throw a stumbling block before the children of Israel, to eat things sacrificed to idols, and to commit sexual immorality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So you also have some who hold to the teaching of the Nicolaitans likewise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Repent therefore, or else I am coming to you quickly, and I will make war against them with the sword of my mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies. To him who overcomes, to him I will give of the hidden manna,  and I will give him a white stone, and on the stone a new name written, which no one knows but he who receives it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Thyatira write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The Son of God, who has his eyes like a flame of fire, and his feet are like burnished brass, says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works, your love, faith, service, patient endurance, and that your last works are more than the first.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But I have this against you, that you tolerate your woman, Jezebel, who calls herself a prophetess. She teaches and seduces my servants to commit sexual immorality, and to eat things sacrificed to idols.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I gave her time to repent, but she refuses to repent of her sexual immorality.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I will throw her into a bed, and those who commit adultery with her into great oppression, unless they repent of her works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will kill her children with Death, and all the assemblies will know that I am he who searches the minds and hearts. I will give to each one of you according to your deeds.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But to you I say, to the rest who are in Thyatira, as many as don’t have this teaching, who don’t know what some call ‘the deep things of Satan,’ to you I say, I am not putting any other burden on you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless, hold that which you have firmly until I come.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who overcomes, and he who keeps my works to the end, to him I will give authority over the nations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will rule them with a rod of iron, shattering them like clay pots; as I also have received of my Father:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and I will give him the morning star.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “And to the angel of the assembly in Sardis write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He who has the seven Spirits of God, and the seven stars says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works, that you have a reputation of being alive, but you are dead.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Wake up, and keep the things that remain, which you were about to throw away, for I have found no works of yours perfected before my God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Remember therefore how you have received and heard. Keep it and repent. If therefore you won’t watch, I will come as a thief, and you won’t know what hour I will come upon you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Nevertheless you have a few names in Sardis that didn’t defile their garments. They will walk with me in white, for they are worthy.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who overcomes will be arrayed in white garments, and I will in no way blot his name out of the book of life, and I will confess his name before my Father, and before his angels.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Philadelphia write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “He who is holy, he who is true, he who has the key of David, he who opens and no one can shut, and who shuts and no one opens, says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works (behold, I have set before you an open door, which no one can shut), that you have a little power, and kept my word, and didn’t deny my name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I give some of the synagogue of Satan, of those who say they are Jews, and they are not, but lie—behold, I will make them to come and worship before your feet, and to know that I have loved you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because you kept my command to endure, I also will keep you from the hour of testing which is to come on the whole world, to test those who dwell on the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am coming quickly! Hold firmly that which you have, so that no one takes your crown.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who overcomes, I will make him a pillar in the temple of my God, and he will go out from there no more. I will write on him the name of my God and the name of the city of my God, the new Jerusalem, which comes down out of heaven from my God, and my own new name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “To the angel of the assembly in Laodicea write:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “The Amen, the Faithful and True Witness, the Beginning of God’s creation, says these things:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “I know your works, that you are neither cold nor hot. I wish you were cold or hot.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: So, because you are lukewarm, and neither hot nor cold, I will vomit you out of my mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Because you say, ‘I am rich, and have gotten riches, and have need of nothing;’ and don’t know that you are the wretched one, miserable, poor, blind, and naked;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I counsel you to buy from me gold refined by fire, that you may become rich; and white garments, that you may clothe yourself, and that the shame of your nakedness may not be revealed; and eye salve to anoint your eyes, that you may see.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: As many as I love, I reprove and chasten. Be zealous therefore, and repent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, then I will come in to him, and will dine with him, and he with me.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who overcomes, I will give to him to sit down with me on my throne, as I also overcame, and sat down with my Father on his throne.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the assemblies.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things I looked and saw a door opened in heaven, and the first voice that I heard, like a trumpet speaking with me, was one saying, “Come up here, and I will show you the things which must happen after this.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Immediately I was in the Spirit. Behold, there was a throne set in heaven, and one sitting on the throne

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that looked like a jasper stone and a sardius. There was a rainbow around the throne, like an emerald to look at.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Around the throne were twenty-four thrones. On the thrones were twenty-four elders sitting, dressed in white garments, with crowns of gold on their heads.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Out of the throne proceed lightnings, sounds, and thunders. There were seven lamps of fire burning before his throne, which are the seven Spirits of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Before the throne was something like a sea of glass, similar to crystal. In the middle of the throne, and around the throne were four living creatures full of eyes before and behind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first creature was like a lion, and the second creature like a calf, and the third creature had a face like a man, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The four living creatures, each one of them having six wings, are full of eyes around and within. They have no rest day and night, saying, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord God, the Almighty, who was and who is and who is to come!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the living creatures give glory, honor, and thanks to him who sits on the throne, to him who lives forever and ever,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the twenty-four elders fall down before him who sits on the throne, and worship him who lives forever and ever, and throw their crowns before the throne, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Worthy are you, our Lord and God, the Holy One, to receive the glory, the honor, and the power, for you created all things, and because of your desire they existed, and were created!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw, in the right hand of him who sat on the throne, a book written inside and outside, sealed shut with seven seals.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw a mighty angel proclaiming with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the book, and to break its seals?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: No one in heaven above, or on the earth, or under the earth, was able to open the book or to look in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open the book or to look in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the elders said to me, “Don’t weep. Behold, the Lion who is of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has overcome: he who opens the book and its seven seals.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw in the middle of the throne and of the four living creatures, and in the middle of the elders, a Lamb standing, as though it had been slain, having seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God, sent out into all the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then he came, and he took it out of the right hand of him who sat on the throne.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now when he had taken the book, the four living creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb, each one having a harp, and golden bowls full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sang a new song, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw, and I heard something like a voice of many angels around the throne, the living creatures, and the elders. The number of them was ten thousands of ten thousands, and thousands of thousands;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying with a loud voice, “Worthy is the Lamb who has been killed to receive the power, wealth, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and blessing!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard every created thing which is in heaven, on the earth, under the earth, on the sea, and everything in them, saying, “To him who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb be the blessing, the honor, the glory, and the dominion, forever and ever! Amen!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The four living creatures said, “Amen!” Then the elders fell down and worshiped.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw that the Lamb opened one of the seven seals, and I heard one of the four living creatures saying, as with a voice of thunder, “Come and see!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And behold, a white horse, and he who sat on it had a bow. A crown was given to him, and he came out conquering, and to conquer.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature saying, “Come!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another came out: a red horse. To him who sat on it was given power to take peace from the earth, and that they should kill one another. There was given to him a great sword.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature saying, “Come and see!” And behold, a black horse, and he who sat on it had a balance in his hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a voice in the middle of the four living creatures saying, “A choenix  of wheat for a denarius, and three choenix of barley for a denarius! Don’t damage the oil and the wine!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he opened the fourth seal, I heard the fourth living creature saying, “Come and see!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And behold, a pale horse, and the name of he who sat on it was Death. Hades  followed with him. Authority over one fourth of the earth, to kill with the sword, with famine, with death, and by the wild animals of the earth was given to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he opened the fifth seal, I saw underneath the altar the souls of those who had been killed for the Word of God, and for the testimony of the Lamb which they had.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “How long, Master, the holy and true, until you judge and avenge our blood on those who dwell on the earth?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A long white robe was given to each of them. They were told that they should rest yet for a while, until their fellow servants and their brothers,  who would also be killed even as they were, should complete their course.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw when he opened the sixth seal, and there was a great earthquake. The sun became black as sackcloth made of hair, and the whole moon became as blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The stars of the sky fell to the earth, like a fig tree dropping its unripe figs when it is shaken by a great wind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sky was removed like a scroll when it is rolled up. Every mountain and island were moved out of their places.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The kings of the earth, the princes, the commanding officers, the rich, the strong, and every slave and free person, hid themselves in the caves and in the rocks of the mountains.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us, and hide us from the face of him who sits on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the great day of his wrath has come; and who is able to stand?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After this, I saw four angels standing at the four corners of the earth, holding the four winds of the earth, so that no wind would blow on the earth, or on the sea, or on any tree.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw another angel ascend from the sunrise, having the seal of the living God. He cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was given to harm the earth and the sea,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Don’t harm the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, until we have sealed the bondservants of our God on their foreheads!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard the number of those who were sealed, one hundred forty-four thousand, sealed out of every tribe of the children of Israel:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things I looked, and behold, a great multitude, which no man could count, out of every nation and of all tribes, peoples, and languages, standing before the throne and before the Lamb, dressed in white robes, with palm branches in their hands.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cried with a loud voice, saying, “Salvation be to our God, who sits on the throne, and to the Lamb!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All the angels were standing around the throne, the elders, and the four living creatures; and they fell on their faces before his throne, and worshiped God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, “Amen! Blessing, glory, wisdom, thanksgiving, honor, power, and might, be to our God forever and ever! Amen.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the elders answered, saying to me, “These who are arrayed in the white robes, who are they, and where did they come from?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I told him, “My lord, you know.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “These are those who came out of the great suffering.They washed their robes, and made them white in the Lamb’s blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore they are before the throne of God, they serve him day and night in his temple. He who sits on the throne will spread his tabernacle over them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will never be hungry or thirsty any more. The sun won’t beat on them, nor any heat;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for the Lamb who is in the middle of the throne shepherds them and leads them to springs of life-giving waters. And God will wipe away every tear from their eyes.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When he opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the seven angels who stand before God, and seven trumpets were given to them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another angel came and stood over the altar, having a golden censer. Much incense was given to him, that he should add it to the prayers of all the saints on the golden altar which was before the throne.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The smoke of the incense, with the prayers of the saints, went up before God out of the angel’s hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel took the censer, and he filled it with the fire of the altar, then threw it on the earth. Thunders, sounds, lightnings, and an earthquake followed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The seven angels who had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first sounded, and there followed hail and fire, mixed with blood, and they were thrown to the earth. One third of the earth was burned up,  and one third of the trees were burned up, and all green grass was burned up.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second angel sounded, and something like a great burning mountain was thrown into the sea. One third of the sea became blood,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and one third of the living creatures which were in the sea died. One third of the ships were destroyed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The third angel sounded, and a great star fell from the sky, burning like a torch, and it fell on one third of the rivers, and on the springs of the waters.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The name of the star is called “Wormwood.” One third of the waters became wormwood. Many people died from the waters, because they were made bitter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The fourth angel sounded, and one third of the sun was struck, and one third of the moon, and one third of the stars; so that one third of them would be darkened, and the day wouldn’t shine for one third of it, and the night in the same way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw, and I heard an eagle, flying in mid heaven, saying with a loud voice, “Woe! Woe! Woe for those who dwell on the earth, because of the other voices of the trumpets of the three angels, who are yet to sound!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The fifth angel sounded, and I saw a star from the sky which had fallen to the earth. The key to the pit of the abyss was given to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He opened the pit of the abyss, and smoke went up out of the pit, like the smoke from a burning furnace. The sun and the air were darkened because of the smoke from the pit.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then out of the smoke came locusts on the earth, and power was given to them, as the scorpions of the earth have power.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were told that they should not hurt the grass of the earth, neither any green thing, neither any tree, but only those people who don’t have God’s seal on their foreheads.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They were given power, not to kill them, but to torment them for five months. Their torment was like the torment of a scorpion when it strikes a person.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In those days people will seek death, and will in no way find it. They will desire to die, and death will flee from them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The shapes of the locusts were like horses prepared for war. On their heads were something like golden crowns, and their faces were like people’s faces.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They had hair like women’s hair, and their teeth were like those of lions.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They had breastplates, like breastplates of iron. The sound of their wings was like the sound of chariots, or of many horses rushing to war.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They have tails like those of scorpions, and stings. In their tails they have power to harm men for five months.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They have over them as king the angel of the abyss. His name in Hebrew is “Abaddon”, but in Greek, he has the name “Apollyon”.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first woe is past. Behold, there are still two woes coming after this.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sixth angel sounded. I heard a voice from the horns of the golden altar which is before God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying to the sixth angel who had the trumpet, “Free the four angels who are bound at the great river Euphrates!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The four angels were freed who had been prepared for that hour and day and month and year, so that they might kill one third of mankind.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The number of the armies of the horsemen was two hundred million. I heard the number of them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Thus I saw the horses in the vision, and those who sat on them, having breastplates of fiery red, hyacinth blue, and sulfur yellow; and the horses’ heads resembled lions’ heads. Out of their mouths proceed fire, smoke, and sulfur.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: By these three plagues were one third of mankind killed: by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur, which proceeded out of their mouths.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For the power of the horses is in their mouths and in their tails. For their tails are like serpents, and have heads, and with them they harm.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rest of mankind, who were not killed with these plagues, didn’t repent of the works of their hands, that they wouldn’t worship demons, and the idols of gold, and of silver, and of brass, and of stone, and of wood; which can’t see, hear, or walk.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t repent of their murders, their sorceries, their sexual immorality, or their thefts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw a mighty angel coming down out of the sky, clothed with a cloud. A rainbow was on his head. His face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He had in his hand a little open book. He set his right foot on the sea, and his left on the land.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He cried with a loud voice, as a lion roars. When he cried, the seven thunders uttered their voices.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the seven thunders sounded, I was about to write; but I heard a voice from the sky saying, “Seal up the things which the seven thunders said, and don’t write them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel whom I saw standing on the sea and on the land lifted up his right hand to the sky,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created heaven and the things that are in it, the earth and the things that are in it, and the sea and the things that are in it, that there will no longer be delay,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound, then the mystery of God is finished, as he declared to his servants, the prophets.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The voice which I heard from heaven, again speaking with me, said, “Go, take the book which is open in the hand of the angel who stands on the sea and on the land.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little book.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “Take it, and eat it. It will make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it will be as sweet as honey.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I took the little book out of the angel’s hand, and ate it. It was as sweet as honey in my mouth. When I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They told me, “You must prophesy again over many peoples, nations, languages, and kings.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A reed like a rod was given to me. Someone said, “Rise, and measure God’s temple, and the altar, and those who worship in it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Leave out the court which is outside of the temple, and don’t measure it, for it has been given to the nations. They will tread the holy city under foot for forty-two months.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I will give power to my two witnesses, and they will prophesy one thousand two hundred sixty days, clothed in sackcloth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are the two olive trees and the two lamp stands, standing before the Lord of the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone desires to harm them, fire proceeds out of their mouth and devours their enemies. If anyone desires to harm them, he must be killed in this way.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These have the power to shut up the sky, that it may not rain during the days of their prophecy. They have power over the waters, to turn them into blood, and to strike the earth with every plague, as often as they desire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When they have finished their testimony, the beast that comes up out of the abyss will make war with them, and overcome them, and kill them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Their dead bodies will be in the street of the great city, which spiritually is called Sodom and Egypt, where also their Lord was crucified.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: From among the peoples, tribes, languages, and nations, people will look at their dead bodies for three and a half days, and will not allow their dead bodies to be laid in a tomb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Those who dwell on the earth rejoice over them, and they will be glad. They will give gifts to one another, because these two prophets tormented those who dwell on the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After the three and a half days, the breath of life from God entered into them, and they stood on their feet. Great fear fell on those who saw them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a loud voice from heaven saying to them, “Come up here!” They went up into heaven in the cloud, and their enemies saw them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In that day there was a great earthquake, and a tenth of the city fell. Seven thousand people were killed in the earthquake, and the rest were terrified, and gave glory to the God of heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second woe is past. Behold, the third woe comes quickly.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The seventh angel sounded, and great voices in heaven followed, saying, “The kingdom of the world has become the Kingdom of our Lord, and of his Christ. He will reign forever and ever!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The twenty-four elders, who sit on their thrones before God’s throne, fell on their faces and worshiped God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying: “We give you thanks, Lord God, the Almighty, the one who is and who was; because you have taken your great power and reigned.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The nations were angry, and your wrath came, as did the time for the dead to be judged, and to give your bondservants the prophets, their reward, as well as to the saints, and those who fear your name, to the small and the great, and to destroy those who destroy the earth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: God’s temple that is in heaven was opened, and the ark of the Lord’s covenant was seen in his temple. Lightnings, sounds, thunders, an earthquake, and great hail followed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A great sign was seen in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She was with child. She cried out in pain, laboring to give birth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another sign was seen in heaven. Behold, a great red dragon, having seven heads and ten horns, and on his heads seven crowns.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His tail drew one third of the stars of the sky, and threw them to the earth. The dragon stood before the woman who was about to give birth, so that when she gave birth he might devour her child.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: She gave birth to a son, a male child, who is to rule all the nations with a rod of iron. Her child was caught up to God, and to his throne.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman fled into the wilderness, where she has a place prepared by God, that there they may nourish her one thousand two hundred sixty days.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There was war in the sky. Michael and his angels made war on the dragon. The dragon and his angels made war.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They didn’t prevail. No place was found for them any more in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The great dragon was thrown down, the old serpent, he who is called the devil and Satan, the deceiver of the whole world. He was thrown down to the earth, and his angels were thrown down with him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a loud voice in heaven, saying, “Now the salvation, the power, and the Kingdom of our God, and the authority of his Christ has come; for the accuser of our brothers has been thrown down, who accuses them before our God day and night.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They overcame him because of the Lamb’s blood, and because of the word of their testimony. They didn’t love their life, even to death.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore rejoice, heavens, and you who dwell in them. Woe to the earth and to the sea, because the devil has gone down to you, having great wrath, knowing that he has but a short time.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: When the dragon saw that he was thrown down to the earth, he persecuted the woman who gave birth to the male child.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Two wings of the great eagle were given to the woman, that she might fly into the wilderness to her place, so that she might be nourished for a time, and times, and half a time, from the face of the serpent.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The serpent spewed water out of his mouth after the woman like a river, that he might cause her to be carried away by the stream.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The earth helped the woman, and the earth opened its mouth and swallowed up the river which the dragon spewed out of his mouth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The dragon grew angry with the woman, and went away to make war with the rest of her offspring, who keep God’s commandments and hold Jesus’ testimony.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Then I stood on the sand of the sea. I saw a beast coming up out of the sea, having ten horns and seven heads. On his horns were ten crowns, and on his heads, blasphemous names.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beast which I saw was like a leopard, and his feet were like those of a bear, and his mouth like the mouth of a lion. The dragon gave him his power, his throne, and great authority.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of his heads looked like it had been wounded fatally. His fatal wound was healed, and the whole earth marveled at the beast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They worshiped the dragon, because he gave his authority to the beast, and they worshiped the beast, saying, “Who is like the beast? Who is able to make war with him?”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A mouth speaking great things and blasphemy was given to him. Authority to make war for forty-two months was given to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He opened his mouth for blasphemy against God, to blaspheme his name, and his dwelling, those who dwell in heaven.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was given to him to make war with the saints, and to overcome them. Authority over every tribe, people, language, and nation was given to him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: All who dwell on the earth will worship him, everyone whose name has not been written from the foundation of the world in the book of life of the Lamb who has been killed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone has an ear, let him hear.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone is to go into captivity, he will go into captivity. If anyone is to be killed with the sword, he must be killed. Here is the endurance and the faith of the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw another beast coming up out of the earth. He had two horns like a lamb, and he spoke like a dragon.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence. He makes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose fatal wound was healed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He performs great signs, even making fire come down out of the sky to the earth in the sight of people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He deceives my own people who dwell on the earth because of the signs he was granted to do in front of the beast, saying to those who dwell on the earth that they should make an image to the beast who had the sword wound and lived.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was given to him to give breath to it, to the image of the beast, that the image of the beast should both speak, and cause as many as wouldn’t worship the image of the beast to be killed.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He causes all, the small and the great, the rich and the poor, and the free and the slave, to be given marks on their right hands, or on their foreheads;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and that no one would be able to buy or to sell, unless he has that mark, which is the name of the beast or the number of his name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here is wisdom. He who has understanding, let him calculate the number of the beast, for it is the number of a man. His number is six hundred sixty-six.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw, and behold, the Lamb standing on Mount Zion, and with him a number, one hundred forty-four thousand, having his name, and the name of his Father, written on their foreheads.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a sound from heaven, like the sound of many waters, and like the sound of a great thunder. The sound which I heard was like that of harpists playing on their harps.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sing a new song before the throne, and before the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn the song except the one hundred forty-four thousand, those who had been redeemed out of the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These are those who were not defiled with women, for they are virgins. These are those who follow the Lamb wherever he goes. These were redeemed by Jesus from among men, the first fruits to God and to the Lamb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In their mouth was found no lie, for they are blameless.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw an angel flying in mid heaven, having an eternal Good News to proclaim to those who dwell on the earth, and to every nation, tribe, language, and people.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said with a loud voice, “Fear the Lord, and give him glory; for the hour of his judgment has come. Worship him who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and the springs of waters!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another, a second angel, followed, saying, “Babylon the great has fallen, which has made all the nations to drink of the wine of the wrath of her sexual immorality.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another angel, a third, followed them, saying with a great voice, “If anyone worships the beast and his image, and receives a mark on his forehead, or on his hand,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: he also will drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is prepared unmixed in the cup of his anger. He will be tormented with fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The smoke of their torment goes up forever and ever. They have no rest day and night, those who worship the beast and his image, and whoever receives the mark of his name.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here is the perseverance of the saints, those who keep the commandments of God, and the faith of Jesus.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a voice from heaven saying, “Write, ‘Blessed are the dead who die in the Lord from now on.’ ”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Yes,” says the Spirit, “that they may rest from their labors; for their works follow with them.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I looked, and saw a white cloud, and on the cloud one sitting like a son of man, having on his head a golden crown, and in his hand a sharp sickle.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another angel came out of the temple, crying with a loud voice to him who sat on the cloud, “Send your sickle, and reap; for the hour to reap has come; for the harvest of the earth is ripe!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who sat on the cloud thrust his sickle on the earth, and the earth was reaped.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another angel came out of the temple which is in heaven. He also had a sharp sickle.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Another angel came out from the altar, he who has power over fire, and he called with a great voice to him who had the sharp sickle, saying, “Send your sharp sickle, and gather the clusters of the vine of the earth, for the earth’s grapes are fully ripe!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel thrust his sickle into the earth, and gathered the vintage of the earth, and threw it into the great wine press of the wrath of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The wine press was trodden outside of the city, and blood came out of the wine press, even to the bridles of the horses, as far as one thousand six hundred stadia.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw another great and marvelous sign in the sky: seven angels having the seven last plagues, for in them God’s wrath is finished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw something like a sea of glass mixed with fire, and those who overcame the beast, his image, and the number of his name, standing on the sea of glass, having harps of God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They sang the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb, saying,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things I looked, and the temple of the tabernacle of the testimony in heaven was opened.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The seven angels who had the seven plagues came out, clothed with pure, bright linen, and wearing golden sashes around their breasts.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the four living creatures gave to the seven angels seven golden bowls full of the wrath of God, who lives forever and ever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The temple was filled with smoke from the glory of God, and from his power. No one was able to enter into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels would be finished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a loud voice out of the temple, saying to the seven angels, “Go and pour out the seven bowls of the wrath of God on the earth!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The first went, and poured out his bowl into the earth, and it became a harmful and evil sore on the people who had the mark of the beast, and who worshiped his image.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The second angel poured out his bowl into the sea, and it became blood as of a dead man. Every living thing in the sea died.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The third poured out his bowl into the rivers and springs of water, and they became blood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard the angel of the waters saying, “You are righteous, who are and who were, O Holy One, because you have judged these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For they poured out the blood of saints and prophets, and you have given them blood to drink. They deserve this.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard the altar saying, “Yes, Lord God, the Almighty, true and righteous are your judgments.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The fourth poured out his bowl on the sun, and it was given to him to scorch men with fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: People were scorched with great heat, and people blasphemed the name of God who has the power over these plagues. They didn’t repent and give him glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The fifth poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast, and his kingdom was darkened. They gnawed their tongues because of the pain,

sources.label: New Testament
