sources.text: and they blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their sores. They didn’t repent of their works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sixth poured out his bowl on the great river, the Euphrates. Its water was dried up, that the way might be prepared for the kings that come from the sunrise.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw coming out of the mouth of the dragon, and out of the mouth of the beast, and out of the mouth of the false prophet, three unclean spirits, something like frogs;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for they are spirits of demons, performing signs; which go out to the kings of the whole inhabited earth, to gather them together for the war of that great day of God, the Almighty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I come like a thief. Blessed is he who watches, and keeps his clothes, so that he doesn’t walk naked, and they see his shame.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He gathered them together into the place which is called in Hebrew, “Megiddo”.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The seventh poured out his bowl into the air. A loud voice came out of the temple of heaven, from the throne, saying, “It is done!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There were lightnings, sounds, and thunders; and there was a great earthquake, such as has not happened since there were men on the earth, so great an earthquake, and so mighty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The great city was divided into three parts, and the cities of the nations fell. Babylon the great was remembered in the sight of God, to give to her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of his wrath.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Every island fled away, and the mountains were not found.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Great hailstones, about the weight of a talent, came down out of the sky on people. People blasphemed God because of the plague of the hail, for this plague is exceedingly severe.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying, “Come here. I will show you the judgment of the great prostitute who sits on many waters,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: with whom the kings of the earth committed sexual immorality. Those who dwell in the earth were made drunken with the wine of her sexual immorality.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness. I saw a woman sitting on a scarlet-colored beast, full of blasphemous names, having seven heads and ten horns.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the impurities of the sexual immorality of the earth.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And on her forehead a name was written, “MYSTERY, BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF THE PROSTITUTES AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints, and with the blood of the martyrs of Jesus. When I saw her, I wondered with great amazement.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The angel said to me, “Why do you wonder? I will tell you the mystery of the woman, and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beast that you saw was, and is not; and is about to come up out of the abyss and to go into destruction. Those who dwell on the earth and whose names have not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world will marvel when they see that the beast was, and is not, and shall be present.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Here is the mind that has wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sits.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They are seven kings. Five have fallen, the one is, the other has not yet come. When he comes, he must continue a little while.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beast that was, and is not, is himself also an eighth, and is of the seven; and he goes to destruction.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have received no kingdom as yet, but they receive authority as kings with the beast for one hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These have one mind, and they give their power and authority to the beast.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: These will war against the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, for he is Lord of lords, and King of kings, and those who are with him are called chosen and faithful.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “The waters which you saw, where the prostitute sits, are peoples, multitudes, nations, and languages.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The ten horns which you saw, and the beast, these will hate the prostitute, will make her desolate, will strip her naked, will eat her flesh, and will burn her utterly with fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For God has put in their hearts to do what he has in mind, to be of one mind, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God should be accomplished.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The woman whom you saw is the great city, which reigns over the kings of the earth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things, I saw another angel coming down out of the sky, having great authority. The earth was illuminated with his glory.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He cried with a mighty voice, saying, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great, and she has become a habitation of demons, a prison of every unclean spirit, and a prison of every unclean and hateful bird!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For all the nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her sexual immorality, the kings of the earth committed sexual immorality with her, and the merchants of the earth grew rich from the abundance of her luxury.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard another voice from heaven, saying, “Come out of her, my people, that you have no participation in her sins, and that you don’t receive of her plagues,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for her sins have reached to the sky, and God has remembered her iniquities.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Return to her just as she returned, and repay her double as she did, and according to her works. In the cup which she mixed, mix to her double.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: However much she glorified herself, and grew wanton, so much give her of torment and mourning. For she says in her heart, ‘I sit a queen, and am no widow, and will in no way see mourning.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Therefore in one day her plagues will come: death, mourning, and famine; and she will be utterly burned with fire; for the Lord God who has judged her is strong.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The kings of the earth who committed sexual immorality and lived wantonly with her will weep and wail over her, when they look at the smoke of her burning,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: standing far away for the fear of her torment, saying, ‘Woe, woe, the great city, Babylon, the strong city! For your judgment has come in one hour.’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The merchants of the earth weep and mourn over her, for no one buys their merchandise any more:

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: merchandise of gold, silver, precious stones, pearls, fine linen, purple, silk, scarlet, all expensive wood, every vessel of ivory, every vessel made of most precious wood, and of brass, and iron, and marble;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and cinnamon, incense, perfume, frankincense, wine, olive oil, fine flour, wheat, sheep, horses, chariots, and people’s bodies and souls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The fruits which your soul lusted after have been lost to you. All things that were dainty and sumptuous have perished from you, and you will find them no more at all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The merchants of these things, who were made rich by her, will stand far away for the fear of her torment, weeping and mourning,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: saying, ‘Woe, woe, the great city, she who was dressed in fine linen, purple, and scarlet, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: For in an hour such great riches are made desolate.’ Every ship master, and everyone who sails anywhere, and mariners, and as many as gain their living by sea, stood far away,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and cried out as they looked at the smoke of her burning, saying, ‘What is like the great city?’

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They cast dust on their heads, and cried, weeping and mourning, saying, ‘Woe, woe, the great city, in which all who had their ships in the sea were made rich by reason of her great wealth!’ For she is made desolate in one hour.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Rejoice over her, O heaven, you saints, apostles, and prophets; for God has judged your judgment on her.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A mighty angel took up a stone like a great millstone and cast it into the sea, saying, “Thus with violence will Babylon, the great city, be thrown down, and will be found no more at all.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The voice of harpists, minstrels, flute players, and trumpeters will be heard no more at all in you. No craftsman, of whatever craft, will be found any more at all in you. The sound of a mill will be heard no more at all in you.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The light of a lamp will shine no more at all in you. The voice of the bridegroom and of the bride will be heard no more at all in you; for your merchants were the princes of the earth; for with your sorcery all the nations were deceived.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: In her was found the blood of prophets and of saints, and of all who have been slain on the earth.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: After these things I heard something like a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, “Hallelujah! Salvation, power, and glory belong to our God;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: for his judgments are true and righteous. For he has judged the great prostitute, who corrupted the earth with her sexual immorality, and he has avenged the blood of his servants at her hand.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A second said, “Hallelujah! Her smoke goes up forever and ever.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sits on the throne, saying, “Amen! Hallelujah!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: A voice came from the throne, saying, “Give praise to our God, all you his servants, you who fear him, the small and the great!”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard something like the voice of a great multitude, and like the voice of many waters, and like the voice of mighty thunders, saying, “Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns!

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Let’s rejoice and be exceedingly glad, and let’s give the glory to him. For the wedding of the Lamb has come, and his wife has made herself ready.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: It was given to her that she would array herself in bright, pure, fine linen: for the fine linen is the righteous acts of the saints.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “Write, ‘Blessed are those who are invited to the wedding supper of the Lamb.’ ” He said to me, “These are true words of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I fell down before his feet to worship him. He said to me, “Look! Don’t do it! I am a fellow bondservant with you and with your brothers who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God, for the testimony of Jesus is the Spirit of Prophecy.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the heaven opened, and behold, a white horse, and he who sat on it is called Faithful and True. In righteousness he judges and makes war.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: His eyes are a flame of fire, and on his head are many crowns. He has names written and a name written which no one knows but he himself.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He is clothed in a garment sprinkled with blood. His name is called “The Word of God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The armies which are in heaven followed him on white horses, clothed in white, pure, fine linen.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Out of his mouth proceeds a sharp, double-edged sword, that with it he should strike the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod. He treads the wine press of the fierceness of the wrath of God, the Almighty.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He has on his garment and on his thigh a name written, “KING OF KINGS, AND LORD OF LORDS.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw an angel standing in the sun. He cried with a loud voice, saying to all the birds that fly in the sky, “Come! Be gathered together to the great supper of God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: that you may eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of captains, the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses and of those who sit on them, and the flesh of all men, both free and slave, small and great.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the beast, and the kings of the earth, and their armies, gathered together to make war against him who sat on the horse, and against his army.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The beast was taken, and with him the false prophet who worked the signs in his sight, with which he deceived those who had received the mark of the beast and those who worshiped his image. These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rest were killed with the sword of him who sat on the horse, the sword which came out of his mouth. All the birds were filled with their flesh.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw an angel coming down out of heaven, having the key of the abyss and a great chain in his hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He seized the dragon, the old serpent, which is the devil and Satan, who deceives the whole inhabited earth, and bound him for a thousand years,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and cast him into the abyss, and shut it, and sealed it over him, that he should deceive the nations no more, until the thousand years were finished. After this, he must be freed for a short time.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw thrones, and they sat on them, and judgment was given to them. I saw the souls of those who had been beheaded for the testimony of Jesus, and for the word of God, and such as didn’t worship the beast nor his image, and didn’t receive the mark on their forehead and on their hand. They lived and reigned with Christ for a thousand years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The rest of the dead didn’t live until the thousand years were finished. This is the first resurrection.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed and holy is he who has part in the first resurrection. Over these, the second death has no power, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and will reign with him one thousand years.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: And after the thousand years, Satan will be released from his prison,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and he will come out to deceive the nations which are in the four corners of the earth, Gog and Magog, to gather them together to the war; the number of whom is as the sand of the sea.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They went up over the width of the earth, and surrounded the camp of the saints, and the beloved city. Fire came down out of heaven from God and devoured them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The devil who deceived them was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet are also. They will be tormented day and night forever and ever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw a great white throne, and him who sat on it, from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away. There was found no place for them.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne, and they opened books. Another book was opened, which is the book of life. The dead were judged out of the things which were written in the books, according to their works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The sea gave up the dead who were in it. Death and Hades gave up the dead who were in them. They were judged, each one according to his works.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Death and Hades were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death, the lake of fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone was not found written in the book of life, he was cast into the lake of fire.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw a new heaven and a new earth: for the first heaven and the first earth have passed away, and the sea is no more.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw the holy city, New Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared like a bride adorned for her husband.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I heard a loud voice out of heaven saying, “Behold, God’s dwelling is with people, and he will dwell with them, and they will be his people, and God himself will be with them as their God.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain, any more. The first things have passed away.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who sits on the throne said, “Behold, I am making all things new.” He said, “Write, for these words of God are faithful and true.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “I have become the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. I will give freely to him who is thirsty from the spring of the water of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who overcomes, I will give him these things. I will be his God, and he will be my son.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: But for the cowardly, unbelieving, sinners, abominable, murderers, sexually immoral, sorcerers, idolaters, and all liars, their part is in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: One of the seven angels who had the seven bowls, who were loaded with the seven last plagues came, and he spoke with me, saying, “Come here. I will show you the wife, the Lamb’s bride.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He carried me away in the Spirit to a great and high mountain, and showed me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having the glory of God. Her light was like a most precious stone, as if it were a jasper stone, clear as crystal;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: having a great and high wall; having twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels; and names written on them, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: On the east were three gates; and on the north three gates; and on the south three gates; and on the west three gates.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The wall of the city had twelve foundations, and on them twelve names of the twelve Apostles of the Lamb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who spoke with me had for a measure a golden reed to measure the city, its gates, and its walls.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The city is square, and its length is as great as its width. He measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand twelve stadia. Its length, width, and height are equal.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Its wall is one hundred forty-four cubits, by the measure of a man, that is, of an angel.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The construction of its wall was jasper. The city was pure gold, like pure glass.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The foundations of the city’s wall were adorned with all kinds of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, chalcedony; the fourth, emerald;

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: the fifth, sardonyx; the sixth, sardius; the seventh, chrysolite; the eighth, beryl; the ninth, topaz; the tenth, chrysoprase; the eleventh, jacinth; and the twelfth, amethyst.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The twelve gates were twelve pearls. Each one of the gates was made of one pearl. The street of the city was pure gold, like transparent glass.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I saw no temple in it, for the Lord God, the Almighty, and the Lamb, are its temple.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The city has no need for the sun or moon to shine, for the very glory of God illuminated it, and its lamp is the Lamb.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The nations will walk in its light. The kings of the earth bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Its gates will in no way be shut by day (for there will be no night there),

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: and they shall bring the glory and the honor of the nations into it so that they may enter.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will in no way enter into it anything profane, or one who causes an abomination or a lie, but only those who are written in the Lamb’s book of life.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He showed me a river of water of life, clear as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb,

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: in the middle of its street. On this side of the river and on that was the tree of life, bearing twelve kinds of fruits, yielding its fruit every month. The leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be no curse any more. The throne of God and of the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will serve him.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: They will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: There will be no night, and they need no lamp light or sun light; for the Lord God will illuminate them. They will reign forever and ever.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “These words are faithful and true. The Lord God of the spirits of the prophets sent his angel to show to his bondservants the things which must happen soon.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I come quickly. Blessed is he who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Now I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. When I heard and saw, I fell down to worship before the feet of the angel who had shown me these things.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “See you don’t do it! I am a fellow bondservant with you and with your brothers, the prophets, and with those who keep the words of this book. Worship God.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He said to me, “Don’t seal up the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is at hand.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who acts unjustly, let him act unjustly still. He who is filthy, let him be filthy still. He who is righteous, let him do righteousness still. He who is holy, let him be holy still.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: “Behold, I come quickly. My reward is with me, to repay to each man according to his work.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Blessed are those who do his commandments, that they may have the right to the tree of life, and may enter in by the gates into the city.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Outside are the dogs, the sorcerers, the sexually immoral, the murderers, the idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I, Jesus, have sent my angel to testify these things to you for the assemblies. I am the root and the offspring of David, the Bright and Morning Star.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” He who hears, let him say, “Come!” He who is thirsty, let him come. He who desires, let him take the water of life freely.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: I testify to everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book, if anyone adds to them, may God add to him the plagues which are written in this book.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: If anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, may God take away his part from the tree of life, and out of the holy city, which are written in this book.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: He who testifies these things says, “Yes, I come quickly.”

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: Amen! Yes, come, Lord Jesus.

sources.label: New Testament
sources.text: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with all the saints. Amen.
